review_text: Lovely school. Kids learn & grow happily here. This is our sports day

As a father, I am absolutely delighted with the experience my children are having at Mulberry Jurong East Childcare Centre! When my wife and I enrolled our son and daughter, we knew we had made the right decision.

The staff at Mulberry are truly exceptional. They are caring, patient, and nurturing, making my children feel comfortable and loved. It brings me peace of mind knowing that my kids are in such capable hands, receiving the best care possible.

The learning environment at Mulberry is outstanding. My children come home every day excited about what they've learned and eager to share their experiences. The curriculum is well-rounded, stimulating their curiosity while also focusing on important social and emotional development.

The facilities are top-notch, providing a safe and clean space for the children to explore and play. I appreciate the variety of activities and resources available to them, which encourage creativity and imagination.

Communication with the teachers and staff is excellent. They keep us informed about our children's progress, and they are always approachable and receptive to any concerns or questions we may have.

The sense of community at Mulberry is heartwarming. The events and activities they organize bring families together, fostering a strong bond among parents, children, and staff alike.

In summary, as a father, I couldn't be happier with the care and education my children receive at Mulberry Jurong East Childcare Centre. It has been a blessing for our family, providing a loving and stimulating environment for our kids to grow and learn. I wholeheartedly recommend Mulberry to any parents seeking a wonderful childcare experience.

Best place where a kid can be! The teachers have lots of patience toward every child. My kids love school so much and they look forward to school every single day. The teachers build good rapport with us and we have full confidence that the school takes good care of our children.

A wonderful school learning environment, teachers with an ever positive attitude and never too far if you need them. Our son has been with MBRCRT since N1 and showered with love by all the teachers. Thumbs up!

Ever since I've send my first born to Mulberry Learning @ Jurong East since she was 2 months old, I have never had a bad impression of the school. The teachers has been so motherly and caring towards my child and her classmates, and the principal has been so thoughtful for the extra care needed to be conveyed to my child. Never have I ever had a second opinion on changing school for my child and with the second child up coming next year, I'll continue them together in the same school.

Small wonder-previously safari house, has really been a really great preschool for my child. Enrolled him when he was an infant back in May 2023 and he has been here from Infant care to Playgroup to Pre Nursery(N1) now. In IFC, the teachers are always happy to be sharing our child’s progress and the activities they did - be it outdoor, indoor, stories, colouring or drawing.

We have been seeing him grow so much and at Playgroup, he knows the sound of each letter - phonics. We really love the way the curriculum are having one story book (one each for Chinese and English) for each term, teaching phonics, role playing the story line, art and craft around the story line and characters. Very interesting and of course, the principal and teachers show genuine compassion and are always ready to share with us how my child is in school and sometimes without us asking, they do share as well.

This school also encourage parents to be involve in school activities like excursion and celebrations involving parents is something that we parents enjoy! Thank you for your hard work both teaching and non teaching staff. Highly recommending it to any parents out there looking for a good pre school for your child!

I have been sending my daughter to Small Wonder since she was just 4 months old, and now she is already in K2. Looking back, Small Wonder has been so much more than a childcare centre—it has been like a second home to her. She grew, learned, and blossomed here. Every milestone, every new word, every confidence she gained… so much of it came from the love and guidance she received in this school.

I cannot put into words how deeply grateful I am for all the love, patience, and dedication the teachers have shown—not only to my daughter, but also to us as parents. She adores her teachers and her friends, and it warms my heart to see how happy she has always been here.

It breaks my heart a little to say goodbye soon, but I know the beautiful memories she made in this school will stay with her forever. Small Wonder will always hold a very special place in our hearts.

Thank you, Teacher Hanna, and every teacher who has cared for and taught my daughter from infant all the way to K2. We will always remember you with so much love and gratitude.

My honest review as a parent because this school deserves more than just 2 5-star reviews. In short, this is one of the BEST infant cares in the west (I’m not sure about the other levels since my kid is under 18 months) with a very dedicated group of principals and teachers.

We’d initially sent our kid to an ifc with a lot of outlets right at our void deck. 2 hours in and it scarred us, with every kid crying and the teachers just casually using their phones and not caring, causing us to find an alternative desperately and immediately. We came across small wonder and the only outlet with vacancy is at ibp (30min drive from our home).

1 hour in the school and we were so impressed by how the kids in the ifc were all smiling and playing with the teachers, the amount of visuals and sensory boards to stimulate the kids, equipment to let older infants climb around and the open concept to let infants observe the older kids to aid them in adjusting when they graduate from ifc.

One event that amazed us the most was a christmas event that we attended 2 months into the school. The principal stood by the door announcing the arrival of every parent confidently ‘XXX’s parents, YYY’s parents’ and so on. We don’t understand how she managed to remember the parents of every child from every level, us included when we only just came!! This amount of love and dedication only comes from small wonder ibp. We’d visited other small wonder outlets before (even other former safari house ones) but this is hands down the best one. Just arrange a visit here and you will know I am not exaggerating.

Other than the first month of adjusting, my baby smiles every time she goes to school and smiles even wider when she sees the teachers. We have not looked back since.

When our previous preschool announced closure, we found MapleBear Jurong East at ICON@IBP. From the tour, we talked to the Centre Director Ms Jacqueline, principal & class teacher and they are so warm and genuine. It felt exactly right for our child, so we enrolled right away.

The play-based, nurturing environment is wonderful. My girl is happy, settled and having lots of fun with her friends. She now looks forward to school every day and loves her activities and friends.

The team’s communication is excellent, we always get prompt updates and clear info.
A BIG THANK YOU to Ms Lyann and 方老师 for helping her settle in so well and improve in all ways.  Teachers who truly partner with parents are priceless.

Highly recommend MapleBear Jurong East at ICON@IBP to any parent looking for a caring, engaging preschool.

My son joined pretty late and we were worried if he would settle in quickly or he might take some time to get comfortable. To my surprise, he was comfortable within the week he joined and would tell us he has made friends and he likes the school! The teachers gave frequent updates and are really easy to communicate with. We also saw vast improvement in his literacy and the school prepares the children well for Primary school.

The class has a variety of experiences during lessons and lots of hands on experience, fun field trips and meaningful celebrations planned. Thank you MB IBP for giving a warm, caring and enjoyable environment for the kids! Highly recommend 👍🏻

We had a very positive experience at Maple Bear Jurong East ICON@IBP. The Centre Director, Ms Jacqueline, together with the principal and the team of teachers, are caring, dedicated, and attentive to my boy’s needs. The learning environment is warm and engaging, and my boy truly enjoys the play-based approach to learning.

We appreciate the school’s strong focus on my boy’s overall development and emotional growth, as well as the guidance and advice given to us to become better parents. It has been wonderful to see such significant improvement in his development, especially in his independence, confidence, and curiosity, with the constant encouragement from the school. We would definitely recommend Maple Bear Jurong East ICON@IBP to parents who are looking for a nurturing and supportive preschool environment!

I am truly so thankful to MapleBear IBP for going the extra mile in helping my child transition so smoothly from her previous school. Her teachers have been incredibly enthusiastic, patient, and attentive, constantly making her feel safe, comfortable, and at ease in school.

Seeing the positive changes in her within a short period at IBP has honestly been amazing. In comparison, during the past months at her previous school, we did not witness even half the improvement, confidence, or happiness that we are seeing now.

Thank you to the wonderful teachers and staff for showing such genuine care and dedication towards my child’s growth and well being. It truly means a lot to us as parents.

Amazing school with kind, wonderful teachers who make the effort to connect with my child and regularly provide feedback during drop-off and through the Maplebear app. Despite being in a business park, the school ensures the children get outdoor playtime almost every day. My child attends school happily every day and there are always events to look forward, which are all very well organised. The curriculum here also prepares the child well for P1, I was surprised at the standard of the spelling tests and take home books to self read - and even more surprised when my child can write and read. I will definitely highly recommend this school to any parent.

Finding the right school for my child was something I thought a lot about—and I’m really glad Posso was recommended to us!

What I love most is that it’s classroom-based, not open concept. It really helps the children stay focused and settled.

The teachers are amazing—they don’t just know their own class, they know all the children. It honestly feels like one big family, and that kind of environment is so comforting as a parent.

The school also puts in a lot of effort to organise term excursions and mini excursions, which I really appreciate. The kids aren’t just learning in class—they’re exposed to different environments that broaden their knowledge and experiences.

Curriculum-wise, it’s very well-rounded: English, Chinese, Maths, water play, outdoor play, dancing, trampoline, arts, cookery!!!… they really cover everything while still making it fun for the kids.

Most importantly, my child is genuinely happy going to school—and that says everything. ❤️

Also really want to give a special shoutout to Principal Sherla, Li Laoshi, Teacher Sara, the operations/admin team, and all of my child’ teachers. Thank you for creating such a warm, nurturing environment for the kids 💛

We’re very grateful to the teachers at Posso! The teachers at infant care are so dedicated and caring, we felt so at ease placing our child in their care. The babies get exposed to different sensory play, and activities - the swimming sessions and the outdoor play. We also get timely updates on our child every week, our child loves going to school everyday. Best infant care! Thank you Chen laoshi, Teacher Regina, Teacher Fiqah for your love and care given to our child ♥️

We’ve had a great experience with Posso Preschool. My kids enjoy school and we feel reassured with the daily updates on their meals and nap duration. The curriculum includes fun activities like cookery and trampoline, which my kids love. We love the classroom rotation for each subject so the kids do not just stay in one classroom throughout the day. The teachers are experienced, caring, and attentive to each child’s needs. Definitely recommend this preschool!

It would highly recommend Posso pre school for parents looking for pre schools or day care.

My son graduated last year from the school and we are extremely happy with personality growth  and learning we saw in him.

The level of personal attention given to him was commendable,
As a non-native Chinese speaker , i saw him participate in all the activities English and Chinese with confidence we never had at his age.

And this all the thanks to superb curriculum, highly supportive and patient teacher & staff who went above and beyond to make him well versed in Mandarin.

From water play to performance, dance , science experiments, trampoline, (and some time 😊)written tests , art and craft and many activities , everyday we heard something exciting from our son when he came back home.

1 month after moving to Grade 1 , all of us  still reminisce about posso almost everyday, many thing he does now in his Grade 1 reminds us how this was in posso , that he already knows etc., shows how well he was prepared for the next grade.

Again , i would highly recommend to newly looking parent to go talk to teacher , principal, let your child experience this school , and pretty sure you would not regret it.

I am incredibly grateful to the dedicated teachers at Posso, led by the nurturing Chen Chunrui Laoshi and her amazing team including LinNa Laoshi and teacher Ng Kim Eng. Their unwavering care and commitment to our child's development have been truly remarkable. We are consistently kept informed about our child's progress and are delighted to receive regular updates and photos showcasing her joyful experiences at school. The exceptional facilities, such as the weekly baby spa sessions at Posso Jurong, have undoubtedly added to our child's happiness. We extend our heartfelt appreciation to the teachers for their warmth and love, which our little one undoubtedly feels every day!

Posso has also a very big and spacious premises with outdoor gardening and playground (including water play). The PG/Nursery and Kindergarten teachers are also very responsive and supportive! 😀

Overall, it’s a Highly recommended preschool. Thank you immensely for your dedication and support 👍😀

As a student pursuing a course in mechanical and electrical engineering at BMC International College, I had a stellar experience. The faculty and staff were extremely supportive and dedicated to ensuring that I had a positive learning experience. I would like to extend my special thanks to Ma'am Ann, who went above and beyond to help me navigate through the challenging coursework and provided invaluable guidance throughout my time at the college. Her expertise and dedication truly made a difference in my academic journey.

The college provided me with a comprehensive understanding of both mechanical and electrical engineering principles, and the practical hands-on experience I gained has proven to be invaluable. The curriculum was well-structured and current, and the professors were passionate and knowledgeable in their respective fields.

The college also provided ample resources for research and practical application of the knowledge gained, and the state-of-the-art facilities and labs further enhanced my learning experience.

Overall, my time at BMC International College has been rewarding and has equipped me with the knowledge and skills necessary to excel in my future career. I am grateful for the education and support I received and would highly recommend this college to anyone seeking a quality education in the field of mechanical and electrical engineering. Thank you, BMC International College, for an exceptional learning experience.

Made payments on 12 feb to retrieve back diploma and transcript certificate. They told me they will take 7 days upon payment. 13 march (ONE MONTH AFTER), i prompted again after several no replies, they asked me "Need proof of my diploma and transcript cert" to verify. Excuse me? I filled up a form with all my details???? Why would i still come to you and pay if i had my certs?

Take note they only started to reply me when i asked for refund

Amazing place to study for diploma, advanced diploma and higher studies as well.
I was a student of diploma and the course was extremely well coordinated by Miss Annalyn and the support provided to the students was extremely amazing .
Really good for international students and amazing syllabus with in depth and practical knowledge was given to us.
Really would like to thank each members of this college to provide me a smooth curriculum and helped me increase my knowledge.

BMC college is so professional in handling students matter from their applications, during their studies and till attaining of their certifications.
Vastly different from other colleges thus with professional lecturers during online class sessions during and post pandemic.

BMC staffs had done a great jobs!

I personally thanks BMC for their service and on behalf of their international students.

Looking for forward to higher degrees for upcoming students

BMC International College is very good, all the staff and lectures, especially Ms. Annalyn are always supportive and helpful. They have very well knowledge and experience about hospitality industry. They always bring a very good atmosphere for students which helps them to study. I took Diploma in International Hospitality Management, all the modules were very good and important for me to study. Moreover, it was fun and memorable time, Ms. Annalyn always supported and encouraged me to do the best during my study. after completed all the modules, BMC International College provided 100% Industrial placement assistance and guided me from interview until prepared all the documents needed for industri attachment.The school tried the best to organize best placement for all the students. After completing my training, the school guided me to submit the logbook and very responsive to help me to get the Diploma certificate which is very important for me to apply professional job in Hospitality industry. Thank you BMC İnternational College🙏

Dewa
student of Diploma in International Hospitality

Bunch of ridiculous students at cck interchange always not queuing up for 190, while the senior citizens all could. And just going up the bus without waiting for the queue to clear. Smart but lack of common sense.
A big shoutout to Mr Steve Poh, Mdm Shasila & Mr Lee Wei Chai who helped me survive in PJC

Shoutout to Mr Tan KG, #1 PE Teacher that helped me get 200 points for IPPT and Top in Inter Class Games. I have benefited greatly from his teaching and I have flourished, from a cocoon to a butterfly. Looking forward to becoming a PE Teacher in the future and nurture the next batch of CHAMPIONS!!! JP ALWAYS STRIVES AS ONE #INFINITEGROWTHBABY
Shoutout to Mr Subash Ramiah wansuey math teacher #goatedasf. He explained everything in life using Mathematics. I will want to become a Math Prof in the future 😍😍😍😍
Very caring teachers that go to the mile to educate. Learn to be a leader. Teachers are very experienced with long service years

My experience at AEG International College has been truly exceptional. As a student pursuing my Higher Diploma in M&E Engineering, I found the college to be a perfect blend of academic excellence. The instructors are knowledgeable and supportive.I’m incredibly thankful for the support and education I received at AEG International College, which played a crucial role in shaping my professional path.

I am proud to be an alumnus of AEG International College. My experience here was nothing short of exceptional. The college provides a well-structured curriculum that blends theoretical knowledge with practical application, preparing students for real-world challenges.

I am currently a student at AEG International College and have had an outstanding experience so far.

The academic programs at AEG are top-notch. The faculty members are knowledgeable, approachable, and genuinely committed to the success of their students. The curriculum is well-designed, providing a comprehensive understanding of the subjects.
The staff and teachers are always ready to assist students, and there's a strong sense of community within the college. Whether it's academic guidance or personal support, I've always felt valued and encouraged.

I recently completed my Higher Diploma in Civil Engineering at AEG International College, and I must say, it has been a rewarding experience overall.

The quality of education at AEG International College is commendable. The instructors are knowledgeable and dedicated, providing in-depth insights into various aspects of engineering

I had an excellent experience at AEG International College, where I completed my Higher Diploma in Mechanical and Electrical Engineering. The college provided a strong foundation in mechanical and electrical   engineering  , with up-to-date facilities and a hands-on approach to learning. The lecturers were highly knowledgeable, supportive, and always encouraged critical thinking and problem-solving.
Can I confirm are there sub aircon contractors manning the aircon cooling system, I heard this is a Singtel data centre? I m enquiring
7 to 8 minutes walk from bus stop. No shelter. Bring brolly
A very outstanding data center. Customer service is good. Security team is excellent.

A Heartfelt Praise for My Beloved School

My school is far more than an educational institution; it is a nurturing haven where knowledge blooms and personalities thrive, and I feel incredibly privileged to be part of this vibrant community.

From the moment you step onto the campus, you are greeted by a harmonious blend of natural beauty and academic vitality. Lush green lawns stretch across the grounds, dotted with vibrant flowers that release a refreshing fragrance every morning, creating a serene atmosphere for learning. The campus facilities are modern and well-equipped: spacious classrooms flooded with natural light, a well-stocked library housing thousands of books that satisfy our intellectual curiosity, and a state-of-the-art gymnasium where we can unwind and pursue our athletic passions after class. Every corner of the school is meticulously maintained, reflecting the institution's commitment to providing the best environment for students.

What truly sets this school apart is its exceptional faculty. Our teachers are not just knowledgeable educators but also caring mentors who go above and beyond to guide us. They design engaging lessons that spark our interest in even the most challenging subjects, encouraging critical thinking and creativity rather than rote memorization. Whenever we encounter difficulties in studies or life, they are always there to offer patient guidance and unwavering support. Their dedication and passion for teaching have instilled in us a lifelong love for learning and the courage to explore our potential.

The school also places great emphasis on holistic development, offering a diverse range of extracurricular activities that cater to every student's interests. From debate clubs that hone our public speaking skills to sports teams that foster teamwork, from art workshops that nurture creativity to community service projects that cultivate empathy, these activities have helped us grow into well-rounded individuals. I still vividly remember the sense of accomplishment when our team won the inter-school debate competition, a moment made possible by the school's encouragement and the support of our peers.

Beyond academics and activities, the school has built a warm and inclusive community. Students from different backgrounds come together, respecting and learning from one another, creating a atmosphere of camaraderie and mutual support. The values of integrity, responsibility, and kindness are woven into every aspect of school life, shaping our character and preparing us to be responsible citizens of the world.

This school has been a cornerstone of my growth, equipping me with not just knowledge but also the skills and values to face the future with confidence. It is more than a school; it is a second home where I have made cherished memories and forged lifelong friendships. I am endlessly grateful for the opportunities and experiences it has given me, and I will always be proud to call myself a student of this remarkable institution.

Very very rude security guard. Happened to go there for pick up. One guard just had to "scream" at me in front of my child for parking in the "shaded" lot. Apparently, that spot was "the boss's" spot. But then, why wasn't there any sign or indication that it was a reserved spot? How are the visitors supposed to know that it is a reserved spot? And please mind your manners. It wasn't a pleasant experience having to be screamed at in front of your child for no good reason. If that specific spot is so important, put up a signage. That boss is not everyone else's boss.

I am very impressed with this school. The teachers are professional, patient, and truly care about the students’ growth. The learning environment is welcoming and well-organized, making it easy for students to stay motivated. The school also provides a variety of activities that help students develop both academically and personally. Overall, it is a wonderful place for learning, and I highly recommend it.

East Asia Institute of Management is one of the most prestigious registered private educational institutions (PEIs) in Singapore (with 4-year Edutrust certified). I am proud to be a hospitality & tourism alumnus here for the degrees of Advanced Diploma, Bachelor of Arts (Hons) and Master of Science (in collaboration with Queen Margaret University, Edinburgh) from 2015 to 2019. Both the BA (Hons) and MSc degrees were recognised by the Vietnamese Ministry of Education and Training.

EAIM (formerly known as EASB) was such a powerful catalyst for achieving my dreams and building my fulfilling career as the hospitality lecturer and education practitioner.

Went there to give a talk to students so not in a position to rate the courses.
But interesting older building with lots of space.
And good how the building was updated with wall murals to reflect their courses
Have to walk for 30 minutes from the subway, but the path is not obvious. Unpleasant office park with little sense of beauty or humanity. Home of the Estonian special consul, every 3 months or so.
Car parking is expensive 😟
Estonia ambassador is located here.
Very friendly security guards
Very nice.
This place is so great and Mr Joel is such a great teacher!
great tuition! love mr joel's moustache :3
great curriculum!
This place is awesome

The trial was very engaging and the course syllabus is exactly what is needed for O level exam prep.  The admin staff was also very patient and responsive right from the start to finalizing and arranging the trial class for us.  Great experience thus far.

place_name: The Thought Tailor (Jurong East) | English & GP Tuition
review_text:
Mr Russell is a very understanding and helpful person. He always takes the time to mark my extra work even outside of tuition hours, which I really appreciate. He explains things clearly and is patient when I don’t understand something. I feel more confident in my work because of his support.

Mr Choon Ray is very encouraging and motivating. His comprehension lessons are always engaging, and I learn a lot from them. He creates a comfortable environment where I’m not afraid to ask questions or clarify my doubts.

place_name: The Thought Tailor (Jurong East) | English & GP Tuition
review_text: Both Mr Russell and Mr Choon Ray are outstanding teachers who make lessons engaging, relevant, and motivating. Their use of real-life examples keeps us updated and helps us apply our knowledge to GP essays more effectively. Mr Russell is always encouraging and pushes us to do our best—despite his busy schedule, he takes the time to mark our work and provide helpful feedback. Mr Choon Ray, on the other hand, is patient, supportive, and full of guidance. He consistently shares useful tips and offers moral encouragement that really helps us stay motivated. I’m very grateful to be learning under such dedicated and inspiring teachers!

place_name: The Thought Tailor (Jurong East) | English & GP Tuition
review_text: Good tuition center, lessons are well planned and engaging, notes are highly detailed and contains some higher level stuff which you can use to impress whoever is marking your script. There are also paper 2 question type worksheets given out with tips on what to look out for and how to answer them. Doing them and getting them marked helped to improve my paper 2 by a lot. Teachers are approachable, and will go the extra mile to help you through one on one sessions. Will definitely recommend signing up for lessons here if you are looking to improve on your GP.

place_name: The Thought Tailor (Jurong East) | English & GP Tuition
review_text: I have been attending the lessons in TTT for the past one year and personally I feel that it has been a very meaningful and fun time learning. Lessons were never boring and there will always be something new to learn from the teachers here every lesson. I am happy to see improvements in my grades for English and is very thankful to all the teachers here that have helped me to improve.

place_name: The Thought Tailor (Jurong East) | English & GP Tuition
review_text: TTTC helped me greatly in improving my writing and comprehension skills. As a student who has been in this centre for 2 years, I have definitely seen significant improvements in my grades for English. Their resources is extremely useful and their structured catering to each component of the subject is also very helpful.

Outstanding library. Convenient location (OPP Science Centre) and many floors or materials. Their program room is great for kid activities.

Surprisingly, toilets are badly maintained :(
Nice, quiet and with lots of books. Highly recommended library of you love reading!

Amazing! Just no words to say. It was my first time to the Jurong regional library and the very scale of it took me in awe. So many collections of different books and so many different levels to go and explore! They have a community space for events and workshops , then there is a cafe and bakery inside with good food options. There is so many different things to explore! I couldn’t even finish going through all the sections! I will definitely go there again soon. The place is also located very close to the MRT  station and the Jurong east bus terminus . It has a sheltered walkway so we could easily reach the place even though it was raining. Many people from all different age groups were there. There is study tables with charging points. For some of the study tables you need to book in advance but for the rest you can always find and empty space. There are DVDs and other stuff as well. If you have to borrow books you need to register , but I just went for reading so I didn’t have to register anything. The library closes at 9pm and is open all days of the week. They will announce over the mike before the closing time so you have time to pack your stuff. I especially liked the photography section and farming section . There’s a separate magazine section and newspaper section as well. Different languages from Tamil to Malay to Korean you will find in the library .

This library was visited few times in the span of 15 years. Still impressed by its cleanliness, calm and  peaceful atmosphere in addition to the silent air, the display of new arrivals also catch my eyes. Always full of study-loaded students and other book-lovers, you will still find a seat for enjoying some books or surfing internet on the provided PC's as the library is very spacious. Remarkably, there is a vast collection of books.

Spacious JE Centre Library with many seats availability. Cool Air-Cond Environment, Front Welcoming Display, Information,  Photo Wall Taking, Green Wall Digital Huge Screen & Clean well maintained Toilet. Great Place for Reading, Desktop PC Computer Internet access & comfy sofas. Wonderful!👏🇸🇬

I have headed here in singapore to head to IMM mall. Metro ride was good. Im thrilled to see everyone using metro besides personal transport. Most of the people are very friendly giving directions when needed. Security team and staff are so helpful in showing the directions. I depended so much on them as I visited and took train for the first time. There was some confusion when taking the circle change in the station.

I love in the West. I've been using the JE MRst exchange since 1991. The ceiling plan hasn't changed at all since then.

Until recently, this roof wasn't washed much at all and even now it's hard to get rid ofnth grime and bird waste accumulating here. I understand that this is very difficult given the business of the interchange. But something has to be done.

This MRT Station is strategically located in Jurong East with two popular shopping malls in the area directly connected to it and with the IMM shopping mall and the Ng Teng Fong Community Hospital just a bit farther away from the aforementioned two malls. Therefore this MRT Station sees a heavy footfall. As I live in Hillview, I can easily get to Jurong East by taking bus number 184 followed by a one stop MRT ride from Clementi MRT Station to Jurong East MRT Station.

I cannot believe my eyes! Totally different from 20 years ago.

I have not been to Jurong East MRT station for 20 years. When I stepped out of the train nowadays, I was shocked. It has changed so much!

20 years ago, this was just a normal station. Now, it looks like a whole new modern city. It is very interesting and well-developed. Here is why I was so impressed:

If you have not been here in a long time, you must visit. It is amazing to see how much Singapore has built and improved this area. It is a true transformation!

Jurong East MRT Station is a very convenient interchange station connecting both the North-South Line and East-West Line. It is well organized and makes travelling across Singapore easier, especially for people working or living in the west area.

The station is directly connected to nearby shopping malls like JEM and Westgate, which is very useful for shopping and dining after travel. Clean environment and clear signboards make transfers smooth even during busy hours. Highly recommended interchange station in Singapore.

Existing MRT station and bus interchange. Plenty of local delights and Western food shopping banking supermarkets library cinemas entertainment and amenities nearby. Free wifi. Conveniently  located within walking distance with covered pathway links to Westgate JEM Jcube IMM Big Box. Friendly fast attentive cosy comfy welcoming ambience for romantic intimates groups students tourists Expats and families..

Jurong East MRT Station (NS1/EW24) is an above-ground cross-platform interchange station along the North South Line (NSL) and East West Line (EWL), while serving as the terminus of the North South Line.

It is located within the Jurong Gateway planning subzone, near Jurong Gateway Road and Venture Avenue, and serves the Jurong New Town.

There is a  bus interchange too which makes it very convenient for shoppers and diners.

There are tons  of local delights and western food options available to choose from.

It is a lovely place for shopping,dining  and banking.

There are  supermarkets, library ,cinemas ,entertainment and amenities near Jurong East MRT Station.

There are links to  Westgate, JEM J,cube, IMM ,Big Box and Eng Teng Fong hospital.

It seems fast paced with many students and families using it's  cosy comfortable and elcoming ambience.

Coffe bean and Starbucks are nearby too.

Jcube has many options for children to get entertained including indoor playgrounds.

It is a great place for tourists as well.

I love Jurong East MRT Station for the choices of shopping items and food options.
Awful queue to enter the station; bottleneck design at the entry point.

jurong east mrt is one of busiest stations because it act as interchange between two mrt lines (i.e. red and green) and a bus interchange. Functional management need to be competent and consistent and whatever one-sided implementation, e.g. traffic flow within premise should be monitored and controlled. Furthermore, ground support should knows how to deal with chaotic situation and not be the scapegoat (from angry commuters) while the clueless manager sits in the aircon office. If you haven't realized, SMRT are mobilizing its staffs to shepherd commuters during peak hour. Done correctly is a good thing, and badly is really annoying. In addition, escalators in stations are experiencing maintenance without expected date of completion pasted. To SMRT, commuters should be grateful that escalators are serviced and they shouldn't demand more information (even if this inconveniences them). All in all, SMRT operations are not good, and customer service is bad on a scale of worst, bad, not good, good and best. There is no neutral here.

Jurong East MRT
This is one of the busiest MRT in the West. It sever as an interception from Pioneer & Bukit Batok. You really cannot.image how it actually like during peck hours or when MRT is delay/ breakdown. LAT is planning to settle it's HQ near here. Perhaps they will have better feel on how bad it is or can be imagine.

Last update Oct2017
Good

It is one of the larger size interchanges, however the layout is not the most convenient. Some of the buses which are going to the same directions or at the very two ends of the station, making it very inconvenient for people to choose between two buses. Despite the fact they do have an update on digital schedule board, the timing isn't totally accurate . The location also becomes extremely hectic for both pedestrian and vehicle traffic.

Pros:
Update digital schedule board for bus arrival
Large

Cons:
Digital schedule board isn't totally punctual
The layout of the line is confusing
New bus interchange, spacious and clean. Though it’s a slightly longer walk from MRT. But nicely sheltered.

This happened on the 19th of March at around 4.25pm, Jurong East Bus Interchange. I arrived at the interchange and saw on My Transport SG app indicating bus service 335 is arriving in 1 minute time. By the time, I rushed to its bus stand, the information panel verified that it’s arriving. Two minute past and the next thing I know, the information panel suddenly indicated that they wait has turned into a whole 8 minute.

Needless to say, those there were in the same queue as I were visibly frustrated. As I stayed around Jurong West Avenue 1, I actually have two more bus service options to choose (namely 98 and 335), but since not bus stands are placed a whole 5 min walk away from the 334 bus stand, it doesn’t allow passengers to quickly switch queue based on first arriving bus service.

I’m highlighting this as a concerned commuter as our bus service has always been pride for its reliability and transparency. As I understand, Tower Transit is a contracted, I would still hold the very same expectations I would with other bus companies like SBS. This gross lapse were fueled when there was no explanation given by the subsequent driver of the bus service nor was there any attempt to quell on the interchange PA system.

This total disregard to the commuters’ time is concerning and honestly, infuriating. Please do better.

Service no 335 punctuality is suck.
Just a bus stop outside the terminal , displaying 1 minutes waiting time can end up waiting for 20 minutes. This happens quite frequently. Need to improve.

New interchange replacing old Jurong East Temp Int to facilitate rail building.

Picture taken from 2019.

Sanitation issues at the bus station; the area is visibly clean but has an overwhelming, persistent smell of urine. Additionally, the real-time bus arrival information is consistently inaccurate, particularly for service 993, which makes commuting unreliable.

Jurong East Bus Interchange is an incredibly well-organized and efficient transport hub. It’s conveniently located next to the Jurong East MRT station, making transfers between bus and train services seamless. The layout is straightforward, with clear signage that makes navigating through the interchange easy, even for first-time visitors.

The facilities are clean and well-maintained, with ample seating areas for waiting passengers. There are also several shops and food options available, so you can grab a quick bite or drink while waiting for your bus. The staff at the interchange are friendly and always ready to assist with any inquiries or directions.

Overall, Jurong East Bus Interchange provides a pleasant and hassle-free experience for commuters. Highly recommended for anyone traveling in the area!
Transport hub linked to Jurong East MRT station. Various food stalls available.

Drivers of service 51 and 993 consistently do not leave interchange as scheduled. Information screen indicates what time the next bus comes, but the bus does not come, then the information screen just rolls one to the subsequent bus timing. Either update the bus schedule, and get the buses to leave as scheduled. This is a long running and frequent issue.

I have seen and got to know many buses do not care for the safety of passengers, especially those that need more help and attention. I am writing this, after knowing there was absolutely no help for a passenger that just alighted bus 49 at 11:25am on 19 Feb 2025 with a toddler in stroller. Not only did the bus stop quite far away from ledge/pavement, bus driver did not bother to help or anything, leaving the poor mother having to struggle and almost dropped the kid off the stroller. Singaporeans these days are a total disgrace! I personally also have experienced signalling the need of using rear doors to board to help carry stroller up the bus, and drivers would just close the door, causing the stroller or myself to be hit. Why people with strollers are not given the same respect and help compared to those on wheelchairs? The picture I took is one of the buses that I have either experienced or witnessed.

Jurung East MRT and then 10 minutes walk to the building. The office space is interior and HAS NO WINDOWS. What a dump of an office. Common toilets are not so clean, and give it a fair rating. Perhaps the other office spaces are better...the ones with windows, but no this one.
Not an ideal place to setup a service centre. Not easily accessible

Very bad experience with this building main lobby security guard. No eating, no drinking, no talking is allowed even me n my son was waiting after office hours! This uncle is quite rude most of time. There are 2 preschool at the building, cannot avoid to have kids running around or wait inside the lobby for taxi or fetching. Hope the building management can solve the problem!
I'm rating it because Google asked me to. It wasn't notably good or bad, other than the signage, which was helpful.
Great offices well deserved with hawker centre, infant care and Gym

Came here to take-out the Bittergourd Fish Soup as I heard good things about this soup. It was indeed well worth it as the generous portion makes me regretted getting the big portion. While all the ingredients were fresh, there's a sour profile to the soup(seemingly due to the seasoning) which I do not quite like as the soup was already robust enough from all its ingredients.

tried bittergourd fish soup.
Its is truly value for money. Very worth it. Air con environment.
Rice is soft. Fish slices is fresh.
The new noodle stall is pretty good in terms of food and presentation, just need to improve their work flow to save faster.

This place can get crowded during the lunch hour. The crowd seems lesser around 11.30 am or after 1 pm. The food prices here are affordable and delicious. The bitter ground soup stall is one of my favourite stalls; something that is not too heavy, savoury teochew-style soup.

Great variety of food options, takeaway or dine-ins are available. Prices are really affordable with zichar and even salad. Super crowded during lunch time and seating are quite limited. Zichar is highly recommended!
The fried chicken  🍗  is the killer, extremely crispy and tasty that goes well with their light but fluffy nasi bryani rice. Sedap!!!
Yummy food court is NOT open on Sunday. Info is wrong please update this Synegy link page.

Juice and fruit are not bad！

Forget everything else….

Coffee shop aunty was extremely rude. First time here and did not know it was cash only for the coffee shop. At the counter she mentioned is cash only, and as a person who mostly uses QR, I didnt have much cash. I only lacked $1.1 and she yelled at me "no money dont eat, put it all back" in front of everyone at the queue😅 A little embarrassing honestly, woudnt go back there. Aunty needs to work on her communication skills

Muslim stall is very friendly.

Coffee stall auntie is no good, her face looks like I owe her $.

I don't think I will drink coffee here again.

Good food deserves good review. Had the same lunch twice in 3 days. The TOM YUM soup is superb. No joke. Generous portion of seafood & mushrooms at a reasonable price. Thank you for coming to the West!!

Tom Yum Thick & creamy soup broth, I had the chicken version, meat were tender and yummy.

Highly recommended
First taste of the food and it instantly brought me to Thailand! Affordable, authentic and generous portion. Will definitely be back for more!

Must Try the Pineapple fried rice!
Came here for a few time but the fried rice always sold out, have to come earlier for the limited fried rice
found this rare gem during lunch hour, surprised by flavour and taste authentic Thai food, good value for its price 👌

Dropped by to grab a drink while waiting for my bus. One of the few shops open early in the morning before 7am. Entire place is right and simple arrangement. Two gentlemen were fast pace enough to clear Q fast in the morning. Nice place for breakfast around the area.

Tried their ice milo and impressively fantastic. Super nice! For the price of $2.10 is so worth it! Good quality cup are used too.
Excellent coffee! Aroma & consistency are always top notch. Highly recommend their mee siam & mee rebus!
Good for 3 meal a day...waffle must try
Like the ambient and also the food very much.  The boss is so friendly and he makes our lunch so enjoyable.  Will definitely be coming back to try other dishes.  I love the spaghetti and the chicken.
We really enjoyed our lunch here on a  Saturday afternoon. Service was exceptional, they were very hospitable. Food was fulfilling and tasted delicious! 😋 Would definitely come back here again!

Konnichiwa dear onisan means bro Chee Yong & lovely Wiwik,

Arigato for your oshii means tasty heavenly pork chop, salmon & spicy chicken wings, keep it up the good work you guys rock, Happy CNY have a great dinner & weekend with your families always kiotsukete means take good care dears....^-^....

Best regards
YukikoK
had the limited edition duck confit & miso carbonara, and other dishes like the sandwich and deviled eggs and it was so so good! :) would def come back

Nice quiet place for a weekend relax brunch. Their services are excellent and food are delicious . The salmon was cook to perfect tenderness and definitely would come back to try other recommended food.

Visited Nandhana’s Restaurant for the first time and tried their briyani chicken rice. It was really delicious and even came with a hidden egg inside the rice! The chicken was a little on the dry side, but the overall flavor made it a very satisfying lunch.

The environment is notably clean and comfortable, which makes for a great dining experience. I also noticed the staff are very attentive and really care about their customers. Definitely a solid spot in the Jurong East area.

We ordered tomato soup tandoori brocholli roti naan veg kadai chicken biryani and chickem fry.. Tomato soup was horrible.. Roti and Naan was like stone.. veg kadai was uncooked..Tandoori Brocholli was uncookeed.. I paid 105 dollars in total.. They deserve only 10 dollars .. We left 30 percent food.. I recommend ppl not to go there at all especially weekends.. Waste of time and Money

Friendly lady boss who serve us.

Quiet at 1pm when I arrive. Excellent chicken dump briyani.

I love the flavor. The yogurt salad is solid so is the curry. The spicy curry spice up my day! Thanks 😊

Big portion and the chicken is tender! I will be back again!
Thank for the great chicken briyani!

Located at ICON @ IBP Level 1

The taste is on point. Excellent service. Love the presentation of the food.
My husband and truly enjoyed the nan, palak manner, lollypop chicken and vegetarian thali
The place did not have ac and was a little hot and the biriyani was a bit average. Rest all was good.

Tucked away at Nordic European Center at International business park, 55 Degrees is a hidden gem for anyone seeking quality and comfort in a café setting.
I dropped by for a quick bite and was pleasantly surprised by the beef wrap — warm, generously filled, and perfectly seasoned with a savoury kick that lingered just right. It was hearty without being heavy, and every bite felt thoughtfully put together.

Paired it with their cold brew, which was smooth, bold, and refreshingly crisp — no bitterness, just a clean caffeine hit that really elevated the meal. You can tell they take their coffee seriously.

What really made the visit memorable, though, was the friendly and attentive service. The staff were warm, welcoming, and took the time to engage without being intrusive. You can tell there’s pride behind the counter, and it adds to the overall charm of the place.

The couple mending the store are both VERY very sweet! the food & drinks made are also seemingly made with lots of love, so do expect a bit of a longer serving time as compared to the usual cafes.
I like coming to this place to get my fill of lunch as the food is super delicious, and the drinks are made with much careful precision. I love their donburi bowls, baked rice, strawberry matcha latte and especially their muffins! super fragrant and yummy 💛

pictures do the food no justice 🤣

Dropped by for a cuppa and ordered their flat white and latte. Firstly the service is prompt and professional. Staff Roy is friendly and attentive. Ambience is comfy and cozy. Gives a smal town cafe vibe. Enjoyed the service more than the coffee even though it was nicely made. The coffee blend is just too bitter for my liking.  Price wise is on the affordable side and portion is good too. Overall a good drinking and chilling experience. So giving it a good 4 out of 5 star.

Went here for lunch with colleagues (a large group, to add) and the staff was very accommodating. The baked rice and pasta were not too bad. Drinks were good, especially the matcha latte (larger cup would be great!). We might have given the staff a lot of pressure because of our group size 😅 So a very huge kudos to the two staff for rushing out the food for everyone in the café!
Matcha was great and tasted legit, aglio olio was well-seasoned too, would pop by again for sure!

A nice and modern mall conveniently located at the Jurong East transportation hub, with sheltered walkways linking it to two other popular malls in the area. The mall is spacious, thoughtfully designed with plenty of greenery, and offers a pleasant shopping experience.

Some sections are non-air-conditioned, which can feel warm during the afternoon, but the open concept adds to its vibrant, lifestyle-focused atmosphere. With a wide variety of food and beverage outlets, it’s a great destination for dining, shopping, and leisure.

Mall is around 10 years old located in Jurong east central and beside the MRT station. Good shopper traffic, more home goods, Fnb and lifestyle/services shops such as Nittori, Dreame and Paradise restaurants etc. Toilet cleanliness and  amenities can be improved. Carpark is easy to navigate.
Great place for casual shopping. Good spread of food. Easy connection to the jurong east train station.

As a frequent shopper in the west, I’ve spent countless hours at Westgate, and it has firmly established itself as one of my go-to destinations. Located at 3 Gateway Drive and directly connected to Jurong East MRT Station, its accessibility is virtually unbeatable . The mall is also linked to JEM and IMM via covered walkways, creating a massive interconnected shopping belt .

Westgate truly shines as a family and lifestyle hub. The layout is a mix of sleek, air-conditioned corridors and its signature open-air "Courtyard," which houses popular alfresco dining spots . However, the crown jewel for families is definitely the Westgate Wonderland on Level 4. This fantasy-themed outdoor playground is a massive hit with children, featuring giant interactive structures where they can burn off energy .

Foodies will be in heaven here. The mall boasts an impressive spectrum of dining options. You can grab a quick, affordable bite at the Food Republic food court in the basement or indulge in world-class ramen at Ippudo and next-level Japanese barbecue at Yakiniku Like . For those with a sweet tooth, the newly opened Kind Kone offers fantastic all-vegan ice cream with unique flavors like "Blue Planet," while Flipper’s serves up their legendary Japanese souffle pancakes . Don’t miss the Isetan Supermarket in the basement, which is a treasure trove of premium Japanese groceries and ready-to-eat meals .

On the retail front, Westgate covers all the bases. You’ll find major anchors like a sprawling two-story Uniqlo and a Japanese department store, alongside high-street favorites like Zara and COS . Tech enthusiasts can explore the Samsung Experience Store or Best Denki, while beauty lovers will appreciate the large Sephora outlet .

If I have to levy one major criticism, it’s the notorious parking situation. The carpark has a single, tightly designed entry and exit point that leads to the pick-up/drop-off area, creating absolute chaos during peak hours . Many reviewers report it can take 30 minutes just to exit . My advice? Take the MRT. It drops you right at the mall’s doorstep, saving you the headache.

Despite the driving frustrations, Westgate remains a vibrant and well-maintained mall. With its excellent store mix, incredible variety of food, and dedicated kids' facilities, it offers a complete package that caters to everyone from office workers to young families. It’s a solid 4 out of 5 stars for me.

Right next to JEM but feels a bit more "atas." The outdoor playground on the top floor is great for families with kids. Lots of popular restaurants like Tim Ho Wan and Din Tai Fung here. The link bridge to Jurong East MRT is very convenient and fully sheltered, which is a life-saver during those heavy afternoon downpours.

We visited IMM Building, Singapore’s largest outlet mall, and had a good shopping experience. This place really lives up to what many reviewers say — it has all varieties from high-end brands to low-end shops, making it easier to find what you need under one roof. You can discover everything from fashion and sportswear to electronics, home goods, and plenty of dining options.

Many visitors also love the discounts on big brands (up to around 80% at outlet stores), and the mall is spacious and easy to navigate. There are lots of food and beverage outlets to take a break while shopping.

Crowds can get heavier on weekends, and some items in outlet stores may not be the latest season stock, but overall it’s a great place for bargain hunting and casual shopping.

Overall experience was pleasant — 4 stars ⭐⭐⭐⭐.

Hum log IMM Building gaye aur overall shopping experience kaafi achhi rahi. Jaise online reviews mein bhi log likhte hain, yahan high-end se leke low-end tak sab varieties mil jaati hain, jis se shopping easy ho jati hai — fashion, sportswear, electronics, home goods aur bahut saare food options sab ek hi jagah par.

Bohot saare visitors ko big brands par ache discounts bhi milte hain aur mall ka layout kaafi spacious hai jisme easily explore kar sakte ho.

Weekend par thoda crowd zyada ho sakta hai aur outlet stores mein sab items latest season ke nahi milte, lekin overall yeh mall bargain hunting aur casual shopping ke liye ek achhi jagah hai.

Overall experience achha raha — 4 stars ⭐⭐⭐⭐.
Singapore's only outlet mall with all major brands under one roof. You will get good deals from top brands here. Don't forget to plan to visit here when you travel to Singapore. Must visit mall.

IMM Mall is a great spot for shopping, especially if you’re looking for outlet deals. It has a wide range of stores, including brands like Coach, Michael Kors, Kate Spade, Charles & Keith, Pedro, Cotton On, and plenty of footwear outlets.
Getting there is easy—take the MRT to Jurong East MRT Station and follow the signs. It’s about a 10 minute walk, and you’ll pass JEM on the way.
There are also lots of dining options, making it a good place to spend a few hours. If you’re shopping, if you spend over SGD 100 in a store, you can request a GST refund receipt—just show your passport at the time of purchase. You can then claim the refund at the airport during departure.
Overall, a solid choice if you’re planning a shopping trip in Singapore.

For my family and me living in the west, IMM is one of our our go-to spots for shopping, leisure, and meals because it is just so convenient.
Also, being directly connected to Jurong East MRT and the Bus Interchange makes it exceptionally accessible.

Have visited IMM since I was a kid. That was like decades ago. Since then, new wings were constructed, parking has expanded, new links to the Jurong shopping cluster has been built and stores have come and go. Really like how there everything here, from daily necessities to outlet stores. Weekend parking is a nightmare though and the gantry should be upgraded to reduce the jam at the carpark entrance. Also, glad that level 3 is pet friendly thanks to the new BYD cafe.

FairPrice Xtra at JEM is an impressively large and bustling supermarket that rarely sees a quiet moment. It’s a go-to destination for shoppers looking for variety, convenience, and value—all under one roof. The store offers an extensive range of products, from fresh produce and household essentials to international snacks and specialty items. One of the most exciting recent additions is the Lotte section, which brings in a vibrant selection of Korean treats and groceries. It’s a delightful surprise for fans of Korean cuisine and culture, adding a unique twist to the already diverse offerings. Whether you’re doing your weekly grocery run or just browsing for something new, FairPrice Xtra at JEM never disappoints.

I love this NTUC FairPrice Xtra at the JEM shopping mall. It is quite MASSIVE and has a SUPER wide variety of products and it is very well stocked. As a "Xtra", this NTUC FairPrice sells far more than food and beverage items - it also sells electrical and electronic products, clothing and cookware. So it is in my opinion a one stop shop for everything that you need except if you are looking for furniture.

A trip to a supermarket is one of my favourite pastimes. I normally like to visit during off peak hours as I don’t really like crowds.
This is one of the better FairPrice supermarkets in Singapore. Big, clean, spacious and sells quite a lot of stuff.
For vegans and vegetarians, there are plenty of plant-based items too. Happy Shopping.

** Chinese vegan/ vegetarian products are labeled with the Chinese characters 素 or 齋.

* Difference between 素 and齋 *
** 素 means vegan / vegetarian
** 齋 means Fasting (for religious reasons)
For details, just use any AI apps

May 2026: they started selling takeaway korean chicken and kimbap, etc 😋

Plus points for the new Lotte mart which opened Dec 2025. Getting my K food supplies here!

I would like to express my sincere thanks to the person who had picked up the Samsung ear buds that I had unknowingly dropped it into the freezer that housed the CP brand dumplings.  This is the new pair of ear buds that was a free gift that came with the purchased of Samsung mobile phone that my hubhy had just bought it at the Jem Samsung outlet this evening at around 6.30pm. I had paired it with my own mobile phone and kept the ear buds in the handbag and happily went to fairprice to shop for food. When we were on the way home ,I wanted to take out the ear buds then only I realised it was disappeared . I felt quite upset about it as it was a new gift . After settling down ,We decided to make a trip back to fairprice, upon arrival , we checked with the customer service counter staff, by the name of Kanagavalli and IDA, to our relief the new Samsung ear buds was kept in the drawer . Both staff shared that a customer who had picked it up from the freezer and returned it back to them. We would like to thank this customer being kind to return my lost ear buds . We also like to thank both IDA and Kanagavalli for their good service.
Once agian thank you to all these kind persons . It made our shopping at fairprice a very pleasure and remembrance experience .

Note to Patrons: the Roasted Chickens under 'READY TO EAT ' section is NOT HALAL. Today I came to this outlet and saw Roasted Honey Sriracha chicken. After the staff assisted me to pack, I took the chicken and was about to head to the cashier when this kind Uncle (Giant Staff) told me; 'better don't buy, this one non-halal'. He then told his staff how can they serve me a non-halal item. I appreciated his kind act and holding accountability to a mistake by the staff.

To the Management, would appreciate quick action to put up a signage to caution patrons that the roasted chicken is actually non-halal.

the supermarket is so pretty now  anf merchandise and shelves are more organised and systematic. the new arragment makes the place looks more spacious and flow better when shopping. more modern feel and no longer looks like a big provision shop. looks brighter pretier and sensible. we love it. we like the live seafood section and managed to bought a live seabass for a good deal. fantastic shopping tume yesterday. will definitely come back to Giant @IMM again.

Giant IMM has undergone a revamp, emerging as a smaller and more focused hypermarket. While the core daily necessities and the reliable Meadows house brand remain, several sections are now gone, including bicycles & kick-scooters as well as clothing & shoes sections.

The electronics section is still there, which is a plus for many. Ultimately, it's a streamlined store for groceries and essentials. There is a wide array of eateries just steps away from Giant for a convenient meal after your shop. A considerable stop for your routine grocery run.

On 06/02/2026 , Had a very frustrating experience at the self check-out counter. I wanted to redeem a Yuu app voucher ($3 off with $15 spend). I had already scanned my Yuu membership before scanning my items for points, which I explained clearly. The staff (Nirosha) insisted that I could not redeem the voucher because I was already at the payment stage and claimed it should have been done before scanning items. I repeatedly explained that I was not trying to redeem points and only needed help with the voucher, and even showed her my phone to clarify. Despite this, she remained persistent and accused me of trying to teach her how to do her job. Another staff later assisted and confirmed that the voucher could be redeemed as I had explained. This situation could have been resolved easily if the staff had listened or checked with a colleague instead of misguiding customers. Very disappointing service.

I want to comment a cashier by the name of Munaliza during my recent visit at Imm. She is very efficient, friendly and helpful. We managed to use our DFI voucher after many tries at the checkout counter! Keep up your good work! Cheers!🥰🥰We will definitely come back and shop at Giant IMM

Best stall for their Briyani Chicken (rice). Never disappoint! Always long queue and crowded with regulars. Must try their fried chicken! Crispy and fragrance, absolutely delicious! Reasonably priced too! Should try if you haven’t done so yet! Highly recommended!

Highly recommended for its fluffy biryani rice and the extremely crispy fried chicken, coupled with a dash of appertizer.  The stall owner make it a point to constantly fry the chicken at close intervals so that customers will be able to consume pipping hot and crispy fried chicken made fresh.  I would certainly say this is probably the best halal biryani rice you can find in Singapore.

Omg. The queue is always long. But the service was very fast also. Yes. The fried chicken is very very good. I had to queue once more for 2nd helping. But i was dissappointed as I didn't get to try the briyani rice as it was sold out at 1pm. I think the briyani is also as good. But Im glad I get to eat 2 x fried chicken. A must try when you are at tradehub 21.

i considered the best fried chicken nasi brani at West Side of Singapore. at value price of $4.50.  This is a highly popular stall at tradehub21, the queue starts at 11:45am and the queue is super long after 12pm. shop is closed on sat and sun and at night.

the fried chicken is very juicy and very crispy outside ! one of the best !
Briyani was superb. Fried chicken was top of the world. Worth it to have my lunch here despite the normal long queue.

Wow saw this promotion at vision exchange….think is a great price, can buy as Christmas gift. The staff was very friendly and eventually recommend more value for money good idea gifts. Thank you and you guys make my day. 🤗

Good shopping experience with friendly staffs. Always love the morning vibes from the staffs before going into my office. Special shout out to store manager Michelle and her team!

2 Jan 24
Back to work today and excited to see 20% storewide discount! Staff as usual is happy to share the promotion information and welcome us back again for new promotion range.. YEAH, cant wait to shop for another round to stock up tomorrow! I wonder does the staff owns the store, good at selling!! heehee...
Attractive items and neat display!! Well done and friendly customer service!!
Neat and tidy stall. Good customer service. Thumbs up!
went there at 11.30pm on Fri, closed till 7am???

Probably the last vestiges of a generation lost in memory, this market is wet alright. No frills. No thrills. Just stuffs that only Merdeka and Pioneer generation aunties will buy.

That's only if you cook and don't have a helper at home. Decidedly utilitarian thus is how a market was in the 60s
The all item very fresh in the market

Hawker food. An indian stall selling tosei, appom and variety of food. There is a corner malay stall selling lots of kuih2, mee siam, also prata and some others. A few stall not open yet. These 2 stall open as early as 5.30am.
Nostalgic experience,old vibes nice food.if you are a mee goteng fanatic this is a place to go.variety of malay foods amd chinese foods.highly recomend
Went there for breakfast. How food prices has gone up after the year end clean up of the market and food centre from my last visit last year.

This is a market you literally visit in the middle of the night. By the time we arrived around 2 a.m., many items were already sold out and we were picking through the scraps.

That said, it's an experience you should try at least once. We scored 5 huge tiger prawns for just $40, which is incredible value considering they'd likely cost $20–$30 per piece in a restaurant. You'll see many traditional fish vendors selling their fresh catch straight from the ships.

Just be mindful that the primary audience here are fish mongers supplying traditional wet market stalls, so navigate accordingly.

Note: bring your Singpass, to get through security.
Not the usual touristy place, but highly recommend it for offbeat Singapore experience. Great for taking pictures also.
Wide arrays of seafood, ranging from fishes to prawns to squids. Prices are better than what you'll normally purchased from  supermarkets or markets.

Come here only after 10pm to get your fresh fish and prawns!
No cutting services though. Be prepared to cut and gut your own purchase.
Drive in or get a rental car. Or just Grab there.
Nice experience.... Lots of fresh Fishes...

place_name: The Gym Pod @ International Business Park Jurong East
review_text:
Only has a smith machine and dumbbells for the weight section. It is quite an old minded thing to have only smith machine and not free weight barbell, people would assume that is the standard, even public’s sector gyms have that. Furthermore, there’s no information on the website where I could see the equipments of the gym.

Paid for 2 sessions for myself, left after 30 mins. Not recommended.

place_name: The Gym Pod @ International Business Park Jurong East
review_text: So convenient to visit gym near my place without signing up for expensive memberships. Awesome!

place_name: The Gym Pod @ International Business Park Jurong East
review_text: It's a chill place to work out at, especially at night.

place_name: The Gym Pod @ International Business Park Jurong East
review_text: Very positive experience. Place is clean and easily accessible.

place_name: The Gym Pod @ International Business Park Jurong East

review_text:
Huge appreciation to the incredible coaching team for guiding us through such an empowering 8-week challenge! A massive shout-out to Nicholas and Jay for their constant motivation, positive energy, and unwavering support. They truly kept us going every step of the way. I absolutely loved how the coaches structured and guided the journey, pushing us to stay consistent and believe in the process.

The results speak for themselves: through this journey, I lost 4.7 kg in body weight, achieved a 5.0 kg reduction in fat mass, while skeletal muscle mass increased by 0.3 kg. My body fat percentage dropped significantly from 34.3% to 29.6%, visceral fat reduced from level 11 to 7, and my bio age decreased by 2 years - the results I’m truly proud of!

I’ll definitely be joining the next 8-week challenge and sincerely hope Nicholas and Jay will be leading us again. Thank you for inspiring real transformation, building confidence and proving what consistency and great coaching can achieve!

Joined 6 months ago and have been going regularly 5 days a week. This is the first gym I’ve been able to stay consistent with for such a long time. There’s enough variety to keep me interested and seeing my progression in strength and body composition also keeps me motivated.

Special mention to Jay and Nic, who have been the main coaches for 8 week challenge, they’re encouraging, give helpful tips, and consistently check in. In 8 weeks, my bio age decreased, body fat % reduced, and muscle mass increased. Even before the 8 weeks, I could already feel a huge shift in my body, posture, mood, energy etc because of regular sessions here. They put in effort to understand everyone individually and adjust their approach. I mainly come to de-stress and switch off from life, so I really appreciate that they are not pushy or adding extra pressure to perform. The environment makes it easy to just show up and do your workout according to how you feel that day. Great day > push harder; lousy day > at least I showed up.

Started going to BFT last year September out of curiosity and national day promo. Ended up continuing until Dec and lost 3kg. Decided to try out the 8 week challenge to challenge myself  and lost another 3kg. The coaches are friendly and approachable. Special shout out to Jay and Nic for motivating me throughout the 8 weeks! Will join the next 8 week challenge to push myself further💪💪

Well done BFT Jurong with your new looks, especially the toilets and shower area, there are like 5 stars hotel. I always enjoyed the strength classes held over lunch hours coached by Jay, he is  sharp and able to correct / make minor adjustments in our movement which indeed help alot. Thank you Jay.

Lovely layout with lots of natural light and wide space.
great bunch of instructors/coaches who looked out and made sure we did our exercises safely, with proper form and to do our best.

Special mention to Coach Jay who took us for the session. his knowledge and experience, along with the positive vibes he brings to the session made us feel ready to take on the exercise.

will return to BFT Jurong

New Revamped Jurong East Swimming Complex with Lazy and waterplay areas for the kids. Hv a good wave generation pool which operates every hour.A bit sad that the water slides has been replaced with a new children water play area for toddlers and young children. Not enough shaded canopies for users of the facility to keep their belongings the heat on hot sunny days. No food vending machines for snacks, only hv a drinks vending machine.
Fun time with kiddos. The toilets are great with large cubicles and compartments to hang your dry clothes. Definitely a comeback for my family.

Although I must say that I missed the huge slides that my generation grew up with. But it is a nice transitional change to me being a Dad now, bringing my little kiddos in what is now a kids play area.

Also the toilets have had a face-lift. Kids changing room is nice and clean. Adults changing room is equally good.

They had a baby pool, kids playground, kids splash area, lazy river and a wave pool. Wave pool had waves every hour lasting about 15mins each time. Lazy river is where we spent most of our time at, easy going and fun. Rates are affordable with activesg. Minus one star because I'd prefer having the slides.

Went with family on a Saturday. Place was busy but still have space for a good swim. Kids enjoyed the new water play area and wave pool (though wave was mild and very far apart). The lazy river is still there.
I took my granddaughter for swimming classes here. She is six years old and just enjoys the class. The coach is good. The pool is well maintained.

Thank you for taking over Cathay! I really enjoyed the Premiure Class experience. Food ordering was easy and steamless. The seat are spacious and comfortable. Thank you for taking over Cathay — the Premiere Class experience at Shaw Theatres was truly impressive 👏

From the moment we stepped in, everything felt elevated. The ordering process was seamless and fuss-free, making the whole experience feel smooth and well thought out.

The seats were incredibly spacious and plush, perfect for fully relaxing throughout the movie. What really stood out was the thick, cozy blanket — a small touch, but it made a huge difference in comfort and warmth.

Overall, it’s a premium cinema experience that delivers on both comfort and convenience. Definitely worth it for a more luxurious movie night — will be back again! 🎬✨

First of all, thank you Shaw for taking over Cathay and giving us West-siders a proper cinema again! Super grateful to have a nice place nearby to catch movies. I want to commend the team for transforming the space into a much more comfortable and movie-vibe environment compared to the previous operator (specifically the hallway and toilets). Took many photos during my trip.

I recently had the opportunity to experience the Premiere halls (specifically Premiere 1 & Premiere 2). I understand that this is a new team and they are still familiarising themselves with the SOPs. I can see they are trying their best. Still, I would like to share both the positive aspects and areas for improvement based on my visits.

What I Loved:
1) I tried the seafood pizza & it was delicious. Although the price is on the higher side, the quality justifies it. The food definitely stands out as one of the highlights of the Premiere experience.

2) The adjustable sofa chairs are very comfortable & the overall privacy in both Premiere halls is excellent. It really elevates the experience and makes the upgrade feel worthwhile.

3) When I caught The Predator at Premiere 2 hall, the sound was loud, immersive & clear. Exactly what I expect from a premium hall. Sadly wasn’t the case for Premiere 1 hall.

Areas of Improvement:
1) The lighting for the seat numbers is very bright in Premiere 1 hall (refer to photo) and was distracting during the movie. Dimming them slightly would improve the overall ambience.

2) Because the team is new, I understand they’re still adapting but there were noticeable inconsistencies:
- Premiere 2 hall, I was given cutlery and tissue with my pizza. Premiere 1 hall, I wasn’t.
- I encountered issues with both Premiere halls: once I couldn’t use the discount code and another time my order didn’t arrive until staff repeatedly came back and forth to resolve it.
The staff are young and clearly trying their best but the service processes may need refinement so the experience feels smooth and premium.

3) When I watched Wicked in Premiere 1, the audio sounded muffled and soft, especially from the front speakers. This was quite noticeable and very different from my excellent experience in Premiere 2. I’m not sure if this was due to the sound mix provided for the film or the hall setup but the inconsistency between the two halls was obvious.

Despite the hiccups, the improvements to the cinema are significant & I appreciate having such a comfortable well-designed place close to home. Just need adjustments to service consistency and technical setup (JEM please don’t disappoint Shaw good sound system reputation).

Thank you.
