Stepping into the Omni-Theatre at Science Centre Singapore felt like entering a completely different world. This isn’t your usual cinema — the massive dome screen and immersive surround sound make you feel like you’re inside the movie, whether you’re flying through space or exploring nature. The visuals are incredibly sharp and engaging, and it’s a great mix of entertainment and learning for both kids and adults.
One big tip: visit on a weekday. That’s truly the best time to go. The crowd is much smaller, and it feels almost like you have the theatre to yourself, especially if you’re going with family. The experience is more relaxed, quieter, and way more enjoyable without the usual weekend rush.
Ticket prices are also very reasonable. Singaporeans and Permanent Residents pay around $8, while tourists pay about $14, which is excellent value considering how unique the experience is compared to a regular movie theatre.
What I also love is how convenient the location is. After the Omni-Theatre, you can easily spend more time nearby. KidsSTOP™ is perfect for younger children with hands-on learning and play, while Snow City is just next door if you want something fun and cooling. It’s easy to turn this into a full half-day or even full-day family outing.
Overall, the Omni-Theatre is a great place to slow down, learn something new, and enjoy quality time together. If you’re looking for something different to do in Singapore — especially on a weekday — this is definitely worth checking out.

Brought my kids here today. It was pleasant even though 20mins into the show, one fell asleep and the other one was looking forward to the “good part” as we were expecting a show about space. However it was more of a NASA showcase. Commented that she came to learn about space, not rockets 😅

Not all of the footage made use of the unique omni dome but this could be due to the film’s origin. Sound could be improved. We sat at the top near the exit. Because the back speakers were off, the show lacked impact. It was also warm as if the aircon was off.

Parking was full. Some cars had to park on the side road inside carpark. Understood from staff that this was unusual as it’s the last day of Robofest at Science Centre. Perhaps some traffic control is required for busy days such as today.

We’ll come again for other shows and hope that the management look into the above especially the show experience. Still think it’s a core memory opportunity for kids as it did for me when I visited the omni theatre back in my school days.

As part of SG60, there was a promotion to visit the science centre and the Omni theatre free.  It was a bit confusing though as although the ticket listed this two places as free, you would have to book separately for each movie slot.  I learned later that each admission ticket that day could book one movie.  However they opened up another queue for walk in admission.  And I believed anyone who used the walk in managed to go in.

I managed to watch all the films that day except for Stars Dreaming.  Animal Kingdom was the most interesting film with nice visuals and interesting facts.  Cities of the future was quite a boring one that covered a few cities around the world on the issues of environment sustainability.  Some audiences actually walked out of it.  Kitz the cat's Secret of the Space Station was the only film with a storyline and also made use of the Onimax effect to make you nauseous and dizzy in a few scenes.  It was an animation targeted at children although the theme can be quite dark.  It was still educational.  I believed it was a Korean production and some dialogues were quite awkward. Overall I would only recommend Animal Kingdom.

I found the dome screen rather dim though.  But all the pixels on the screen were working fine.  The images were not clear and the animal kingdom felt like a low Res film.  The theatre is digital 8k though according to the staff.

In terms of environment, you need to put up with talking children and eating.  Even the mother next to me kept using her hp and I need to tell her about it.  Later she started to provide narration to her child.  So be prepared to put up with the annoyances of these people.

There were a lot of stairs climbing up and down.  If you are mobility challenged you could seek help from the staff and gained access via a lift.

This theatre along with the science centre would be torn down for another new building.  Not sure would there be an Omni theatre in the future.

Long ago I watched other shows like Grand Canyon, Space walk and diving in the sea.  All were good documentaries.

Got vouchers and brought my kids (5, 10, 12 and 14yo) to watch "Ocean Paradise". The venue is very clean (no eating and drinking allowed), comfortable and has a magnificent dome screen. The movie length of 35min was perfect for my 5yo (not too long) but also not so short that the trip wasn't worth it for my 14yo. The images from this show were stunningly beautiful and the messaging was clear. Nothing scary whatsoever, which is great for my easily-scared kids, but still having a strong storyline to keep everyone engaged throughout. Volume of sounds were also not too loud (unlike movie theaters) but still clear. Do check out the highly rated Indian Food stall just outside the omnitheater, or adjourn to the Regale for some pasta (tts what we ate anyways) if spice is not an option. Bottom line: fantastic immersive movie experience for kids (and adults)!!

I think the omni is losing its touch. The documentary is very boring. People expect to feel " they are in the movie" and yet we only feel like it is looking at a documentary. A lot of people end up sleeping.

I  brought my child here and saw mold mainly in the ball pit area, so we avoided it and did not visit for a long time. After a recent renovation and the addition of new web play structures, I expected a total renovation and proper cleaning. Apparently not.

However, the situation appears worse. Mold is now visibly present not only in high-contact areas children touch but also on elevated, low-contact surfaces, together with heavy dust accumulation. This indicates a systemic hygiene and moisture-control issue rather than normal wear or “aging fabric.” Several damaged areas are only patched with tape, few digital interactive screens removed / are no longer working, and a new consent form liability waiver has been introduced since many kids injured themselves.

ChatGPT confirmed the presence of mold, accumulation of skin oil, bacteria, dust and dirt over the years without insufficient cleaning regime that creates this situation. It is presenting legitimate health and safety concern. I have attached the screenshot on chatGPT analysis.

The current presence of 2 cleaning lady is too late and insufficient. They need to do total refurbishing and special mold disinfection is required.

A vibrant and well-designed indoor playground that’s perfect for toddlers and younger kids. Yooland at Westgate feels brand new — clean, brightly lit, and packed with interactive zones, including a massive ball pit, mini trampolines, a pretend play area (“Space Kitchen”), and lots of padded play equipment. The outer-space theme adds a playful futuristic vibe.

However, at $25 for just an hour, it’s on the pricey side. That said, you’re paying for a safe, air-conditioned environment with clear zoning, attentive staff, and thoughtful touches like ample seating for parents.

Worth visiting at least once if you’re in the area or looking for a treat day out — just go early to avoid peak crowd times.

We had an amazing time at Yooland! I brought my kids, and they absolutely loved the wide variety of play areas. There’s plenty of space for them to explore, which kept them entertained for hours. The staff was also incredibly friendly and accommodating, making sure we had everything we needed and keeping the environment fun and safe. Overall, it’s a fantastic place to bring kids for a day of active fun. We loved it and will definitely be back.

A lovely small rainforest park tucked away in the west that feels surprisingly immersive despite its compact size. The walking trails are well-paved and shaded by tall trees, making it comfortable for both casual strolls and light workouts. There’s also a playground and a simple fitness corner, so it works well for families as well as those looking for a quick exercise session

One of the highlights is the lookout point overlooking the quarry — a peaceful spot where you can pause, enjoy the greenery, and listen to the sounds of nature. Wildlife sightings are quite common too; it’s not unusual to spot colourful birds, squirrels, and even monkeys if you’re lucky. History buffs will appreciate the remnants of WWII structures such as old pillboxes scattered around the park, which add a subtle layer of heritage to the natural scenery

The only downside at the moment is that the path leading to the Syonan Chureito (the WWII Japanese remembrance site) is under maintenance, so access is temporarily closed

It was my first and hope it's not the last visit to this place. I really want to go there again.

Really liked the place and enjoyed walking around this place. This would definitely be a cute for all of your sadness.

Didn't realise there was a quarry in the park, took a long walk in nature and found many wonderful things! Was caught by a sudden downpour, appreciated the thick trees around the area which sheltered me from the rain.

Good mix of trails, some paths are cobblestones so be in a proper footwear.

Caught a squirrel in action too!

🌳A lush greenery that is just a stone throw away from the Bukit Batok Housing Estate, providing residents living here a green lung to relax, exercise and be with nature.
🚶‍➡️🏃‍➡️Many came here in the morning for exercises and brisk walk.
🛝🏋️At the Bt Batok Nature Park, besides having numerous shelters and walking paths, there is a Children Playground for little ones to enjoy, a Foot Reflexology Path and an Adult Fitness Corner that are next to the Playground.
🏞️But the centrepiece is the Lookout Point, where one can rest, take in the sight of the tranquil water and it's scenic surrounding.
⛩️Another interesting place around the Park with World War II history is Syonan Chureito Memorial
🚧Unfortunately, the access path from the park proper is closed for safety reason and is undergoing stabilisation work which is expected to be completed end Dec 2027.
Nevertheless, the site can be reached from Lorong Sesuai.
🪨Fun fact:
Bukit Batok Nature Park was formerly a granite quarry site.
Like many other granite quarry around, it ceased operation for safety and environmental reasons. And was then transformed into a nature park for all to enjoy.
Bukit batok natural park nice and peaceful park see sunrise depend on the cloud good place exercise 2 jan 2026

Very nice place to either have a slow walk, trail walk, or trail run. There are challenging slopes & steps, but there are also friendly sections to catch your breath. Distance wise, one can choose to walk from a short 3 KM route or a longer 6KM+ route. With the terrain, it is a good place to burn some calories and at the same time, enjoy the greenery with some  sightings of animals in the wild. So far, I have encountered calugo, blue diamond coral snake, pangolin, wild boar, monitor lizards, drongo (bird) and of course, monkeys (plenty of them).

Went hiking at Bukit Timah and Rifle Range and it turned out to be a really refreshing experience. The trail was surrounded by tall trees and thick greenery, making the whole walk feel calm and peaceful. As I went down the wooden steps, I could hear the sounds of nature all around me—birds, insects, and the rustling of leaves.
Along the way, I noticed small details that made the hike even more special, like tiny plants growing out of old wood and patches of moss spreading across fallen logs. It reminded me how alive the forest is, even in the smallest places.
The hike wasn’t too easy, especially with the long paths and steps, but it felt satisfying. I managed to cover about 7.8 kilometers, and even though I was tired, it was a good kind of tired. Overall, it was a simple but meaningful experience—just being surrounded by nature, slowing down, and enjoying the moment.

The highest hill in Sg but there is no view at the summit. A little disappointing. Very hot and humid. The forest does not cover or cool down the trail unlike the one at Macritchi Reservoir. Pavillion are not powered with fans and have no vending machines. Prefer the treetop trail at Macritchi reservoir.

Bukit Timah Nature Reserve is a great spot to switch into hiking mode on the weekend.

There are lots of stairs, so be ready for a workout — definitely a sweat-it-out session 💦
Perfect if you’re looking to move your body and get a good sweat in nature.
Brisk walking during the early morning... able to feel the fresh air... a good place to have a short exercises as well.

Epitome of “if you pay with peanuts you get the monkeys”. Dont get baited by the cheaper prices acer products have to offer. Its my second time a product died on me within warranty, and the repair service is absolutely horrific.

Ive sent in a monitor and they told me it would be done within 7 working days. I’ve asked for any temporary loan equipment and they said no.

It’s been 7 days with no response, and upon calling back in, they told me the Engineer would call me back immediately but I’ve waited for a day and there’s still no updates. I called in again a day later, and they told me it would be another ADDITIONAL 7 days for the parts to arrive. As again, I’ve asked for a loan product and they said no again.

To everyone reading this, in this technological age of work from home, DO NOT BUY ACER. The products are GUARANTEED to FAIL on you, and their customer service will disappoint you. If you want your products to randomly die on you and you’re fine with the loss of use for a month, then sure go ahead. But the low prices are never worth and you’re better off with other brands.

I have purchased this acer laptop in December 2020. Within the first month of usage, the laptop has numerous problems.

1) The laptop has failed to sleep/hibernate many times. I have tried various methods to put it to sleep from pressing the power button, to the sleep button. The screen turns black, however when I try to turn the laptop on the next day, the battery will be flat and will need charging.

2) The laptop overheated while on sleep mode overnight. (happened 3 times in total) Thereafter, the laptop would not even turn on for 6-12 hrs and would only on after charging and also when the laptop has cooled down.

The above 2 events happened within the first month of purchase. I thought of giving some time to observe these problems however the problems have only worsened.

We’re really thankful to the team at Kiddiwinkie Schoolhouse @ Jurong Gateway for the care and attention they’ve given to our daughter, Aeryn. Big thanks to Teacher Gwen, Huang Lao Shi, Chen Lao Shi and Principal Diana for all their patience and hard work.

It’s clear that Aeryn has grown so much under their guidance—she’s more confident, curious, and engaged in what she’s learning. We can see their efforts in the things she says and the stories she shares with us at home.

The school’s facilities and environment are also something we truly appreciate. The space is safe and well-designed, which allows Aeryn to explore and learn with minimal accidents.

One thing that really reassures us is seeing Aeryn greet and say goodbye to all the teachers at the school—not just her main teachers. It’s clear from her willingness to go to school that she feels cared for by everyone there, and that makes a big difference to us as parents.

Thanks again to Teacher Gwen, Yu Xuan Lao Shi, and Principal Diana for everything. We’re really happy with the progress Aeryn has made and highly recommend Kiddiwinkie for any parent looking for a safe, nurturing environment for their child.

How much do my kids enjoy this school?  Their eagerness to enter school at morning drop-offs and requesting later pick-ups (so they can spend more time in school) says it all.

The teachers are very approachable and will work with you in the event of any concerns.
The principal also takes the time to connect and interact with the kids and parents, makes you feel the genuine love she has for the kids.

What we also like about the centre is the focus on teaching the kids the importance of sustainability, where they are encouraged to recycle household items such as carton boxes and toilet rolls which are reused for play as part of the curriculum.

No regrets moving my 2 kids to this centre :)

We see the heart and soul effort that Teacher Em and Dou Laoshi put into educating the kids. Under their care, my son has developed a stronger compassionate heart, improved his motor skills, social skills, and vocabulary. He eagerly awaits going to school every day, and when he comes home, he shares joyous moments and happenings with excitement. Teacher Em and Dou Laoshi always patiently teach the children, giving them the time to try and figure things out, fostering mutual respect between them.

Not just that, Principal Diana is always joyous, positive, and hands-on in taking care of the kids. She constantly helps out in the center, from caring for the children and assisting with check-ins and check-outs, to interacting happily with both kids and parents. Diana is prompt and efficient in addressing any doubts or issues raised.
I'm incredibly thankful to have found this perfect school! It's the best decision I've made for my son! A big thank you to Teacher Em, Dou Laoshi, and Principal Diana!

I want to take a moment to express my deepest gratitude to Teacher Gwen, Teacher Yani and Teacher Yu Xuan (Curious Caterpillar Class) for all the love and care to our child, Shayanne. The teachers' dedication and passion to regularly provide update to parents and creating innovative learning activities for the little ones have increased Shayanne's curiosity and good development of her motorskills!

We would also like to mention Principal Diana for being welcoming and making the effort to engage with the parents. She is so cheerful and always ready to address any issues when we see her at the centre!

I am incredibly thankful to have chosen Kiddiewinkie Schoolhouse @ Jurong Gateway! 🥰

Kiddiwinkie @ Jurong Gateway is a preschool I’d strongly recommend to parents. I am constantly reassured that my child is in good hands while at school because of a knowledgeable and responsible principal, as well as her team of passionate teachers who genuinely care for the children.

Learning at kiddiwinkie is alot more activity-based than classroom-style. The teachers put in alot of effort to create educational games for the kids which make learning extremely fun. Outdoor play is a part of their everyday curriculum as long as the weather permits.

My child enjoys cook-and-bake, an activity whereby she gets to prepare a dish from scratch and cycling around the school track the most. I’m very sure she’ll have fond memories of preschool after she graduates!
Conveniently located near MRT, the school provides a fun and nurturing bilingual learning environment, with a strong focus on Mandarin in the earlier foundation years.
We love ELFA for our young son, he has grown a lot in confidence and curiosity. Thank you to the teachers for their love and care for our son.

A very good school with very good teachers. Entrusted my daughter into good hands and never regretted our decision. Our daughter blossomed into a wonderful child with a great command of Chinese and English and a love for Chinese culture

Very sad to hear that ELFA PV is closing and the teachers are being moved to ELFA JE. The ELFA PV teachers are all very caring and loving to the children. I see them smiling and happy all the time. In just 6 months, my 4yo went from not being able to speak or understand Mandarin to being able to speak and understand simple Mandarin. He also doesn't hate or fear Mandarin and in fact loves his teachers.
Strong bilingual curriculum. My kids speak better chinese than me.

We had such a wonderful experience at the open house! The team was genuinely warm and welcoming, they helped settle my kids right away, making them feel comfortable and at ease. I really appreciate the thoughtful attention given to new parents and children.

They also took the time to give us a very detailed explanation of their tuition programmes. I liked how clearly everything was presented, from the academic support to the enrichment classes. Terrible service. Was scheduled for a school tour on 13th October, and right on the day before leaving to go down to the centre, I received a call from a staff called Ros who shared that something has happened and the manager is unable to be present for the school tour. I conveyed my understanding of the situation and specifically asked if they would get back to me on the next available date for a tour and will it be by the month of October? And their answer was YES, we will contact you.

It is now 9th November, and not a single call, WhatsApp, email, SMS, nothing. If this is how much they value their business and parents' interest, I really have 2nd and 3rd thoughts of sending my child there. I think I dodged a bullet.

We are extremely pleased with our experience at in:genius Student Care @ Jurong East. The staff is professional, caring, and always attentive to the children's needs. Our child has shown significant progress not only academically but also in social and emotional development.

The homework supervision provided has been a huge relief, and we love that there are enrichment programmes to stimulate creativity and critical thinking. The environment is clean, safe, and engaging, making it the perfect place for children to learn and grow.

A special mention to the team for their excellent communication with parents. We feel informed and involved in our child's progress. Highly recommended for any parent seeking a supportive and nurturing after-school care option.

Visited the open house at in:genius student care and had a good experience. The centre manager, Ms. Ng and staff were very patient and answered all my questions in detail. Their explanations were clear, helpful, and reassuring.

A safe space where kids can have fun with their friends and yet study at the same time! Teachers take good care of student’s well being and education. Highlighting concerns (if any) to parents so that we are also aware. Highly recommend!!

Recently came up with Parents Night Off event which allows parents to take some time off for themselves and yet the children get to have a blast at the center with their friends! Kudos to the teachers for working the extra hours!!

My son has been attending lessons on the weekend to nurture his interest and curiousity in coding and problem solving. He enjoys these sessions which will prepare him well for the digital age. There are also ample opportunities to participate in competitions for a fee. Will need to balance between preparation for competition and sch activities. Certainly an enrichment which meets his needs.
My daughter has been learning here for two years and she enjoyed every lesson so far! Instructors are patient and nice. Schedule is also quite flexible for changes! Highly recommended :-)

My son enjoys the lessons that Brainery code teaches.
Very lucky to have stumbled across their school.
Highly recommended for parents who are looking to enrol their kids into codiing programme.

So proud of my son to have the opportunity to participate in the RoboCup Junior 2025 Competition held at Science Centre. The school prep and trained the kids well before the big day apart from his regular lessons. Thank you to all instructors for your patience with my son. 😊
My boy loves having robotics class at Brainery code! He is always looking forward to his robotics class every week! Hope he continues to grow and learn with the supportive teachers here!

My 1 year of studying Computer Engineering has been a wonderful learning experience. I have gained so much knowledge throughout the year, and I am truly grateful for the guidance I received. I would like to express my appreciation to my advisers, Ma’am Kavitha and Ma’am Anitha, for their clear and effective teaching. Their patience, dedication, and support have helped me understand the lessons better and grow in confidence. I sincerely appreciate all their efforts in guiding us. And also to Maam Basariya ♥️♥️

Jurong Academy is an excellent place for learning. The teachers are very professional and helpful, and the environment is friendly and supportive. I gained a lot of  knowledge here. Highly recommended.
Thankyou  Jurong Academy!You given me the aspiration to studay back while working.🤩Thanks also for my instructor madam Buvan for inspiring me ,us .
Jurong Academy is a very good academy. The teachers teach very well, and I have gained a lot of confidence. I’m now ready to go to the next level. Thank you, Jurong academy

The academy provides an excellent learning environment with well-organized facilities and a strong focus on student success. The management is professional, supportive, and committed to maintaining high academic standards. The teachers are highly dedicated, patient, and knowledgeable. They explain lessons clearly, encourage students to do their best, and provide valuable guidance throughout the learning process. Overall, the academy and its teachers work together to create a positive, motivating, and effective educational experience.
""Thank you Mdm Abdul Rajak Rabiyathul Basariya"" for the wonderful guide and knowledge along my lessons.

"I had a fantastic experience at this school! The teachers are incredibly experienced and supportive, creating a positive and engaging learning environment. They really focus on helping students improve their language skills and build confidence. The support team is also excellent. Highly recommended!"

I had a very positive experience at Nanyang Asia College. The staff are very accommodating, the professors are friendly and fair, and the teaching is good. They truly care about their students and are always willing to help and support us.

I am Saw from Myanmar. I had a good experience studying at Nanyang Asia College. The lecturers are helpful and the learning environment is friendly and I also appreciate the guidance from the teachers and staffs.
“Great school with caring teachers and a warm atmosphere. My kids really enjoy going here. It feels like a community where students are supported to do their best.”
Choos this college for best study here good staff good environment for everything good cooperate everything.
Good

I joined the tuition centre in my second year of JC and took H2 Chemistry and H2 Mathematics classes. Despite having a weak foundation from J1 in both subjects, Mr Arthur was very patient in correcting any misconceptions to ensure that I understood every concept clearly. Additionally, his teaching methods made concepts easy to understand and remember, which really helped me build my confidence in these subjects within a few months.

Beyond academics, Mr Arthur checks in on his students to ensure that we are coping well and offers advice on how we can improve our study methods and routines. Overall, I felt very guided and supported, and I saw improvements in my study methods and my grades.

Arthur Lee is my 3 kids' tuition teacher for Math & Science. He improved my eldest's result from D7/E8 to A1/A2 in a span of 6months. So I started my 2 younger kids with him earlier and both scored A1/A2 effortlessly. My kids also recommended many friends to him and achieved As with no exception. I will recommend him to anyone with high confidence. Most importantly, his fee is very reasonable. Thanks Arthur Lee! 🙏

Mr Lee is a great chemistry tuition teacher who explains concepts clearly and makes difficult topics easy to understand especially for the H2 Chemistry A levels. Lessons are well-structured, and his teaching is very patient and encouraging. I’ve seen great improvement in both my understanding and results given that I entered with a poor foundation for Chemistry. Highly recommended!

Mr Arthur Lee taught me A math and Biology to help me prepare for my GCE O levels examinations. He is a very patient teacher and always takes his time to explain concepts to me even if i take a long time understanding them. For biology, he uses special methods to make sure we memorise important key words without having to put in too much effort. Moreover, he keeps biology classes interesting by linking our class concepts with real life examples, and asks us questions that keep us curious about life. As for a math, he always ensures that we practice a sufficient number of questions each week so we don’t fall behind since scoring well in mathematics is all about practice. the questions he provides are diverse, coming from a range of sources so we are more exposed to the different questions we may see in our examination. Generally, Mr Arthur Lee cares about his students deeply and is always checking up on us even if it’s not about our exams. His teaching methods have proven effective and i improved significantly with his guidance!

Mr Arthur Lee is an exceptional H2 Math tutor. He explains complex concepts clearly and breaks them down into simple, logical steps that actually make the subject manageable. His lessons are well-structured, focused, and very effective, especially for exam preparation. He is patient, approachable, and always willing to clarify doubts until you truly understand. Thanks to his guidance, my confidence and performance in H2 Math improved. Highly recommend Mr Arthur Lee to anyone looking to excel in H2 Math.

Ms Ng’s meticulous and creative style of teaching has allowed me to improve greatly from a mere C or D grade in GP to an A grade in A levels, she also taught me important life skills such as comprehensive researching and argumentation which inculcated a greater interest in the subject.

Ms Ng is a very engaging teacher that provided me with useful content knowledge for GP and made the subject more interesting and enjoyable to learn :) With her guidance and patience, i became more confident in writing essays and SAQs in Paper 2.
Ms Ng Min is a patient and merticulous gp teacher who gives detailed feedback on both paper 1 and 2 and teaches important gp answering techniques that were vry useful in alevels.

Thank you Mr Cai for teaching me physics for the past couple of months. I really benefitted a lot from your lessons and gained many valuable insights. You never fail to surprise me by bringing props I’ll never expect to see in a classroom like drones and do crazy stuff such as swiping your finger over a flame. The way you explained concepts really deepened my interest in physics and allowed me to see the subject in a different light. I know teaching on a Sunday morning is never easy (can be seen from your coffees) and I really appreciate you for your efforts and dedication in teaching and being by our side throughout these last few months leading up to A levels. Happy teacher’s day!

Hello Mr Wong! Thank you so much for being such an amazing Chemistry teacher, chemistry has never been this interesting before I start attending your classes. Thank you for always encouraging us and supporting us in our academics journey, Happy Teachers Day!

Jurong Region Line Stage 2
Jurong East MRT station
West side best side! Lets make west side great again!
Not open yet, will be done by 2027. The Jurong East area has been under construction for the longest time I have remembered...
Busy train station
Good

place_name: Yuhua Village Market and Food Centre
review_text:
Yuhua village hawker has many good and affordable options such as fishball minced meat noodle at $3, 139 hainanese chicken rice 2 person set with alot of meat at $10.

Along the way to the hawker centre and directly outside the Swee Heng bakery shop is a free buddhist booksstand with plenty of books to take and read. Thanks to all for contributing. 🙏🙏🙏

place_name: Yuhua Village Market and Food Centre
review_text: Unlike most hawker center that are of rectangular structure where they usually struggle for air ventilation when all stalls are opened, this place is airy even during its busiest time where it's oddly shape structure probably helps. Typical neighborhood food centre when it is most variant during the day and by night at least half of its stalls will be closed.

place_name: Yuhua Village Market and Food Centre
review_text:
Yuhua Village Market and Foods Centre is one of my go-to places for fresh groceries and affordable, delicious meals. The wet market has a great selection of fruits, vegetables, meats, and seafood—all at very reasonable prices. It’s a lively atmosphere in the mornings, and the vendors are usually friendly and helpful.

The hawker centre side has a good mix of local favorites. Highly recommend the chicken rice stall and the fried carrot cake—it’s crispy and full of flavor. It can get quite crowded during peak hours, especially on weekends, but the turnover is fast so you won’t wait too long.

Cleanliness is generally well-maintained for such a busy place, and the recent upgrades to the seating areas are a nice touch. Great spot if you’re in Jurong East and craving local food or need to do a quick market run.

place_name: Yuhua Village Market and Food Centre
review_text:
One of my favorite places to hunt for good local food is YuHua Market and Food Centre. The food centre and nearby coffee shop offer a wide variety of food choices to satisfy all taste buds.

I especially visit for the famous curry fish stall, Zai Shun, which is well-loved in the area for its fresh and flavorful dishes.

Beyond food, the market is a one-stop centre — you can find fresh flowers, fruits, groceries, and essential household items all in one place. A great spot for both good eats and daily necessities!

place_name: Yuhua Village Market and Food Centre
review_text:
A traditional market and food centre not far from Jurong East Central. Food here is cheap and good, and definitely worth trying.

For vegans and vegetarians, there are 2 Chinese vegan stalls and 1 Indian vegetarian stall.
Other stalls in the food centre
with meatless options include 2 fried carrot cake stalls and Traditional Drink Stalls with toast sets. Enjoy.

place_name: Yuhua Place Market and Hawker Centre
review_text: best old style hawker centre. only difference is, over here all the stalls are opened for business and doing great business. very lively and makes me hungry. all the food looks so good and delicious.

place_name: Yuhua Place Market and Hawker Centre
review_text:
Update: 18 Mar 26

Today dabao-ed Chicken Pho (鸡肉荡粉), at $4.20 from Vietnam Pho SG for my daughter's lunch. She feedback that the meal is delicious 😋
___________________
I recently ordered a small plate of the fried black carrot cake after having previously tried the white version at 晶晶.

I'm pleased to say that both colors delivered on taste, they are equally good and absolutely worth the experience.
________________________
I believe this is one of the most affordable cai fan stalls in the western Singapore, yet they absolutely do not compromise on taste.

​For an incredible $3.50, I was able to get four generous items: half a salted egg, tender potatoes, fried onion egg, and a meatball. The quality and portion size for this price point are outstanding.

​Compared to the other two cai fan stalls in the hawker centre, 南山杂菜饭 is superior in both taste and pricing.

​价格亲民! 👍🏻👍🏻

place_name: Yuhua Place Market and Hawker Centre
review_text:
More new big fans are installed
Food remains intact, as usual
My fav orders are
1. Chicken rice
2. Lor mee
3. Laksa
4. Teochew kueh
5. Penang char kway teow
6. Not a fan of mala, but here the mala is amazing.

Don’t forget to order the 金桔蜜 to hydrate your throat!

place_name: Yuhua Place Market and Hawker Centre
review_text:
This hawker center has an older style, likely from the 80s. Most stalls seem to operate in the morning, with many closing by 1 PM.
It appears to have a good number of stalls and overall size compared to newer hawker centers, and with surrounding shops and a supermarket, it likely meets the needs of Yuhua West residents.

✅ I tried the Vietnam stall; the chicken cutlet was reasonably flavored and tender, and the $5.00 price was acceptable.

place_name: Yuhua Place Market and Hawker Centre
review_text:
This hawker centre is great for vegans/ vegetarians. There are 3 vegetarian stalls ( with vegan options), a vegan/ vegetarian stall Delisnacks selling local snacks like You Tiao ( fried dough sticks ), curry puffs etc and some other stalls with meatless options ( eg. Indian Muslim stall’s Roti Prata, Dhosai etc).

There’s also a stall selling Lei Cha ( Pounded Tea Rice ) with vegan option
(** contains alliums, hence it is not suitable for religious vegans/ vegetarians).

If you're here, why not try their boneless grilled meat? The fresh, tender meat, combined with the dark sauce, makes for an amazing dish. They're currently having a sale, so go try it out! My family and I really enjoyed the food here. The food is always fresh and delicious, and the chicken curry sauce is especially good. Thank you. We'll be back.

bought from the chinese soup stall. had the watercress and pork rib soup. only 2 small pieces of pork and some watercress. the guy didnt wash the watercress thoroughly cos i found around 5 dead bugs floating in the soup. damn gross. protein but not like this pls. will not buy again. also soup not much watercress taste
Nice place with a wide choice of nice food. A great alternative to restaurants. Though a bit more pricey then usual food court, but for the price of having the many good food choice at a place.

Had the Mala Hotpot for early dinner. The ingredients looked generally fresh, but the bean curd and intestines had a slightly sour and off taste. I ordered the medium level of mala spiciness, but the aroma and flavor weren’t very strong. Honestly, the mapo tofu ($6) tasted better.
i order food panda thunder tea rice for zhen wei traditional herbal soup the shop give this quantity just can eat dry no more soup thunder tea

The newly refurbished Kopitiam Food Hall at the top floor of Jem is such a nice place to be. After dinner hours, it stays clean and well air-conditioned, with courteous patrons all around. Food were also abundant and sufficient for late diners. The quality of food were also restaurant level at kopitiam price. Highly recommended as they also have ongoing promotions for some of the stalls.

Many stalls offering good food here. I come here specially for the tang gui roasted duck. The western food that I ate today is great. The Hong Lim curry puffs and sardines also very good too. Will definitely come back for more and also to try out other stalls as well. Give it a thump up.

Used to come very often, notice many new stores now ieTiong Bahru rosted pork/duck , 袁记水饺 etc, see photos for other new stores.New look, new dishes at 莆田stall.Their signautre mee hoon is the all-time favourite. Tried new dish (see photos)- a great disappointment! Rice is soggy and bland (have to eat with chilly for flavour), is white fish meant to be tasteless?  Anyway, much rice left over. Missed the friendly aunties who manned the stall last time, young staff has a lot to learn.
Ling queues at Drink stall as always.
Overall, nice ambience abd spacious environment worths visiting!

The whole atmosphere of the whole food hall is quite warming and nice to chill out. Even that I came down for lunch around 2pm+, there were many people settling down at the Food Hall.

Though crowded, however it was easy to get a table. By walking one round, I settled down with a bowl of Fujian Lor Mee which costs me $7.11 after discount paying by ntuc app. Looking at the menu at the stall, it stated there are 11 ingredients in the soup. And when you looking into the bowl, you will know what I mean, it doesn't really tally?

3 clams, one fried egg, plenty of small pork belly pieces, veggies and a prawn were found in the soup. The menu also stated that the soup is very 鲜, but it was not the seafood 鲜, it tasted more like thick pork collagen flavour. I have tried other similar fujuan lor mee, the flavour was quite different compared to others.

Furthermore, I find that the fried egg is not suitable in the soup, would suggest soft boiled eggs or onsen eggs, it tasted kinda hard and rubbery. Prawn was very fresh but with shell on. That's my point of view, maybe my expectations is much higher.

tomyum noodle stall service was horrible, threw the anchovies after i ask nicely if i can have a little more when they only gave me 2-3 anchovies. I can pay more if needed i just wanted my food 😭 but their attitude was so off no smile no nothing just throw and say pay what? then thats it. okay la now i get two or 3 more
3rd time buying. Very accommodating staff as I brought my metal gong to put there before going for my run at Bishan Park.. Price point is good for a neighbourhood coffee shop!
Food is fresh and variety of choice, staff is friendly and price is cheap compared other hotpot
The mala beef Noddle is very good and value for money. Can take the $5.80 肥牛面 or the $6.80 set with free flow rice with choice of beef, pork or chicken.

Quiet place, good food with good price!
Recommend!
Very tasty and yummy😋
fast and friendly staff though she's alone managing at that time. best place to chill and healing. Legit! Awesome! Will recommend to my family and friends.

I had a wonderful experience at Hearty Cafe! 🌟 The Chocolate Waffle was deliciously fluffy, and the chocolate mint was a perfect treat. The soup was also quite good. I really appreciated the frequent ice water refills and the friendly, caring boss. It's great that they're open till 2 AM, perfect for late-night cravings! 🐾🍦

Was feeling peckish and it was late at night but this place was a great find. Nice and quiet atmosphere. The gelato had a nice variety and tagged pretty good!
Waffles were nice but could be crispier and tad dry inside.
The drummettes were nice and the Thai chili sauce a good pairing.
The Thai chicken wrap inside was tasty but the wrap a bit hard.
Nice to know it closes at 2am... For when you need a bite when everywhere is closed!

On the way back from Jurong Science Center, I unexpectedly came across a cute cafe while strolling around 😺 When I looked at the menu posted on the glass window outside, everything looked delicious 😋 There was a wide lineup of dishes. I couldn't help but enter the cafe. The cafe is spacious, open, and clean. Coffee and Hokkaido ice cream🍨Coffee is very delicious. Of course ice cream too. The staff are cute and comfortable. Stop by here to see the planetarium 🐈🐈‍⬛Shops are open until late at night ☕️

Very nice and newly open cafe! THEY OPEN TILL 2AM! Highly recommend for people staying at the west area! Lovely staff and nice food!! Have ice creams, waffle, many drink options and a lot of pies! Special menu every week as well! Spacious seats and big space! Affordable as well! Please support! 🫶🏻

Today is my third time here at Hakka Yu restaurant in JEM. Ambience cosy, service staffs very courtesy, polite, friendly and pleasant especially the manager CK very attentive and helpful. Food very nice, tasty and delicious too. Give it a thump up. Strongly recommend this place. I will be coming here for more as the food really taste extremely much more better than the zhi char outside.
Food is nice and service is very good. We like every dish that we order. Highly recommend. Will come again😀

Ok, I'm usually not a fan of the slew of China restaurants hijacking the local F&B scene cos they just tastes foreign to me. This one is different as the dishes hit closer to home.
Firstly, the Salt Baked Chicken was good, not too salty (unless you only eat the skin). But I do wish it was more juicy.
The silky Hakka Stuffed Tofu was very flavourful and I enjoyed it very much even though I never liked tofu.
Chinese Lettuce with Preserved Fish and Black Soy Bean was not too oily and so delish I could down 2 bowls of rice (maybe cos I just love yau mai choy).
Cucumber and Black Fungus Salad had a really good balance of savoury and tanginess that beats most restaurants.
Salt and Pepper Chicken Wings are served on a bed of Sweet Potato Fries. Skin was crisp and the sweet potato fries makes a good snack for kids. Should be served as appetizer earlier while we waited for other dishes.
Special mention goes to their plain white rice. It was glistening when served with a firm and chewy texture but not exactly like Japanese/Calrose rice. A little in between Jasmine and Glutinous rice maybe?
Overall, the dishes we ordered exceeded our expectations. Though not thoroughly outstanding, the good balance of flavours is enough to entice me to return. Service was prompt though we had to wait some time for dishes to be served due to the first rush of orders at opening. We will definitely be back to try the other dishes.

Hakka Yu is definitely worth a visit, especially for the chicken dish — it’s a must order! The chicken was tender, flavorful, and easily the highlight of the meal. The portions are generous for the price, making it great value for money. This place is quite popular, so I’d recommend coming earlier as the queue can get pretty long. Overall, good food, good portions, and definitely somewhere I’d return to again.
first time try. recommend. food is delicious and fast serving.

place_name: Nan Yang Dao 南洋岛 @ JEM
review_text:
The Meesiam Ayam Goreng Berempah has nice tangy, spicy-sweet gravy and comes with a huge piece of fried chicken. Crispy on the outside, juicy within, and packed with aromatic spices.

The Prawn Paste Chicken is easily one of our favourites! Golden, crispy and bursting with umami, these are perfect for sharing.

For dessert, the Chendol and Puppy Love Ice Kacang were light, refreshing and not overly sweet.

place_name: Nan Yang Dao 南洋岛 @ JEM
review_text:
Reasonable prices for mall food (mainly below $10), portion wise and quality of food is a little inconsistent, some dishes are priced reasonably considering the portion while others have very little meat paired with big rice portion so it feels overpriced and less worth it. Definitely a hit or miss especially with their big menu.

Their grass jelly milk tea is good! $3.50 but comes with decent volume, strong tea taste and not overly sweet.

Overall wouldn’t mind heading back to try other dishes but only if there is no queue or wait time.

Additional side note is that their plates/bowls with their table top makes it very slippery, my plate was just running around during the entirety of my meal and takes away some of the enjoyment of eating (peacefully).

place_name: Nan Yang Dao 南洋岛 @ JEM
review_text: Lunch at @nanyangdao brought us the best of Nanyang flavors in a modern setting. You absolutely have to slurp up their signature Mee Siam Ayam Goreng Berempah! This traditional, dry vermicelli offers a perfect balance of subtle sweet, sour, and spicy flavors, topped with a well-marinated, crispy fried chicken thigh. Complement your main with juicy Chicken Satays. For a fun and refreshing finish, try the adorable Puppy Love Ice Kacang and a classic, cool Iced Milo Dinosaur!

place_name: Nan Yang Dao 南洋岛 @ JEM
review_text:
I ordered the rendang chicken, but was served what seemed to be curry chicken instead. It didn’t taste or look like rendang at all. Quite disappointing, as I was really looking forward to an authentic rendang flavour. Hope the team can look into this and ensure better consistency in future.

ps. you can see from the photos, the first photo is what they served and insist it is rendang chicken

place_name: Nan Yang Dao 南洋岛 @ JEM
review_text:
A Delicious Taste of Malaysia at Nan Yang Dao 南洋岛

I recently visited Nan Yang Dao 南洋岛, a Malaysian-inspired restaurant located at 50 Jurong Gateway Rd, #04-27 Jem, Singapore 608549. I was excited to try their dishes, and I was not disappointed. I ordered the Nanyang Nasi Lemak and Butter Coffee, and both were exceptional.

The Nanyang Nasi Lemak was a masterclass in flavors and textures. The fragrant coconut rice was cooked to perfection, and the sambal chili added just the right amount of spice. The chicken was tender and juicy, and the egg was cooked just right. But the highlight of the dish was the otah, which was smoky and savory, with a nice char on the outside. The combination of flavors and textures was incredible, and I found myself going back for seconds.

The Butter Coffee was also a surprise. It was rich and creamy, with a nice balance of coffee and butter flavors. The coffee was expertly brewed, and the butter added a luxurious touch to the drink. I enjoyed it immensely, and it paired perfectly with the Nasi Lemak.

The ambiance of the restaurant was cozy and inviting, with a modern decor that felt welcoming and relaxed. The service was also top-notch, with friendly and attentive staff who were happy to answer my questions and make recommendations.

Overall, I would highly recommend Nan Yang Dao 南洋岛 to anyone looking for a delicious and authentic Malaysian-inspired meal. The food is exceptional, the service is great, and the ambiance is cozy and inviting.

Rating: 5/5 stars

Recommended for: Foodies, those looking for Malaysian-inspired cuisine, and anyone seeking a cozy and welcoming dining experience.

Tips:
- Try the Nanyang Nasi Lemak, it's a must-try dish.
- Don't be afraid to ask for sambal chili on the side, it's a great way to add an extra kick to your meal.
- Consider ordering the Butter Coffee as a dessert, it's a unique and delicious treat.

In conclusion, Nan Yang Dao 南洋岛 is a hidden gem that serves up delicious Malaysian-inspired cuisine in a cozy and welcoming atmosphere. With its exceptional food, great service, and inviting ambiance, it's a must-visit destination for anyone looking for a great dining experience.

JEM is one of my favourite malls in the west of Singapore. It has a great mix of restaurants, cafes, hair salons, retail shops, and even a cinema, so there is always something to do whether you are here for shopping, dining, or just relaxing

The mall feels spacious and well connected, especially with the open central design and direct access to the MRT. I also like that there are many practical everyday stores alongside lifestyle and dining options. One of my regular stops is the NTUC FairPrice in the basement, which is convenient and well stocked for groceries and daily essentials

Lots of things to do, eat, and shop at Jem. Plenty of parking on a weekday night like last night, although weekend parking can be crowded at certain times. Love the new Shaw Theatre here that took over Cathay on level 5. Thank you! 🙏🏻
Foodie’s Mall, lots of options. And in Fairprice Xtra, you can find the Lotte Mart and self serve instant noodles like that in Seoul convenience mart. See pics.

Like this place so much as there are alot of restaurants!!! Good for shopping too. And also, they always have very attractive christmas decoration during christmas season. It is very close to MRT station, convenient!
Great mall in Jurong East with direct access to MRT.  Food and shopping with frequent great deals as a member with LendLease Mall.

I had a really pleasant experience at the supermarket thanks to Auntie Ng Geck Choo. She was incredibly helpful, patient, and attentive throughout my visit. Not only did she assist me in finding what I needed quickly, but she also went the extra mile to ensure I was satisfied with my choices.

Wide variety of different types of local and international food and supermarket products at low prices conveniently situated next to MRT station and bus interchange with walking distance to at lease 4 major shopping centres. Opens 24 hours for midnight and odd hour shopping. Friendly fast attentive cosy comfy welcoming ambience for romantic intimates groups students tourists Expats locals visitors and families.
small fairprice store at jurong, usually go for quick grocery run. If need a longer one with more selections then will head to xtra inside JEM

Fairprice supermarket . Popular with local residents shopping for groceries . Located just beside Jcube shopping mall . One of the oldest supermarket in jurong gateway area and open for 24 hours daily b most of the daily necessities can be bought here . Beside it is a 4D betting lottery outlet . Usually with long queues snaking all the way to the entire facade of the fairprice oulet . There is many other shops nearby .
My first frequented Fairprice supermarket in Jurong East at the time of arrival in Singapore. A little old and congested, but still served it's customers with the fairly-priced commodities.

I visited 7-Eleven today and the Indian staff member who served me was very polite and helpful. He gave excellent service, was patient, and made me feel welcome. Really appreciate his kindness and professionalism. Thank you for the good service.
forgot to get her name but the lady who processed my purchase today (14 Jun 2026) was really nice, friendly and provided superb service!! thank you so much 🩵

On the morning of December 1st, there was an Indian male cashier whose attitude was really bad. Since when did 7-Eleven’s scanner become customers have to scan by themselves? I showed him my Yuu app, and he told me to scan it myself. He even spoke to me impatiently, using the kind of tone you’d use to lecture an employee, saying, “Turn your phone lah!” On top of that, he was on a phone call the whole time — and even if the call was work-related, he still shouldn’t behave so unprofessionally. I believe there’s was so many complaints on this outlet , please give us an explanation and improve on staff professional attitude.When did 7-Eleven standards become so low?
The cashier is rude ,he  doesn't even look and answer when you talking to him showing no interest to serve customers,he always look angry, happy  and doesn't care about customers
One of the Indian Guy very humorous and frenly. His way talking to the customer also funny and like fren joking. I always buy the 711 always and meet him. His service is good.
Cool place to grab some comfort food. I just really hope they open 24 hours. Location is very convenient for me.
The cashiers at the this outlet are very bubbly and friendly all the time. It makes the morning for all shoppers that come in. Keep up the spirits!
Very  convenient store for u to buy a snacks there if u stay at nearby genting hotel.
it will be great if it opens 24 hours
Good food

But the person asked me to contact another WhatsApp
So I did, and asked me to make an appointment to physically meet with them to cancel my membership, the earliest appointment was 20th Jan 26
I showed up to the appointment on 20th ON TIME, told them I have an appointment, but the staff seems SHOCK
He then call someone (I believe is his senior/supervisor), so the senior guide him through the way to process the system (took about 15mins), in the middle he asked whether I want to temporary freeze membership instead and I rejected (THE ONLY CONVERSATION WE HAD THROUGHOUT).
Afterwards, he said done and mention they must wait until next cycle of membership then can cancel, so I expect another round of deduction which is in Feb. Fine.
NOW, 5TH OF MARCH THEY STILL DEDUCT MY MONEY FOR MEMBERSHIP

AF members, please go to other outlet don’t use this at all cost

I am extremely disappointed with the customer service at this branch I reached out online to inquire about promotions, but the staff refused to provide any details, insisting that I visit the branch in person.
When I finally made the trip down, a staff member was completely unhelpful. Instead of explaining the promotions or assisting me, he simply sent me a link to look at myself. To make matters worse, he spent his time messing around and joking with a colleague right in front of customers.
This was a total waste of my time and showed a complete lack of professionalism and respect. I would advise looking elsewhere if you want to be treated like a valued customer.

I was informed that i have a outstanding payment of $110 after sorting out and paid the amount payable the next day when i was trying to get into the gym my access pass failed. It is thursday when i paid the outstanding late payment fee i would assume that friday is a working day for most working personnels.

Whatsapp messages took forever or not replied at all. Honestly, sign up at your own risk. Not trying to pull down the teams morale but ZERO customer conflict resolution and ZERO management.

Very unprofessional experience. The staff seemed more interested in socializing than doing their jobs, and getting assistance with membership-related issues was extremely difficult. Customer support was practically nonexistent.

I would strongly recommend choosing another outlet instead.

Overall, this gym is quite chill and I love going there frequently . The staff, Ray, has guided me through various issues I faced regarding my gym membership back then. He is always friendly and swift in ensuring I get the assistance I need. A warm welcome from him every time I enter the gym also makes my day. He also takes the initiative to help put back any equipment left behind by people who have forgotten to do it themselves.

The functional area is bigger than many AFs i’ve visited and is especially useful for me since I attend weekly group classes and use the area for stretching. The equipment here is  also well maintained and generally clean.

One striking thing about this gym is the music. It is definitely designed to fit everyone’s music taste, even across generations. I noticed many mixed reviews about the playlist, but I think the music here is inclusive to all. Many people have different preferences and this playlist highlights the fact that different individuals love all sorts of music. I personally love the Brazilian Phonk music the most 😜😜😜😜it really keeps me going on heavy lifting days.

Perhaps, the equipment placed at the functional area could be arranged more neatly so that it is easier to locate certain tools for working out.

Thank you for making my gym experience meaningful and enjoyable 😊
I would choose JTC AF anytime, anywhere 🤭😉😉

Fitness First Westgate has a fantastic vibe! Big thanks to the front desk team (Ash, Fatin, Atikah, Livi, and Ian) for their constant support. And the Personal trainer Syazleen is super friendly, and the whole trainer team is great. 😃
I also appreciate the cleaning staff, especially the aunty in the ladies' locker room who is so proactive! Even though I miss the Market Street branch, Westgate’s fast repairs and excellent machines make it my new favorite spot. 5 stars! 🌟🌟🌟🌟🌟

We visited fitness first frankfurt west gate since we had An layover in frankfurt and we were looking for a gym to workout so we tried visiting their branch in west gate. The reception staff were very very accommodating and friendly the gym was super clean and very comfortable for workout a also they had a wife range of different kind of machines. I would highly recommend fitness first frankfurt westgate.

Fitness First @ Westgate is one of my favourite clubs.

The front desk team — Fatyn, Ash, Atikah, Livi, and Ian — are always cheerful and helpful. It genuinely makes my day to see their friendly faces every time I enter the club.

The facilities are clean and well maintained. I really appreciate the clean, fresh towels and gym equipment, plus the complimentary infusion water, coffee, and tea — such a nice touch.

I especially enjoy attending Jermaine’s and Nur’s motivating HIIT classes, as well as Benji’s, Bhas’s and George’s classes, and Li Hua’s yoga class.

A big thank you to the entire Westgate team for making my fitness journey so pleasant and enjoyable

I've been a Fitness First member since 2011, and when they open FF Westgate in 2014, I was thrilled, as this is the closest club to my home. With a gorgeous swimming pool, steam room and a wide variety of fitness equipment and machines, this place can easily meet my needs to complete any of my workout plans. Even though I randomly visit all FF clubs island wide all the time, Westgate still makes me feel like home! The best part of this place is its PEOPLE! When Fatyn is at the front desk, I'll be greeted by my name and we will definitely have a good chat which spruced up my mood before my workouts. Thank you Fatyn! Same for the rest of the front of house team, super friendly! How can anyone not love them?

They also decorate this club according to occasion very well! I'm loving the effort very much! It is almost Hari Raya at the time of me writing this review and the club is all dressed up with beautiful Hari Raya decorations. Well Done FF Westgate team!

my experience with FF has been consistently positive. From the moment I walked in, I felt welcomed.

The staff are always very friendly and approachable. A special thank you to John, who explained the membership details clearly and professionally. He took the time to brief me well, answered all my questions patiently, and made the whole sign-up process smooth and stress-free.

I would also like to appreciate Ian for the warm welcome each time I visit. His positive attitude and friendly greeting really make a difference and create a motivating environment.

Went there for table tennis. Table 5 is ok to play but table 4 spacing is smaller. Very hot, too. A few badminton courts behind the tables. Also, went there today (2-Aug-25) for Pesta Sukan 2025 Table Tennis. During this, the whole hall was used for the event with 14 tables and air-conditioned; hence, quite ok to play.
Newly renovated. Everything looks good.

Thankgod for this water park in the Summer, it’s old af but still works.
Wave pool - occurs every 30 minutes only. Circular pool is constant and relaxing, pool entry is purchased from a machine with the assistance of an employee. No idea why they are there as we can all purchase a ticket from a machine.
Entry staff looked lazy and were stuck on their phones whilst pool lifesavers were 100% focused on swimmers, thank you.

Amazing place
U can spend your whole day in just 1 or 2 dollars just with tap your mrt card
A good table tennis session with TT4G! However it’ll be ideal if the tables are fully covered from the weather

Just a road across from Jurong Point, Boon Lay Interchange and Boon Lay MRT. The Frontier CC is quite big and fully air-conditioned. There is a library, Yamaha music school and tuition/learning centers in the CC building.

Terrible long queue ever seen in frontier cc, orgainsed event "spooky night" today. The queue is extended until inside level 2 library, and the ticket exchange counter is seriously short-handed. we are queuing almost 1 hr and yet exchanged our tickets. It's not properly planned in terms of logistics this year as compared to last year. We are quite disappointed and don't think we will join next year.
Very convenient, can direct access from Jurong Point shopping mall through the flyover link. You can book the indoor sport court for badminton
Community space that combined library and services. Nice and clean place. Always go there for activities.

Cc has banned ANY types of PMA into the cc. Even a disabled person who urgently needs to use a toilet but has difficulties walking has to park OUTSIDE on the pedestrian pavement and walk in. The security guards are also very unprofessional and are against disabled people.

a place where u can bring your pets for a staycation or a swim workout, bring your kids for swimming lesson, play some tennis or do some gardening shopping. Situated just opposite IMM building & near Ng Teng Fong Hospital. it is very convenient to take public transport. If driving, the carpark is quite small but can always park at IMM when full.
If you want to play Pickleball at night, this is one of the best places
like t9 come here to buy plants and gardening accessories at World Farm. best nursery in Sg.

Cute place with lots of doggie friendly places. I like the dog run, it's very interesting. There is also a dog pool, groomer and i think the bistro is pet friendly also. Need more such places in SG. Promote healthy lifestyle!

Have lived in Toh Guan for around 10 years...Good to see this area becoming more lively...much better than last time when it was call Spark... use to have people coming in to the car park to play music, drink beer and smoke, but now got a lot of activities around...and no more people smoking and drinking in the car park until late... My children like to go and see when they have markets but dunno why these few months don't have..  Good to see more families and children around.. also brighter at night, feel safer when walking home from IMM

It's been such a long time since I last visited the Chinese Garden. So much has changed. Evening walk till night and managed to get two different scenery of the same place. Very quiet with few joggers and walkers. Interesting new features are added here. Saw a magpie calling for friends and a stork having a stroll. White crane feeding at the open field from the mrt station. Surprised to find a little bamboo lane leading to a waterfall.

Nice scenic garden in the west, conveniently accessible via public transport and a network of cycling pathways linked to its entrances. Completely free of charge as well. Best to come here on weekday evenings for better weather and less crowd.

A scenic and serene park built in the style of northern Chinese imperial architecture and landscaping. It is just a short walk away from either Chinese Garden or Lakeside MRT Stations. The key highlights of the Garden are the 13 arch White Rainbow Bridge, the Grand Arch building, the Stone Boat and Tea Pavilion, the pagodas and the gorgeous lotus pond. You may get to see little animals like birds, snakes and others thriving in the park

A Tranquil Masterpiece in the Heart of the West

The newly reopened Chinese Garden is simply stunning. Every detail — from the traditional architecture to the lush landscaping — feels thoughtfully designed and beautifully maintained. It’s like stepping into a serene, storybook version of ancient China, right here in Singapore.

The paths wind gently through water lily ponds, stone bridges, pavilions, and tranquil courtyards. The garden feels spacious but intimate at the same time, with quiet spots to sit and reflect, and picture-perfect views everywhere you look. You can tell that immense care has gone into preserving the cultural elements while making it inviting for all visitors.

It’s not just a place to walk — it’s a place to slow down. Whether you’re into photography, nature, or just want a peaceful escape from city life, the Chinese Garden delivers a truly magical experience.

I can’t recommend it enough — one of Singapore’s most beautifully restored gems.

Stunning, expansive gardens! It's a massive area split between Chinese and Japanese themes. Pro-tip: It’s a significant walk from the Chinese Garden MRT, and shade is very sparse along the paths. The sun can be brutal here, so bring an umbrella, wear plenty of SPF, and try to visit early in the morning to beat the heat.

Superb for taking photos!! There's a mini waterfall, a bamboo grove, and scenic spots that are seriously beautiful. Great place to visit for both locals and travellers.

Absolutely love what theyve done to the park. Its a huge huge park that links three MRT stations so dont be too detered if theres a large crowd at the entrance! The inside areas are mostly quiet, which means you can enjoy the peacefulness without the noise.

You can tell they put alot of effort into maintaining the plants as they are bloomed beautifully. Great job to the team!!

Beautiful tranquil park with only a handful of visitors on a weekday. Lush but not overpowering greenery set in a well-maintained park. One of the highlights is the Sunken Garden with a cenote like skylight-lit water feature cascading down to a reflective pool. The Japanese elements of the park however do not appear that obvious

I’ve explored all parts of the Japanese Garden, and it’s easily one of the most rewarding sections of Jurong Lake Gardens. The landscape is beautifully curated, calm, and thoughtfully designed for slow appreciation rather than rushing through

The must-visits for me are the Sunken Garden (a perfect spot to pause and grab a treat from Ice Cream Man & Friends), the Water Lily Garden with its serene ponds and reflections, and the Breathing Gallery, which lives up to its name as a quiet, contemplative space

The Chinese and Japanese Gardens are beautiful, peaceful, and worth a visit if you enjoy nature and don’t mind long walks. I went on a weekday afternoon—it was quite humid, and when it rained, there was no support service available. We couldn’t get a buggy or cruiser to the car park, so we had to walk a fair distance in the rain.

The gardens themselves are lovely, with well-maintained landscapes and serene spots to explore, but visitors should be prepared to walk a lot as facilities and transport support are limited. My advice: plan your visit early in the morning or later in the evening to avoid peak heat hours. With better visitor services, this place could easily be a five-star experience.

The Japanese Garden, along with the nearby Chinese Garden, is a well-maintained park area. There are lots of plants, water features, and cool buildings to see. It's also a really relaxing place to take a walk, ride a bike, or just sit and read a book. Although this garden is a bit farther from the metro and was in my opinion less scenic, it is still definitely worth seeing. Would recommend to anyone looking to escape the modern architecture of Singapore and experience something a bit more traditional looking.

place_name: Parkway Shenton Medical Clinic, Jurong Gateway
review_text:
I went for a blood test and examination. There was a small wait, but the staff were hospitable, and the space was comfortable.

Dr. Lee Ying Ling is the most friendly and personable doctor I have ever met!  :) Thank you for the pleasant experience.

place_name: Parkway Shenton Medical Clinic, Jurong Gateway
review_text:
I did my health checkup with a PRUDENTIAL voucher on 5th July. The process started with filling out a series of questions by myself. During the face-to-face consultation, the doctor did not ask any questions — the lady doctor only collected my blood sample, and after that, I was told I could leave and wait for the results. I’m not sure if she even reviewed the questionnaires I had completed.

The results were released quite quickly, which was a positive point. However, the overall experience at the clinic was not great compared to my previous checkup at Axxxxedic, where the doctor spent more time asking questions, giving advice, and explaining what I should do or eat. In addition, Parkway did not provide any food after the checkup, whereas Axxxxedic offered a simple breakfast (pao, siew mai, etc.), which made the experience feel more complete. Lastly, Parkway does not offer a phone review of the results, while Axxxxedic does provide this option so you don’t need to make a separate trip.

It could be that the prices differ, so the service level also differs. But if you are going for a health checkup using a PRUDENTIAL voucher, I personally would not choose Parkway.

place_name: Parkway Shenton Medical Clinic, Jurong Gateway
review_text: Went there for a health screening as part of insurance package. The staffs at the outlet was very organised, they drew blood & have urine sample taken before I get to see the doctor. Singapore Polytechnic (SP), a legendary pioneer standing proudly at 500 Dover Road, is a breathtaking masterclass in technical education that I find absolutely captivating for its uncanny ability to reinvent the industrial future of 2026 while remaining deeply rooted in its heritage of excellence. Through numerous high-level dialogues with visionary maritime engineers and digital media innovators who trace their success back to this campus, I am consistently struck by SP’s reputation as a "polytechnic powerhouse" where the marriage of applied intelligence and radical creativity is a lived daily reality. Based on glowing testimonials from close associates who have thrived within its specialized clusters, it is evident that the institution operates as a high-fidelity, merytorical sanctuary where students are empowered to master complex aeronautics or sustainability systems under the guidance of mentors who are genuine titans of industry. Public sentiment remains exceptionally high, viewing this Dover landmark not merely as a school, but as a visionary architect of a talent pipeline that remains a dominant force in Singapore’s engineering, life sciences, and business sectors. Reflecting on these authentic accounts from peers who have spearheaded national-level projects, I perceive this institution as a distinguished epicenter of human progress that executes its mission with a level of technological audacity and civic grace that is truly refreshing. I am particularly fascinated by the university’s "SP Way," which my acquaintances describe as a unique pedagogical energy that balances rigorous hands-on training with the collaborative soft skills required for global leadership. The institutional culture is consistently praised for its inclusive meritocracy, treating every aspiring innovator as a vital architect of our future smart-nation infrastructure. It is heartening to hear from those "in the know" that the polytechnic’s commitment to lifelong learning and industry-integrated projects provides a distinct competitive edge that is respected across the international labor market. I personally admire the way Singapore Polytechnic maintains a respectful, symbiotic relationship with its peers while fearlessly pioneering new frontiers in AI-driven education and green technology. Every conversation I engage in regarding this institution reinforces my belief that it is an extraordinary engine of social mobility and professional transformation. Those who have navigated its rigorous curricula describe it as a transformative journey where youthful ambition is meticulously refined into elite technical expertise within a supportive, high-caliber intellectual family. Ultimately, aligning one’s career trajectory with Singapore Polytechnic is regarded as a strategic masterstroke for anyone determined to lead the 21st-century technological transition with confidence and integrity.
Amazing! It is great place for the student. I visited for a mentoring session and it was a great experience.

Easy place to access as its near Dover mrt. May need a student pass to get thru the gantry. I was there to attend a public workshop and so the helpful security assisted me. Many students I met to ask for directions were very helpful. There are a few Food Courts (air-conditioning and non air-conditioning) plus varieties of food at reasonable price. As the venue is huge, one need to walk (take it as part of exercise). O
Present for graduation day a well known polytechnic in Singapore

I attended the SP EAE Open House today. Overall, the venue was well set up and I could see that effort had been put into organising the event for students and parents. However, I feel that the on-ground execution and communication could be improved.

The registration process at the entrance was generally clear, but the walk-in queue management was not very well coordinated. At around 9.30am, there was a long queue of parents and participants who seemed to believe that walk-ins were allowed. When asked, some student ambassadors were unsure whether the walk-in queue was open, which created confusion.

Wayfinding from the carpark to the event venue could also be clearer. More visible signage or better-positioned ambassadors would have helped visitors find their way more smoothly.

Inside the hall, the seating arrangement by faculty appeared to create some disruption. Participants who arrived late had to cross rows to get to their designated faculty areas, which made the session look quite chaotic at times. There were also many empty seats, which gave the impression that some who registered did not turn up, while others who were genuinely interested may not have been able to attend. Perhaps the main talk could remain registration-based, but seating could be on a first-come, first-seated basis instead of being divided by faculty.

For the workshop segment, I received different instructions from different ambassadors regarding whether separate registration was required. Some said it was not needed, while others said I had to re-register after the talk. In the end, I only managed to clarify with a lecturer that separate registration was not required. This should be clearly briefed to all ambassadors so visitors receive consistent instructions.

I also observed parents streaming into the workshop room without much guidance while the lecturer was still speaking to or briefing the student ambassadors. This made the flow look uncoordinated and slightly distracting. It would be better to have clearer crowd control and complete internal briefings before attendees are allowed into the room.

Overall, the event was meaningful and useful, but the on-ground communication, queue management, wayfinding, seating flow, and ambassador briefing can be reviewed. The website instructions may have been clear, but the actual ground communication needs to match that clarity.

Beautiful big campus. Can be quite a walk to the various schools. The canteen near the health sciences zone is very spacious, and quite a few international cuisines on offer. Lucky staff and students.
The staff and students at the optometry dept were all polite and professional. The Facility looked like a very good place to groom future optometrist.

​Ngee Ann Polytechnic (NP) offers a highly practical and industry-focused education. The curriculum is regularly updated with input from industry partners, and lecturers, often with professional experience, provide a realistic and engaging learning environment. The focus on project-based learning encourages critical thinking and problem-solving, which I found to be a more effective method of learning than traditional memorization.

​While the polytechnic's modular system can be demanding, with a constant cycle of assignments and projects, it effectively prepares students for the pressures of both higher education and the workplace. The campus life is equally vibrant, with a wide array of CCAs, excellent facilities, and diverse food options.

​Overall, NP provides a well-rounded experience, blending academic rigor with a rich student life. It's a great choice for students who thrive in a hands-on environment and are seeking to build a strong foundation for their future careers.
such a nice campus with best view and good food although quite hilly and requires extra effort to climb hehehe

It sounds like you're planning a trip to Ngee Ann Polytechnic (NP) Block 23! Here's a quick rundown to help you get there:

1. Take the MRT to Clementi Station.
2. At Clementi Station, exit towards Bus Interchange.
3. Board Bus 154 from Clementi Bus Interchange.
4. Get off at Ngee Ann Polytechnic (NP) stop.

You can use Google Maps or a transit app like Grab or Moovit for the most up-to-date bus schedules and routes. Have a smooth ride to your company event! 🚍🎉..
