place_name: EduFirst Learning Centre (Jurong East Branch) - English Math Chinese Science Tuition Centre Singapore
review_text: So far i am happy with the teacher here, they are patient and constanly update the progress of the child.  They are working hand in hand with parent on how to improve the child work.

Jurong West Public Library is a cozy and well-organized community library that’s perfect for quiet reading or studying. It offers a good selection of books across various genres, including a solid children’s section and useful resources for students. The seating areas are comfortable, and the environment is calm — ideal for concentration. Staff are friendly and helpful, and the self-service machines make borrowing and returning books easy. While it’s not the largest library in Singapore, it’s a welcoming spot for residents to read, learn, and relax.
A library at Frontier Community Club, within walking distance from Jurong Point. Clean and well maintained. Perfect for some quiet time.

Very nice service here. This is a 3 storey building merged with the community centre. It is accessible to the mrt through a pedestrian bridge from level3 of jurong point shopping mall. The 3 level are divided into sub categories like adult teenager and children stories. Cool to see a library integrated with the community club. Will be back again for more story books

Quiet, peaceful and cozy this place is perfect for book lovers that just one to have some quiet time alone in this quiet environment. Not only is this library used to read and borrow books, you can also bring your textbooks laptop phone here to do some revision or homework. There is also a variety of books that you can choose from. From comic books to novels and more. Highly recommended.

However it is a bit troublesome for people who drives. There is no waiting area and you have go 1 round to avoid blocking other drivers.
Love this library it's compact and kids section is awesome. No parking or stopping though. Difficult to return books
Few malay stalls here and gd food

There are 7 stalls now. 1 economic mixed rice with nasi lemak, 1 fishball noodle, 1 chick rice, 2 muslim food, 2 snacks and drinks.
One of Muslim food stall is Syed Ibrahim Biryani. Their trademark Mutton and chicken biryani available Tuesday and Thursday lunch time.
It’s a simple and basic food centre with a couple of food stalls (halal foods too) and many tables/seats.  It’s mainly opens for breakfast and lunch.
Good quality malay food with nice varieties! Best to come early before 12noon
Halal food Chinese food
Good food center 😋

Self service coffeeshop. Had black coffee and crispy pancakes here for breakfast. Black coffee is very good. The crispy pancakes are freshly made upon ordering, hot and fresh from the pan! Will be back.
The pancake was amazing — golden, crispy, and not too oily. It tasted very authentic and freshly made. Highly recommend if you love traditional flavours!

ordered
• set A (traditional kaya butter toast, 2 eggs, kopi) - $5.20
• set C (traditional peanut pancake, curry puff, iced milo - $5.10 ($4.40 + $0.70)
• black sesame crispy pancake - $1.80

reviews
• traditional kaya butter toast (4/5) - nice! but prefer yakun (crispier toast, more kaya & cold storage large slab butter)
• eggs (4/5) - for me it’s okay, normal eggs but it came broken
• kopi (3/5) - mediocre
• traditional peanut pancake (3/5) - nice and crispy, but a bit too thick for me — but that’s my personal preference
• curry puff (2/5) - prefer old chang kee, this is a decent traditional curry puff but skin could be crispier
• iced milo kosong gao (5/5) - good and thick enough
• [MUST ORDER ⭐️]black sesame crispy pancake (5/5) - perfect!!! must order 🤤

First time trying this cafe.  No aircon but with ceiling fans turning quite strong.   Got a set of peanut pancake + curry puff + teh @ $4.5.  Pancake taste fresh and good.  Curry puff was also filled with potatoes and small piece of egg.  Add on another crispy peanut pancake and another kaya pancake both taste good especially the crispy version.   Cheap and good breakfast.

Crust of the puffs was done nicely but they could have used more fillings. Cold drinks (coffee and tea) were too diluted, frankly very bland. Min jiang kueh was quite nice. The interior was quite modern. Gelato waffles were available.

The food was really good and tasty! Thought it would be a normal cafe but it exceeded our expectations. The music playlist was a total vibe as well. It was quiet and this cafe needs more attention for the quality it’s offering. I live at the east and came to Chinese Garden to visit and was happy we made the choice to have our food at Canopy. The service was also really nice and warm.

We had smoked duck , fish and chips, Caesar Salad , calamari , chicken pesto and margarita and tea

A great cafe in the midst of Chinese Gardens.  We were able to enjoy a nice and quiet lunch despite it being a weekend and everywhere would normally be swamped.  Food was surprising good as noted by many.  Our Caesar salad, mushroom soup and carbonara pasta was nicely presented and great tasting.  Happy that it wasn’t one of those ‘tourist trap’!  The vegetable growing display was somewhat strange looking due to the lighting.  Neither appetizing nor imparting a natural organic vibe.  Cafe was a small walk from the car park entering through Chinese Gardens gates.  Better signages will be appreciated as we had to ask for directions from the park’s customer service.

Bought my intern out for lunch to celebrate the final day of her internship today. Asked the counter guy to help us take this picture and accidentally mentioned it’s my intern’s last day. Fella and his team proceeded to surprise her with a cake. The food wasn’t mindblowing, but for the view and the vibes, it’s more than we could ask for. Thanks Canopy Jurong for adding this twist to make this day unforgettable for all of us present!

Pretty good, I must say, despite being pricey. The smoked salmon salad was good. And the crispy chicken starter was great (loved the sauce). Was it because I was hungry after birding? 🤣. Came back here for beer-battered fish and chips on 18/Feb/26. They make good stuff, really.

We held a ROM ceremony here, the staff was very helpful and friendly with our special dietary request. The ambience of the restaurant was very cozy and have good lighting for photo taking. Will definitely recommend this place if you are looking for event venue.

We came as early as 11:45 AM on a Friday, hoping for a good experience. Unfortunately, it was disappointing. Both the breast and thigh parts of the chicken were tough and dry. We also ordered the sizzling fish, which turned out to be just as hard. While the flavours were okay, the texture of the meat especially the chicken really let us down. It felt like the taste was there, but the inside was completely off. Definitely not what we expected. The chicken in Lucky plaza is superb 👍🏻 or maybe try to visit this shop on later day when they cook new batches.

You can see that this place serves good food when people always lining up and can smell how they grill their chicken in a unique sauce - Inasal way! Plus the owner is hands- on with his food business so great quality and taste always present here! Make sure to try the Kansi the beef so tender and love the tangy taste plus the chicken juicy and tender and a lot more of Filipino Food that our kababayan will surely enjoy esp when we miss our food back home. Value for money and top notch service always! Thanks to Ronald their store manager he was always friendly and attentive to all the customers. Pls visit this place just in Level 3 Makan Heritage IMM and its really a big place counter u can see at the end part. Nearest mrt Jurong East.

If you’re looking for authentic Pinoy food, visit them at Lucky Plaza and IMM . Highly recommended Sizzling Beef with bone marrow, chicken Inasal, boneless bangus and  chicken tail skewer. Perfect and will surely come back. Thanks for the sumptuous experience ❤️

Food is not satisfying. Ensaladang talong is not the traditional ensaladang talong that was grilled imstead it looks like it was boiled. Does not taste like ensaladang talong at all. Chicken inasal is average. Even the plain rice is a bummer, taste like a NFA rice

Tried the pecho (breast and wing) at JT’s Manukan Grille in IMM. The chicken was tender, juicy, and nicely grilled with a good smoky flavor. Paired well with the garlic rice and dipping sauce. Worth a try if you’re craving Filipino-style grilled chicken.
🤓Beer is cheap, liquor is cheap , cigarette prices are reasonable. They even sell frozen food !!! Nuggets, onion rings, chicken wings.
good to go after 12am if you wanna buy fresh seafood. so many varieties to choose from. Quite cheap if buy fresh from here if buy in bulk. Wear boots coz the floor is super wet

Jurong Fishery Port is Singapore's Biggest Fishery Port, and the other will be Senoko Fishery Port which is located around Sembawang area.
They're many varieties of Fishes & Seafood.
Great place to visit and buy all sort of sea food.. fresh and cheap .. If you are going to buy fishes or seafood, go before 3am. 1am is the most active timing of the Market where all Fishes and Seafood stock are just laid out, many varieties to choose from. I have shared more photos for a quick view.

Very cheap and fresh seafood to be purchased here. Many choices, many “vendors” and very helpful assistants. Asked them if they can help me to clean and cut the fish, they helped me for free. They even give discount for buying in bulk.

Have to change your NRIC or passport for a pass because you’re entering a port. But process was simple. Would recommend to come here to buy seafood. Best prime time to visit is 1am-3am because this is when the freshest seafood comes.

It is opened to the public...  Fish merchants start their operations at 12am.   Many Fishing vessels will call to port at about 2am to 4am.  That is the most busy time for the merchants.

Some pointers before you head to Jurong Fishery port.

1. Bring along IC or Passport (The pass office will not accept drivers license)

2. Bring an ice-box or a bucket..  This is to ensure that liquid from the seafood do not wet the cabin or boot of your vehicle.

3.  Footwear - best if you can wear waterproof rubber boots. (Like the ones Phua Chu Kang dons).  If not, put on an old pair of shoes or slippers.  Bring an extra pair of footwear so that you can change into before getting into your vehicle.

There is a public toilet with a facility to let you rinse your feet.

4. Purchase of seafood, try go with a minimal purchase of 1 kilogram.   Most fish merchants have ice and they are willing to put ice to the bag of seafood that you had purchased.   Should you feel that ice is not enough, you can purchase it from the ice vendor located just below AVA's port operation office.  They usually charge about $1 to fill up your icebox of seafood.

5.  When buying fish, do not expect them to descale or clean for you.  You will have to do it yourself.

6.  The following seafood are not always available.  Mud Crab, Rock Lobsters (the type without claws), flower crab, Lala (clams), Venus clams, Batang, thread fins (五渔- aka: ngoh - hir).

Happy Exploring...   Feel free to PM me if you have any questions.

Public transport- bus 98 nearest, 2nd bus 79 (need to walk longer) . Taxi or drop off/pick up outside the Guard house.

The Port is 24/7. No Market on Sunday

The Fish Market opens to visitors/public Monday to Saturday. Closed on Sundays and Public holidays. Operation time is 9pm to 5pm ( may not be accurate ).

There is a few coffee shops and seafood restaurants here. You got to try the food.

Best timing to come, early mornings for fresh seafoods buys and take away.

Toilet here abit old already, still functioning.
Newly renovated space with a great variety of machines. Much better than how it was previously. Still on the smaller side, but shouldn’t be an issue on non-peak periods. Staffs are super nice.

Wow…with facilities like have at the gym, it’s clean environment, central location and professional staff managing the gym, who needs to pay ridiculous membership fees for private gyms. Only downside is the hours of operation. It would be great if they could open at say 0600 hrs and close at 2300 hrs. But that’s really nitpicking - everyone has a life, including those who mind the gym.

decent gym but no long sleeve shirts allowed apparently cuz it's "inappropriate attire" even if you roll up your sleeves.
the staff are king so just take note ig

Small gym when compared to many other ActiveSG gyms.  Equipment is generally new enuff and functional.  A hawker center is located right across from the compound, making it easy to get some food after workout.
Good! Very new and great! 👍👍👍

Golden Village is a reliable cinema option in the west, located conveniently within Jurong Point near Boon Lay MRT. The cineplex has multiple screening halls with comfortable seating and good screen and sound quality, making it suitable for regular movie outings.

Ticketing is efficient with self-service kiosks and automated entry gates, which helps reduce waiting time during peak hours. The cinema typically screens a wide range of movies, from mainstream releases to popular blockbusters.

The vibe is casual and family-friendly, though it can get bustling during weekends and evening shows. Being inside Jurong Point also means easy access to food outlets and shops before or after your movie.

A practical choice if you’re looking for a straightforward and comfortable cinema experience in the Jurong area. 🎬🍿

Relatively great queue management for the Teochew movie but felt that information could’ve been disseminated better that tickets could also be bought from the self service kiosks too… was simply asked to queue up.

Was served at counter 2 by a young girl, very much appreciated her speedy and efficient service in helping me to select my desired seats even when most shows were almost sold out. Thank you!

Watched hoppers last night, last showtime of the day. Ordered a mix of sweet and salted popcorn, but some of the popcorn tasted weird, like antiseptic. It did have a numbing sensation. And it was mostly from the left side of my popcorn box (not sure if this even matters). Thankfully we already read the other review before we visited, so we stopped ourselves from consuming the rest of the popcorn 💔💔 Never had such issues with other GV outlets… please QC!!

24 Nov 2025, Went for Jurong GV, 11am Wicked for Good. Delay for 39 mins, picture of not align, seeing blur movie, later got picture no music, later got movie no picture. Could not finish movie because of delay. What GV is trying to do? Look more like a scam... Need to report to CASE for such issue. It so irresponsible. Spoil the whole program for the family days. Some audience left.

At 12pm, the staff ask the rest of the audience whether they would like to move to another theater to see the movie properly.

Haven’t been to the cinema in forever and finally went to catch Zootopia 2 but the seats smells here (Theatre 4). Wonder if it’s because not much people watch movies these days so the hygiene level dropped?

3 stars cause seat is pretty comfy and there’s Ijooz now which you can bring into the theatre! It’s priced $1 higher than other machine tho. GV toilets v big and clean.

Excellent outdoor play area for kids. Area is windy, clean and well maintained. There is eatery nearby (hawker, coffeeshop etc). Parking is a challenge for weekend morning. Playground is just beside busstop too.

Area is well covered by trees. Likely to be under sun during 12pm-2pm only.

Quite a big playground comprises of a musical section, slides and swings. Ideal for 5yo and above but I think some 2-4yo are also able to have fun here. Like the fact that even on a hot day, it's still shady here at 11.30am. There's also a fitness corner beside it. The nearest carpark is through the Block 255 entrance. Carpark is usually crowded on weekends. Can pack at other car parks which are slightly further but still within 5 min walking distance.
Apart from the tree-house themed playground which my kids enjoyed, there was an area with illusion exhibits further in which amused my kids. McDonald's is nearby. Great for an evening outing.
One of the bigger playground compound in Singapore, it is coupled with futsal field and fitness corner.  located in housing area, within close proximity to food drinks and other amenities.
Nice place for children and their parents for bonding times. At the nearby exercise area, parents can do their workout and watch over their children at playground the same time.

A pleasant neighbourhood park that’s generally well maintained and easy to enjoy. The greenery is neatly kept, paths are clean, and there’s a good mix of features like open lawns, walking paths, small water features, and basic fitness and play areas. It’s a comfortable space for a casual walk, light exercise, or a short break outdoors

The park is relatively small compared to some of Singapore’s larger parks, so it doesn’t take very long to explore. That said, its compact size also makes it convenient and accessible, especially if you live or work nearby and just want a quick escape into nature without travelling far

The park is steeped in history and local knowledge about plants if you know who to look for. Overall also easily 3 degree Celsius cooler.

I spotted a local water hen having a blast and thought it's cute

Weekend Wanders: Jurong Central Park
Took a stroll through Jurong Central Park over the weekend — a peaceful green escape nestled just minutes away from Jurong Point and Boon Lay MRT.

Though it’s not as expansive as Jurong Lake Gardens, the park offers a refreshing and lush environment that’s perfect for unwinding after a hearty lunch or shopping spree at the mall. With its open lawns, shaded walking paths, and the gentle rustling of trees, it’s a wonderful spot to just pause and breathe.

If you’re around Jurong and need a short nature break, this park is definitely worth a visit. Sometimes, it’s the smaller green spaces that offer the biggest reset.

Jurong Central Park is a well-maintained, family-friendly park in Singapore, featuring creative play areas for children and ample space for walking, running, and jogging.

Some visitors have noted its location in the western part of the island, which may be less convenient for travelers staying in other areas.

Overall, the park is appreciated for its clean environment and suitability for various outdoor activities.

🌳Jurong Central Park is located across Jurong Point.
🪱🪜🛩️The Park have unique Playgrounds, with one featuring a life-sized snakes & ladders game and another play area with life-sized ludo game, although these were a little rundown now.
🎲A distinctive red Dice Clock Tower stands majestically right across Boon Lay MRT Station at the West Entrance
❓Guess what is the Tower for?
🛝There is a regular sand-filled Playground with slides, see-saw, car ride, carousel, balance beam and more for smaller children.
🧢This spacious green lung is well shaded with jogging paths winding around the entire serene Park.
🪻Over at the southern end of the lush Park is a Water Lily Pond with an abundance of lovely water lilies and other aquatic plants.
🐛🐝🌺🌽Allotment Garden, Community-in-Bloom Garden and Boon Lay Nature Garden, allow gardening enthusiasts to grow herbs, vegetables and ornamental plants.
🏋️A recently upgraded Fitness Corner located located across the Water Lily Garden, let joggers warm up and have some muscle toning and strength training in between the regular jogging.
🛴🚲The Park is well connected to various Park Connectors.
🚊It's conveniently located right at the doorstep of Boon Lay MRT.
🌳Jurong Central Park is a conveniently located green lung with regular Park amenities for all to enjoy.

A beautiful, relaxing and vibrant place for walks, for taking a step back from the hectic pace.
Spending 3 hours walking and appreciating the fresh air, the manicured gardens, the lake and wonderful architecture, helped to destress and reset.
If you've not been, well worth some time out.

Definitely one of the nicest and must go to parks in Singapore if you are alone, couples, families group of friends wanting to escape from the concrete jungle of the city. Jurong used to be perceived as a very faraway venue to commute to but with so many new MRT train lines it’s so much easier and faster to reach. Do take note of opening hours. Free entry and easily accessible with many entrances by Chinese Garden MRT or Lakeside MRT and buses. Great for all activities, walking , strolling, jogging, cycling, picnics and there is even a skate park and dog run. What makes this park stand out from the rest is the sheer size, the large lake with canoeing; stunning replicas of historical Chinese and Japanese architecture and pagodas, bonsai garden and wildlife. And especially the stunning Lily Flower Pond at the Japanese Garden and the Lotus Flower Pond at the Chinese Garden. Definitely worth to make a trip down to enjoy this green oasis escapade rich in flora and fauna and water. It’s very tranquil and peaceful from Mondays to Saturdays. Ample toilets and drinking water dispenser and also a beverage shop selling nice cold beverages.

NParks really did a good job in revamping this park.
There's plenty of interesting plants and wildlife to spot.
Took me and my friends 3 hours to casually explore the entire area.
It's definitely a nice place to run in the mornings/evenings.

I visit Jurong Lake Gardens quite frequently since I am staying nearby. The walking tracks are well-maintained, and the view around the lake at anytime is just impressive. It’s a wonderful place to clear your mind and enjoy the beautiful scenery during the walks.

I highly recommend to visit this gardens, as there are a lot of attractions to see and they are all free! It’s a beautiful area to dive into culture and nature, just 40 min from Singapore with the bus. Very relaxing and a moment to take a pause from the overtourism and consumistic view of marina bay and the Singapore center.
Not as big as the botanic garden, but I’d say it’s better to view this than that.

If you’re looking for a peaceful retreat that feels miles away from the city without actually leaving it, Rasau Walk at Jurong Lake Gardens is an absolute gem. I recently spent several minutes here, and it is easily one of the most picturesque boardwalks in Singapore

Rasau Walk, located in Jurong Lake Gardens, is a peaceful boardwalk surrounded by lush greenery and wetlands. Named after the Rasau tree, it offers scenic views of aquatic plants like lotus flowers and water lilies. The family-friendly trail is perfect for birdwatching and spotting dragonflies. It provides a tranquil escape, ideal for a leisurely stroll or relaxation.

The best part of Jurong Lake Gardens. The definite visit time would be at night when all the surrounding is dark, where you see river of orange light along the pathway. Very nice. There are several public benches but no rain shelter, and it's totally understandable as you don't want to break the view.
Good view, but not as the photo shown on maps, the wood is old, but it’s still a great walk and really enjoyable.

Rasau Walk is a scenic Boardwalk that meanders along the freshwater Jurong Lake's water edge. This carefully restored natural habitat is home to a variety of Palm Trees that include Nibong, Sealing Wax (aka Lipstick Palm) and Rasau (Screwpine) aka Mengkuang Laut in Malay.
Others include
-Nipah Palm, - "attap chee", attap roof thatches
-Salak Palm - Salak or snake fruit
-Sago Palm - sago
The habitat is also a feeding ground for shore birds.
Come enjoy the tranquility of the Rasau Walk snaking along the water's edge and try spotting the attap seeds and salak fruits.

Rifle Range Nature Park is a great extension to the Bukit Timah nature area, offering a mix of easy trails, lush rainforest scenery, and interesting glimpses into Singapore’s natural and historical heritage. The park is well connected, with clearly marked paths, boardwalks, and links to nearby nature reserves

One unexpected highlight was spotting a durian tree - and even seeing fallen durians on the ground, a reminder of how rich and diverse the forest ecosystem is. Along the trails, you’ll also find impressive giant trees, dense vegetation, quarry views, and remnants of the area’s past hidden among the greenery

I loved Rifle Range Nature Park — it’s very friendly for children and the elderly. The boardwalks are well-paved, anti-slip, and mostly barrier-free, so it’s easy for prams and seniors to walk comfortably.

The access is super convenient: just take Beauty World MRT Exit A, go to Level 4 of Beauty World Centre, and cross the overhead bridge that leads directly into the park. Highlights include the Quarry Wetland, Colugo Deck, and a fun Forest Exploration Trail for kids. Highly recommended for a relaxed family nature walk.

Nice new park with natural trails and paved paths. Has many unspoilt flora and fauna, saw several durian and starfruit trees along the paths. Not so easy to get to if depending on public transport. Toilets and carpark are available and some paths r equipped with CCTV! Not crowded on a weekday morning.

A nice trek away from the hustle and bustle of the city located in the Bukit Timah area of Singapore. Amidst a lush green rain forest with thriving ecosystems Easy to access and to reach on foot and public transport from the nearest MRT station called Beauty World - Downtown Line (Blue Coloured Line). An overhead bridge links one of the entrances of the park to the roof top Food Court (highest floor) of Beauty World Shopping Centre. Easy enough for amateur trekkers, family outings, a stroll in the park or a jog. There is a main building for meeting area, car drop off and pick up, giant ceiling fans to cool you down, chilled drinking water dispenser, vending machines, maps, loo and a park ranger on duty.  Good place to shelter if it rains There are some slopes on the way up to the cliff top of the old quarry with a stunning viewing Birds Eye area perched right at the top of the cliff. Not a long distance but short slightly steep slopes but totally worth the climb. Saw families with kids around the age of 5 and above at the top so it’s really not that challenging or difficult. Also Not as far as I expected. But do take note of your way back because if you take a wrong turn and end up on Rifle Range Road it’s more than a 1 hour walk to the nearest MRT station especially if you are already tired. However the paths are all well paved and safe. Do note the park closes at 7pm. You can also access this park from the Green Corridor.

Beautiful place for a calm walk. The trail is very well-maintained and the elevated boardwalk makes the whole route super easy, even for beginners. The Colugo Deck is the main highlight amazing views and a great breeze at the top. Saw a few birds and a monitor lizard along the way. Not too crowded in the morning. Really nice spot if you want nature without a tough hike.

As a nearby resident, I visit Jurong Lake Gardens quite frequently. The walking tracks are always well-maintained, and I enjoy the scenic lake views. There are also plenty of shaded paths, which make for a very relaxing walk.

A pleasant but fairly small section of Jurong Lake Gardens. The landscaping is neat and well maintained, and there are some nice plants and boardwalk paths to stroll through, but it doesn’t take very long to cover the entire area

It’s fine to visit if you’re already nearby or doing a full loop of the gardens, but probably not worth going out of your way just for this section alone

The newly renovated Jurong Park @Chinese Garden @Japanese Garden is amazing. Many interesting plants, ponds, and fixtures are unique to only this park. Many themes are nicely decorated.

Food stores are scattered everywhere while facilities like toilets and water dispensers are generously available.

It's a good park for exploration, evening walks, and slow biking.

A huge garden! Took about 2 hours to slowly stroll, enjoy the view and reach the other end of the garden. It’s home to many animals and there’s so much to see. My favorite starts from the Japanese Garden onwards. Really enjoy the peaceful environment there and nice place to totally unwind. It’s a short walking distance from the MRT station.
nice and peaceful place. great for walks, jogs and runs. some do cycle around.
You can improvement yourself with skillful jobs techniques. Learn new skills.

Horrible website. Load longer than using a windows 95 computer. Please do something about it.

It’s so unstable and unusable.

No idea why it requires OTP. who is even gonna hack your account and what for? Unnecessary extra step.

It is a very huge port. If you need to go to main gate there's always a free shuttle service for every 30 mins. There's a seaman club which has free WiFi and other indoor games. Walking through the port isn't advisable as it's too huge

Absolutely horrible unmanaged Pass Office.
- SMS queue system is a big joke.
- Missed queue numbers screen is not used.
Spent 1.5h waiting to complete 7min formality.
It’s ridiculous! What a shame!
JURONG PORT PASS OFFICE AREA

1. It is the 1st largest man-made floating wetlands in SG.
2. Opened in 2021, it is a key feature of the lake enhancement, offering a large boardwalk and serving as a natural habitat that improves water quality.
3. It is managed under National Water Agency PUB’s Active, Beautiful, Clean Waters (ABC Waters) Programme, the wetlands not only play a part in maintaining the water quality in the reservoir but also creates a natural habitat for wildlife.
4. It consists of a 22 cm thick buoyant layer made from five layers of foam mats and coconut mats, which supports diverse aquatic plants.
5. Area of constructed floating wetlands 3,850m²; Area of Jurong Lake 70 hectares (700,000m²)

I was Just passing by Jurong Lakeside and just decided to walk through and that was a nice decision as it was really peaceful and relaxing! The view there is Especially nice and Moreso from the Chinese Garden MRT Side, definitely will be coming here again to check out the Chinese Garden Islet!

A fantastic spot for wide, open views of Jurong Lake. From here, you get a clear and scenic perspective of both Jurong West and Jurong East buildings, nicely framed by greenery and water. The elevated boardwalks make it feel immersive yet peaceful, especially on a breezy or cloudy day

It’s a great place to pause, take photos, or simply enjoy the contrast between nature and the surrounding urban skyline

A good place to take a small walk alone or with your friends and family. But you gotta know that this place is really small. If you got enough time then why not take a look at the nearby Chinese and Japanese gardens.

Beautifully created floating platform on top of the lake with a closer next-to-water experience on the lake. Has many seats along this platform for resting as well as to catch sunrise and potentially some sunset views.

Good placement in front of Starbucks that you can take a break there if you’re tired or thirsty as well!

As full-time working parents without a village, Superland Jurong has truly become our village. My child has been with Superland since his infant care days, and I’m incredibly grateful for the care and dedication the team has shown throughout his journey.

One thing that really stood out to me when my child first started was that every staff member in the school seems to know every child by name. It made the environment feel very warm, personal, and reassuring as a parent, knowing that the children are genuinely seen and cared for by the entire team.

The environment is relatively new, as the centre only started in 2023. The classrooms are fully air-conditioned and thoughtfully designed with glass-partitioned rooms instead of an open concept, which I personally prefer as it creates a calmer environment that supports better learning and concentration.

One of my favourite aspects of Superland is the strong emphasis on parent involvement and school excursions. These opportunities allow parents to participate in the learning journey and create meaningful bonding moments with our children within the school environment.

The teachers consistently put in a tremendous amount of effort into preparing learning materials, decorating the classrooms, and coordinating school events. Their dedication is something I noticed and appreciated from day one. The curriculum follows a Montessori-integrated approach, and I love that the children also get outdoor time every day.

Choosing Superland was a well-thought-out decision after touring more than 10 preschools, and I have never once regretted it. My child is thriving, learning new things every day, and most importantly, he is always happy to go to school. He loves his teachers and his friends.

A heartfelt thank you to Ms Jolene and the entire Superland Jurong team for being such caring, responsible, and passionate educators!

We moved our child from a POP school to Superland Jurong, and the difference has been very noticeable. Since making the switch in last year, our child has been able to clearly share what he learns in school every day, which really shows how engaged the children are.

Principal Jolene leads the team wonderfully, and the team are incredibly passionate about the children and genuinely care about their development.

The school offers a very enriching program — from Sports Day to the impressive year-end performance and art showcase. The weekly activities like show-and-tell, cookery, art, PE and Montessori classes are all conducted by different specialized teachers, which adds a lot of variety and depth to the learning experience.

Another thing we appreciate is the teacher-to-child ratio. Classes are often split into smaller groups for different activities, allowing more attention for each child. Even when we travel for longer holidays, the teachers take the time to help our child catch up on any missed work.

What stands out most is how warm and attentive the environment is, many teachers, even those from other classes, remember the children by name.

We’re very happy with our experience at Superland and grateful to the entire team for creating such a nurturing and engaging environment for the kids.

My son attends Superland at PBC since PG last year, he loves it there and shares about his day and activities happily at the end of each day. He knows all the trs by their names and trs are passionate and dedicated in their work, we can see it in their various interactions with the kids and at events.

I have only praises for his trs, Tr Reena and Jin Lao Shi. They post regular updates of his progress and activities via the sch app, and also post weekly curriculum plans with links to resources so we know what the children are learning and we can reinforce at home. They both are experienced and knowledgeable in their craft and inspire children to learn with them enjoyably. They are prompt in communicating over the app and face to face and are always readily available and open to comms. Their monthly subject update also clearly show the various interesting activities the children engage in. The termly parent-tr meetings and reading the semestral individual portfolio tell me that they really have close interaction with the kids and they know my son’s strengths and areas for improvement.

Tr Reena has been with the class since PG in 2024 and has built strong rapport with all her students and their parents. She knows my son well and offers insightful advice about his learning and behaviour, and tips for toilet training etc. all at very developmentally-appropriate junctures. When managing his final recovery from lingering illnesses, she handles these responsibilties with care and commitment, meticulously ensuring that his needs are consistently met by other teachers who also help out with the class especially when she is not around and that parents receive regular updates.

She has also played a strong supportive role in helping to monitor and manage my son’s emotional needs from school when a new sibling came along, such that he experienced minimal disruptions, was able to express himself openly and cope with the changes in his life. We are extremely grateful for her help and the support of teachers who regularly interact with my son, such as Jin Laoshi, Tr Ferah, Tr Hidayah, Tr Latha, Tr Shermaine etc.

The building has v high ceiling so the sch feels v spacious, each clsrm is separated w its own glass doors/walls. The meal plan shows a gd variety and pics of the meal are shared frequently. They have bus service as well to n fro sch. The two indoor playgrounds are the main attraction for my son, they play frequently there.

There are regular updates on my son’s learning through individual montessori activities, art activities as they have in-hse art specialist tr, and weekly PE lessons. Photos of classroom learning and celebrations are also posted in the app in a v timely manner. There are curriculum briefings, annual art exhibition that parents can attend. All major NE events are celebrated like in pri sch, even sports day and graduation day (PG participated too, v gd exposure). There are also termly excursions that align to curriculum, some are parent-accompanied. The school and P (Ms Jolene) are also receptive to and have acted on parents’ feedback constructively and we are thankful for their prompt actions. Hopefully the school will continue to be able to retain their good teachers and look out for their welfare as sometimes there are consecutive events close to one another (art exhib & RHD). Perhaps can look at combining celebrations and themes tgt, e.g. parents’ day + curriculum sharing this year was a good move.

Lastly, the location is awesome as it’s walkable from JE train station n sheltered all the way. Right nx to the sch there is koufu food court n a tiny cafe. Link bridges to ntfgh/jem where there r more food options.

I want to express my heartfelt appreciation for the wonderful preschool experience my child has at Superland Jurong East.

The teachers and principal Ms Jolene are truly lovely and provide frequent updates that keep us informed and engaged. The learning materials prepared by the teachers are exceptional, fostering a rich environment for our children's growth. I also love how beautifully decorated the school is for each festival—it really adds to the community spirit and teaches the children to embrace their friends of different races. The decorations and games truly never fail to impress me!

Thank you for your prompt responses to our questions; it makes a world of difference. We are so grateful for the care and dedication you show every day :)

My girl transferred to Superland since mid of her N2.

Choosing the right school had been a tiring journey for me and her daddy but we are glad that we chose Superland eventually.

We were impressed by the size and layout of the center during the school tour and was fascinated that they had a playground inside their centre and my girl enjoy it very much . Love the weekly updates and photos of my girl progress and can feel the genuine passion and love from the teachers. Most importantly is my girl enjoy learning at school and happily share with us the journey of her daily learning.

Kudos to all the teachers and staffs of Superland JE. Their dedication, passion and nurturing to kids are greatly appreciated!

Hi, I am a september intake student. Just wanted to share some good notes of this college, as it is the second month pf mine being here the atmosphere has given a comfort. The trainers are so good that their explanations match students level. All the facilities here are up to the mark & all in all it can be known as the Singapore’s best college.
Highly recommended.

experience at this college has been truly enriching. The faculty members are knowledgeable, supportive, and always ready to guide students beyond academics. The campus environment is friendly and encourages learning, creativity, and personal growth. Various seminars, workshops, and cultural activities helped me build confidence and practical skills. Overall, this college has played an important role in shaping my academic and professional journey.

I want to share my genuine appreciation for the recent Diwali celebration at Spring College International. As an international student, this event genuinely made me feel at home.

The college put in exceptional effort. The Cultural Quiz was a fun challenge, and it was great to see prizes given not just to the quiz winners, but also for the Best Traditional Dress! Recognizing these efforts, along with sharing snacks, shows how much they care.

This commitment to ensuring international students feel welcomed is truly commendable. It makes Spring College International a wonderful place to study.

Hey, I’m the student of this college and i want to share my experience with you. I’m genuinely feel grateful that i came to this college and pursue my studies from here. My experience was quite nice with them throughout my stay in Singapore and the college supported me so much under my student pass especially during unexpected situations and challenges. This college provides me a great environment. Thank you so much!

Being an international student isn't easy, but spring college helped me face every challenge - from academics to settling in . The faculty and staff always made me feel supported . Truly grateful for the guidance. I highly recommend this college to all especially international students

vibrant, authentic, cool.brings me back in time. really enjoyed coming here because it gave that 1990s Singapore feels. Everything changed too fast now & everywhere seems to look the same. However Yuhua has their own character and charm that makes it stand out. good place to explore if around Chinese Garden.
This neighbourhood has a market, a hawker centre, a few kopitiams and many small businesses. There was a surprisingly large number of massage parlours. Nothing particularly interesting here.
Good place to eat some teochew cuisines such as freshly steamed Red Grouper Fish, kidneys, livers, bak kut teh (pork ribs soup), 'chap chye', 'chye buay', 'yu tiao', teochew kways!
First time trying prawn noodles from Seng Kee Family Restaurant. Flavourful & the portion is just right. We are back for the 担担面 & 红油抄手 from Ah Wei Jing Dian, the best we have tried so far 👍🏻

Old neighbourhood with lots of good eats, local hawker centre and coffeeshops, entire row of shophouse housing PRC steamboat, fast casual Western restaurant

Lots of wellness shops for Spa, massage, TCM

Medical clinic and Dentist also available

There is a public stadium, adventure swimming pool, gym and sports lab but alot are closed now or limited access due to COVID-19 Phase 2...
SCAMMERS. Over charged me 3.60 cents for an extra egg. And dishes were cold, no flavour, and people were rude. Will never ever come back to this cai fan again. Horrible!

The food court have a variety of food was craving for stingray have it at $15 for small size and mapo tofu at the mala stall it is not bad for the taste and it’s not that hot at night.There are adequate seats as well my favourite duck stall is still available I recall it’s been open since I’m a child.

Claypot rice was quite good. Very fairly priced. Will eat there again. Try other stuff on the menu.

Tried the chicken rice stall too. Not bad and good price for the chicken.

Tried the new Taiwanese food that replaced the Pao fan stall. Slightly pricey considering the portion and taste. $5.70 for the mala fried rice (no meat) and also 5.70 for the chicken cutlet noodle, noodles was starchy. Chicken was bad, looks pathetic. Mala fried rice was ok. Would have been great if there was meat added.

A coffeeshop for all Singaporeans, with Chinese, Malay and Indian food.
The Indian Muslim food stall has vegetarian options for vegetarians, the Bee Hoon Goreng Putih ( I ordered without the Ikan Bilis ) is delicious, freshly fried with Wok Hei, highly recommended.
There’s also a vegetarian stall at the back. Enjoy your meal.

Tried Zero Hour Coffee and got their Iced V60, really solid! Super clean and refreshing, smooth all the way even after the ice melted. You can tell they care about how they brew it.

It’s homebased and on Grab, which makes it even better. Honestly quite rare to find this kind of quality coffee in the Jurong area.

Will definitely order again.

Great tasting coffee!! I have gotten 2 flat white and 1 ice latte, both are really fresh. These coffee taste are distinct and can tell that it was made with great care. The coffee amount was good as there was barely any ice in the coffee so it does not dilute the taste😍😍

The coffee was very fragrant and even my parents enjoyed it a lot. You can tell that the owner used high grade quality coffee beans for each cup of coffee as beans did not taste sour like most cafes do. The grab driver even mentioned that while on the way of delivering it, his whole car smells super fragrant from the coffee. Giving 2 thumbs up suggesting that he will try it one day too!
Really enjoyed the coffee, super fragrant and easy to drink! Definitely will purchase again!
Super good coffee, ordered an Oat Milk Latte!

place_name: YC Dining & Bar | Western Restaurant Bar
review_text:
First time here after searching for nearby burger joints. The burger is 9/10 even though it's not a burger joint. The other dishes are pretty tasty too with reasonable sharing portions for 2. Food was so yummy that emptied the plates!

The aglio olio was tasty but too spicy and the staff kindly exchanged upon request. The service was great and attentive too.

Will be back for a family meal for sure.
👍👍👍

place_name: YC Dining & Bar | Western Restaurant Bar
review_text:
Alfresco dinning as too limited sitting indoor. Service were excellent, friendly and attentive.
The food recommended were up to beat. We throughly enjoyed our evening.

place_name: YC Dining & Bar | Western Restaurant Bar
review_text: Tried the steak and it didn’t disappoint. It was super juicy, tender, and had a really nice char on the outside. Cooked just right and full of flavor—definitely worth ordering again.

place_name: YC Dining & Bar | Western Restaurant Bar
review_text:
Definitely a no-go for dinner on a Friday night.

Can't speak for other days, but it felt like the venue was seating customers from multiple restaurants despite the kitchen clearly not being able to handle the volume. We waited about an hour for our food, and there were 10-minute gaps between dishes.

Taste-wise, the food was decent, though at this price point I was expecting a bit more. We did not order alcohol but just 3 dishes and the bill added up to $80++ . Service was great and could see the indian and filipino service lady and man were trying their best to serve everyone. 100/100 for them.

If you're going just for drinks, I'd recommend it. But for dinner, especially on a busy night, probably not.

place_name: YC Dining & Bar | Western Restaurant Bar
review_text: Nice environment sitting outside with wind blowing. The service is very good. Just a bit slow for food, but understandable as there's isn't many staffs working. Food is on point. Great place for a cosy chit chat with friends.

Absolutely fantastic dining experience at He Jia Huan Chinese Restaurant! The food was authentic, flavorful, and clearly prepared with great attention to detail. Every dish we ordered was delicious, from the perfectly seasoned stir-fries to the rich, comforting soups.

The service was equally impressive—staff were attentive, friendly, and made us feel very welcome throughout the meal. The atmosphere was clean, cozy, and perfect for both family gatherings and casual dinners.

Great value for money as well, with generous portions and reasonable prices. Will definitely be coming back again. Highly recommended for anyone craving quality Chinese cuisine!

Stumbled upon this place and honestly, it exceeded expectations. The ambience is warm and vibrant with a nice Chinese-style interior that feels both cozy and lively. Perfect spot for casual dinners or supper.

Food was really solid — dishes were flavorful, well-seasoned, and came out quite fast. You can tell it’s proper wok cooking with that nice “wok hei” taste. Portions are decent for sharing too.

Service was friendly and efficient despite the crowd, and the staff were attentive without being intrusive.

Overall, great value for money with good vibes, good food, and smooth service. Will definitely come back again 👍🏻

Portion size is huge and wide variety of choices. BBQ skewers are nice but forgot to take photos.

This place filled out fast but the seating area is huge I came on a weekday with no reservations but manage to get a table

Had a great experience at this Chinese restaurant! The food was delicious fresh, flavorful, and nicely presented. The staff were friendly and attentive, making sure everything was served promptly. The restaurant had a cozy atmosphere with clean and comfortable seating. Prices were reasonable for the quality and portion sizes. I’d definitely come back and recommend it to others
I’ve enjoyed dining here before, but this time the food was a little too salty for my taste. Hope it was just an off day. Will still be back.

Came here for lunch on a Friday and it was a cool respite from the heat! Ordered the Har Cheong Gai Waffles and they were so flavourful and crispy! The waffles were similar to those from dessert shops: soft and fluffy, which complimented the super crispy chicken very well. We'll be back for this!!

We got a Eggs Benedict with Smoked Salmon and it was alright. Eggs were well done but not very warm. The bread was very tasty and easy to cut up too.

The vibes are lovely as you're overlooking the gardens and greenery. Ice water is also available which is a huge plus in our hot climate!

21 February 2026, Saturday

We had a wonderful experience at Eden.
The staff kindly took the initiative to WhatsApp us beforehand so we could place our order in advance since we were coming for iftar, which was very thoughtful and convenient. They also made sure we had a lovely spot with a beautiful background to celebrate our son’s birthday.

The food was delicious, the ambience was pleasant, and the service was excellent. There are both indoor and outdoor seating options. The outdoor area is perfect if you enjoy a natural setting, but if you prefer to avoid insects, the indoor seating may be more comfortable.

Highly recommended for special occasions and gatherings.

We have heard quite a bit about the café located in Chinese Garden, but little did we know that Eden Café sits just a stone’s throw away from the iconic Cloud Pagoda, directly in front of Pagoda Plaza.Tourist Destinations

Getting there by MRT is simple and convenient. Just alight at Chinese Garden station, cross the Chinese Garden Bridge, and turn right. For those who are driving, the walk is slightly longer as the recommended parking spot is the MSCP at Blk 354 Jurong East Ave 1.

We arrived at Eden Café during sunset, and it turned out to be the perfect time to visit. A gentle breeze, warm sunlight, and the surrounding greenery created a very relaxing garden atmosphere. The exterior blends naturally with the park setting, while the interior of the café is kept modern, clean, and simple.

As the sky gradually darkened, the ambience transformed into something equally charming. With the Cloud Pagoda lit up in the background, the café offered a completely different yet equally pleasant evening vibe.

To cool ourselves down, we started with two refreshing drinks, the Lychee Pink Splash and Honey Grapefruit. The Lychee Pink Splash was a delightful mix of lychee fruit, strawberry, and calamansi, giving it a sweet yet tangy profile. The Honey Grapefruit was light, citrusy, and equally refreshing.

From the All Day Brunch menu, we tried the Singapore Har Cheong Gai Waffles. The crispy shrimp paste fried chicken paired surprisingly well with waffles, garnished with fresh parsley. A drizzle of maple syrup tied the flavours together, making this a bold yet enjoyable and shareable dish.

It was hard to ignore the local delights section of the menu, especially after spotting curry chicken and nasi lemak. We eventually decided on the Eden Nasi Lemak with Curry Chicken. The fragrant coconut rice was served warm, accompanied by tender curry chicken in a separate bowl, along with sliced cucumber, hard-boiled egg, sambal chilli, peanuts, and fried anchovies. It was comforting, satisfying, and flavourful.

Sticking to the brunch selections, we also ordered the Creamy Avocado Buttermilk Fried Cod Fish Pasta. The fried cod fish sat atop pasta coated in a creamy avocado sauce, complemented by cherry tomatoes, yellow onions, salsa, and pea sprouts. The result was a rich and well-balanced pasta dish.

Before leaving the café, we made sure to try their unique Coconut Ice Cream. Served in an actual coconut shell imprinted with “Eden,” the coconut ice cream was drizzled with gula melaka and sprinkled with crushed peanuts. On the side, fresh coconut juice from the fruit was served in a small glass, making the experience even more memorable.

Overall, beyond its pleasant garden setting and scenic view of the Cloud Pagoda, the food at Eden Café was above average. It’s definitely a place I wouldn’t mind bringing friends to. The café is also halal-certified, making it a welcoming spot for many diners.

This place is so remote that you need to spend time navigating just to get there.
Do not attempt to drive, as the parking is so freaking far away, as you'd need to park at an HDB car park and spend another 15 minutes of walking.
The weather was scorching hot, and the assistant manager told me that indoor tables were not available. But she was fast enough to say, "Let me check if I can seat you in the indoor section."
The air conditioner blowing inside wasn't too generous, and my partner and I had our lunch with perspiration.
The menu looks tempting, but it was far from it in taste. The rendang bolognese was so diluted and had no taste. I would give it to the seafood tofu dish. That's the only dish that was worth paying for. The waiters serving your food make you feel that you're just another annoying customer who made them do more things, as they had to serve your food.

Visited Eden with a group of seven friends on a Saturday afternoon, and we were pleasantly surprised that it wasn’t crowded at all. We had originally booked outdoor seating since the indoor area was apparently full, but upon arrival, we were able to switch to indoor seating with no issue. Really appreciated that kind of flexibility—it made the experience even better.

Set within a lush, garden-like environment, the space felt peaceful and serene—perfect for a relaxed catch-up over lunch.

We ordered a variety of dishes across the menu, from pasta and toast to rice options. I went for their signature Eden Curry Chicken with Rice, and it did not disappoint. The chicken was tender, the curry gravy rich and full of flavour, and the level of spice was just right. A small but thoughtful touch—the chicken was already deboned—made it even more enjoyable (especially for someone like me who prefers a fuss-free meal!).

Another highlight was the complimentary drinking water, with a cold option available, which was much appreciated in the warm weather. The staff were friendly and welcoming, adding to the overall pleasant dining experience.

Definitely a spot I’d return to, especially to try more items on their menu.
love the deals to be had here. come here to get cheap green tea ayakata and sometimes got beer deal as welll ah

I called this old lady Chinese lady to serve me because I’ve been waiting in the solve for 15 minutes, but she tacked somehow did not listen to me. I do not know if she have some hearing problems or what but her attitude for the past few times that I visit the 711 storewas very bad. This review is to inform people out there please get ready for an unpleasant trip or experience.  Was very bad. This review is to inform people out there please get ready for an unpleasant trip or experience to this store to the store to this Store due to the attitude of these staffs!!!

Everytime when i go during the evening around 1600-1800 there will be that one employee who will just do its own business while people are queuing up waiting to pay for the items where there is no one at the cashier. Never encounter any other 711 with that problem. Not sure why is that person still employed with that substandard service. Other than that the other employess are great.

On 8 Apr around 10.30am, I Brought a 25% off scrambled egg and ham wrap where original price is $3 and a 1 for 1 coffee which is $3.30 and one plastic bag which is $0.10. Got charged $6.85 for some reason when the total should have been $5.65 ($3.30 + $2.25 + $0.10) and I was rushing and paid without checking and only realised after I have left. Would love to know where the extra $1.20 came from…
The Chinese old lady at the 6.30am shift really mean. Maybe she’s cranky after an overnight shift I understand but quite rude to just ignore/argue with customer

A popular local food centre among residents in the area, this spot offers a variety of options for vegans, vegetarians, and anyone looking for meatless meals.
There are two Chinese vegetarian stalls - Wan Yuan Vegetarian and Tofu Vegetarian Food — both of which are vegan-friendly and allium-free. You'll also find a Delisnacks stall serving traditional vegan and vegetarian snacks. In addition, some stalls, such as Thunder Bowl, offer dishes with vegetarian options.

There are a number of stalls i like. Lots of choices within this hawker centre.

The traditional Yam Cake and 肠粉 stall,
the Chicken porridge and chinese salad stall, Porridge stall that sells pork trotters, 擂茶，云吞面，酿豆腐，fishball noodles and Tang gui duck & roast pork rice. Recently another new stall Hello Fish. Pretty good. I tried the Assam style.

My favourite beef noodles stall had gone. So sadz.

I wish the tables can be cleaned promptly and concerned party to monitor the issue of excessive crows, magpies and pigeons picking food around the hawker centre. Some crows can be aggressive too.

Very crowded even on weekday morning. Soaking in the surrounding booths selling CNY goodies. Had the Beo Crescent Signature Lor Mee. The volume is big with lots of ingredients of pork, egg, fried fish etc. lots of good food in this Food centre. Highly recommended.

​Update: 12 Mar 26

Had the $7 set at 潮汕卤味 Kway Chap for brunch. The taste was just so-so, but the main issue was the chilli, way too much vinegar. All in all, this Kway Chap is not to my personal taste, so I won't be visiting again. ⭐️⭐️

_________________________
First time trying the Nasi Lemak at 隆兴 today, as my usual stalls at Boon Lay Hawker Centre are temporarily closed for renovation. The rice was surprisingly good and fragrant, and the chili was spot-on. I paid $3.80 for fish, luncheon meat, and otah.
Definitely a good temporary fix.

Actually not bad for a hawker stall food ! Can’t say as same as Malaysian Food, but for a standard level curry mee, it is slightly higher then average, which is satisfactory. Owner of the franchise will listen to suggestions and improve over time. Which I think this values more then the price itself.
Obviously I won’t eat this at this price every day. But when feeling to reward myself or bringing guest to eat some good but not too expensive fine dining, this is one of the options.
Poster for free dinner for senior citzon was put on life on sunday 30 nov 25. On 10.30am 1 dec 25.  The staff said sold out. Like that mihht not put up the poster.

Excellent location for neighbourhood hartland community club near Jurong East Swimming Complex Water Park Chinese Garden with plenty of greenery natural river Jurong Lake connection to Lakeside MRT station via bus services. Closed to Hua Yio Secondary school Jurong Junior College Boon Lay Shopping Centre Taman Jurong Market Food Centre and many other hawker centres with plenty of dinning shopping banking library postage entertainment and amenities nearby. Friendly cosy comfy welcoming ambience for students tourists expats locals visitors and families.
Very calm place ...no sports activities allowed.  Good place to spend some solitude  time.  McD is there if you are hungry.  Weekend just nice to be here..

Jurong Green Community Club situated along Jurong West Ave 1.
It houses fast food restaurants, enrichment centre, basketball court and many more.

Last update/ review Feb2017

A vibrant community centre in Jurong East. Lots of activities. The MP gives out study award, scholarships yearly here. Collection of masks, trace together, COVID19 vaccination etc are done here. It has a big air conditioned multi purpose hall that can be used for badminton, indoor basket ball court, MacDonalds and Dominos Pizza.
The car park gantry is always having problem to exit. Can Wilson carpark do something about it?
big swimming pools

A really nice building with fantastic views over Jurong Lake. The architecture stands out beautifully, especially in the evening, and it’s a great place to pause and enjoy the surroundings even if you’re just passing by on a walk

What I really like is the range of community activities offered here — from water sports to programmes that bring people together. It adds a lot of life and purpose to the lakeside area and makes the gardens feel more vibrant and inclusive
Good place to run and walk. Recommend time morning and before evening

I recently visited Passion Wave at Jurong Lake Gardens and had an amazing experience. Nestled by the lake, this waterfront facility offers various activities like kayaking and pedal boating, perfect for both families and beginners. I opted for a double kayak, and the staff were incredibly friendly and provided a detailed safety briefing, ensuring I felt comfortable before heading out onto the water.

What sets Passion Wave apart is its eco-conscious focus. I joined a kayak-based cleanup activity, which was not only fun but eye-opening. Paddling through the lake while picking up litter felt like a purposeful adventure. Despite the lake looking clean at first glance, there was a surprising amount of trash hidden along the shore. It was fulfilling to be part of a group effort to keep the environment clean while enjoying the peaceful surroundings of Jurong Lake.

The location is also family-friendly, with amenities like diaper-changing rooms and a nearby children's playground. Passion Wave at Jurong Lake Gardens is more than just a spot for water sports; it’s a place to connect with nature and contribute to its preservation. I highly recommend it for anyone looking for a meaningful and fun outdoor activity.
A nice spacious compound with meeting and training rooms available! Can serve as a resting spot or dance platform given the space it offers! It has really nice sceneries too!

Nice facilities for outdoor family activities!
Friendly staff and services.
The whole area is spacious and nice to walk around (not much shelter, if it rains though)

Outstanding Care and Guidance at Lakeside Student Care (Jurong East Branch)

We are writing this testimonial to express our deepest gratitude and heartfelt appreciation for the wonderful team at Lakeside Student Care (Jurong East Branch), especially the amazing teachers—Ms. Faith, Ms. Suay Hong, Mr. Ritchie, and Mr. Elvis. Their dedication, professionalism, and genuine care have made a significant positive impact on my son’s growth and well-being.

We would like to extend a special thank you to Ms. Faith for her exemplary professionalism in handling a situation involving my son’s misplaced pencil. She turned a simple incident into a valuable learning experience for all the children. Instead of just resolving the matter, she used it as an opportunity to educate the students about responsibility, understanding, and respect for each other’s belongings. Her thoughtful approach impressed us deeply as it reflects the values ​​we want our son to learn, and we are incredibly thankful for her guidance.

A heartfelt thanks also goes to Ms. Suay Hong for her extraordinary thoughtfulness and kindness. During an outing event, my son, who does not want to eat fast food on that day, faced a challenge with the planned meal. Without hesitation, Ms. Suay Hong went out of her way to bring him to another shop to allow him to buy a meal. This act of going the extra mile touched us profoundly. It speaks volumes about her caring nature and genuine commitment to ensuring every child feels valued and included.

Furthermore, we want to acknowledge Mr. Ritchie and Mr. Elvis for their consistent encouragement and dedication in mentoring and caring for our son. Their positive energy and supportive approach have greatly contributed to his development and confidence.

As parents, we entrust the Lakeside Student Care team with the responsibility of nurturing our child, and they have exceeded our expectations time and time again. The patience, care, and personalized attention that each teacher provides have made this center a safe and enriching environment for my son to grow academically, socially, and emotionally.

Thank you once again to the entire team at Lakeside Student Care (Jurong East Branch). Your kindness and commitment do not go Unnoticed, and we are truly grateful for the positive impact you have had on our family.

Warm regards,

Mr. & Mrs. Lee
