Big building with lots of space including many EV charging lots (Volt).  Directly connected to JEM mall with comparatively cheap parking.

Honestly, Level 2 is where I get most of my work done as an entrepreneur seeking refuge from home procrastination. The outdoor seating has its benefits, as some use it to rest and others rush urgent documents.

The nearby food options are convenient, though I would like to see more variety and perhaps additional stalls. This place has strong potential to become a business-centric hub where colleagues and professionals can connect and collaborate.

Building with new entertainment coming up.
Badminton, gym, gymnastics, food court, sushuiro, 24hr entertainment center.

Asked the security to remove this car for EV lot. Security was quite knowledgeable but driver could not be found on 22 Mar 2026….
Update: wow 2 cars in a row

Perennial Business City as most Singaporeans would have recognised it, is a former Big Box Outlet Mall that has been reconfigured as an MICE convention centre for exhibitions, conventions and meetings along with a DHL Singapore outlet that monitors the cargo movements within this western region. There is ample space for relaxation and a nice view of Jurong Town Hall's neighbourhood.
1 tutor with 10 students in 1.5 hour with each student getting an average 9 mins of time works?
Elite helps me to improve my learning skills

The Trinity Math PSLE bootcamp was simply amazing!
The bootcamp was really engaging, and it helped me build a good sense of time in exams as he timed us for questions and lets us know our timing. It is very helpful and I know how to better manage time in exams and also , I learnt new concepts. This bootcamp was very fun and I strongly recommend you to attend next year's bootcamp if you are taking PSLE next year. This bootcamp is not like regular classes and the explanations are clearer and more detailed. You might even learn new concepts!
(For those people who love eating out there, it's worth mentioning that you will be very well-fed both in knowledge and literal food)

Teacher benjamin's lessons are not just boring but it is fun and engaging. He fins ways to make lesson interesting. During the boot camp, I strugled with most of the question but after he went through, I felt the question was like a primary one question even though they were primary 6. He also has live sessions on sundsy and they are very helpful

I think Mr Low's explanations are really easy to understand and when I dont understand a question he explains in a way which is a another way to solve the word problem. For example: when I ask him how to do a specific question , he slowly explains it. I have been his student since p3 and he has been helping me understand questions. I recommend his learning centre as it will help a lot for students struggling. :)

This is Yu Tong and Yu De
1. Mr Low methods are easy to understand and gives us hard questions to brainstorm.
2. He is also very funny when he teaches us and makes sure we understands.
3. He also traces our exam points and teaches our weak points.

The teacher Mr Low is very patient and explains challenging topics to the class in a fun and enjoyable way to understand. He takes effort and makes sure that all of his students are thorough with topics. He gives us extra practices to prepare us well for our tests

Lakeside MRT Station is clean, well-maintained, and convenient for daily commuters. The station provides easy access to nearby residential areas, schools, and Jurong Lake Gardens. Trains are frequent, and the station is generally well organized.
The sheltered walkways and clear signage make traveling comfortable and convenient. A reliable MRT station for both residents and visitors.
🚆🇸🇬

I am reluctant to leave a negative review, but the service I received at the Passenger Service Centre (EZ-Link) was unacceptable. Both staff members on duty—a Chinese male and a Malay male—were dismissive and spoke in an aggressive, angry tone. Their half-hearted approach to assisting commuters is a poor reflection of the service line, and I hope management addresses this lack of professionalism. Negative star
Crowed MRT Station. I visited Jurong Lake Park via this station as Chinese garden was closed. There are some stores and food stall around the station, good point!
Not only has its bean curd price increased, the quantity has also reduced. So upset.
Great station with plenty of good food options to grab and go. Has a SAM machine, post box, POSB and OCBC ATMS.
You can use Chinese Garden MRT to reach here. This place is so relaxing and very comfartable to go walking, jogging and even just sitting at the bench.
During the week the gardens are very quiet.  The weekends it becomes much busier.  Weekends are great for extroverted folks week days are great for introverts.
Chinese Garden was beautiful.  A lovely location for a picnic & evening stroll
It’s good place to take snap photo.
Just across the japanese garden. Everything in this place is amazing! You can also enter the Live Turtle Tortoise museum. Oh, and the roaming lizard. :-)

another one of them huge long bus stops to cater to the to and fro passengers from the train station and across. will always be crowded at most times, but it is amazingly always kept pretty clean. has pretty decent amount of seating too should you need to sit while waiting for your bus,

They always have offers,friendly staff and over variety of cakes and pastries.....however,lately I can see them selling food which is cold and tastes stale..perhaps it could be due to less sales but selling old food is also not good for customers health.. business ethics are important too
Take bus 240 or 246 to go to Boon Lay Shopping centre
Old MRT Station needs to refurbish and expand the capacity yo house more passengers during peak hours.
Typical MRT station in the West
It needs to see the whole road side as vehicle is blocking while searching for pick up drop off point
Construction still ongoing ,sheltered pathway on its way...not sure when the wait will be over.
Beautiful greeny grass with healthy trees
A big shelter bus stop for commuters
Extremely busy during peak hours!

We were nearby after our visit to Lake Garden, so we came here for breakfast. The place is easy to find, with a large parking area surrounding the food court. It’s located on the second floor, while the wet market is on the ground floor.

The food court is neat and clean, and although it was quite crowded, it didn’t feel too packed and the ventilation was decent. There is a wide variety of food stalls to choose from, and the prices are very reasonable. Surprisingly, my meal cost only $3!

Overall, it’s a nice neighbourhood food court with good food, affordable prices, and a pleasant dining experience. Would definitely come back again.

Been eating there since 2025.

My favourites are:

beef noodle soup (02–72),
western stall (03-171)
Cai fan with porridge (02-80)
Lei Cha (03-120)

More than half the stalls will close after 2pm.

Ample wall fans around. Careful where u sit cuz some tables are smokers’ area.

The cleaning staff are so hardworking and busy. Really appreciate them!

For Taman Jurong Market and Food Centre, its ground floor is a busy wet market, while the second and third levels are dedicated to food stalls. Taman Jurong Shopping Centre is right next to the market and food centre. In fact, they are separated only by a small road (Yung Sheng Road) and are less than a minute's walk apart.
Btw, my favorite eating stalls are 58 minced meat noodle, Ying Ji Wanton Mee, Xin Kee Pig’s Organ Soup and, coffee in most stalls there are nice too.

"A multi-story hawker paradise in the West! If you’re coming here, you HAVE to queue for Feng Zhen Lor Mee (#03-146)—the gravy is thick, flavorful, and those handmade meatballs are life-changing.

If you’re not in the mood for noodles, Tien Lai Rice Stall (#02-66) has some of the best Char Siew and Roast Duck in Jurong (go early, they sell out fast!). For something unique, try ButterNut (#02-108) for their fusion pizzas.

Pro-tip: The center is circular, so it can be easy to lose your bearings. Use the central courtyard as your landmark. Plenty of parking and very breezy on the upper floors!"

This hawker centre has two storeys. There are quite a number of good food choices. Place was quite clean too. Interestingly, there is a smoking area. This site apparently is the location of Singapore’s first hawker centre, and is part of the Jurong Heritage Trail.

I need to repeat myself 3 times to the cashier that my Laksa order is take away. I had to wait for 5 minutes standing that they are not attending my order until I buy coffee then my coffee comes out first then I had to repeat myself again that my laksa is for take away for 2 people inside (1 guy, 1 auntie) but they still serve my food for dine in. Even if they transfer it to a take away box, the food cannot even fit, cannot even close the take away box. HOW CAN YOU EAT LAKSA WITHOUT BROTH???

Bare in mind this location all people here mostly working office hours, how?

All the staff not friendly. Somemore cannot take a special instruction.

Kinda pricey here. The caifan store is interesting, sells in modular dishes, like Korean style. Good thing is free flow of soup and rice. But if u take too many dishes, gonna be expensive caifan. Good thing is everything is pre portion out so u get what you pay. Another dish I tried was the Malay nasi lemak store. Almost charged me for 2 mee siam, quite spicy. The fish is a rip off, all flour and no meat. Nasi lemak was 12 dollars and caifan 6 dollars , ask for merchant discounts if possible

Is this how you pack your food? I can understand the poor service from the male staff, but the aunties are always very nice. Did I owe this guy money? And then he packed my food like this? It's disgusting to look at; how am I supposed to eat this? I frequent this koufu place! This is the first time I've encountered something like this!

I had the nasi ambeng set from the Malay nasi padang store. The portion was big, the food was quite tasty and the price reasonable. The inside of Koufu can be quite tight and the ventilation not so great. But now we have the option of sitting outside. There are a good number of tables and fans overhead. The view is great, you get to see the beautiful Ng Teng Fong General Hospital and a chance to get out of the office air con. After your meal you can go to the Glyph Supply Co. next to Kou Fu for good coffee.

⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐
Saba-licious Surprise at Perennial Business City Koufu!

Dropped by the Japanese-Korean stall at Koufu inside Perennial Business City and got hit with a wave of delicious nostalgia—and a bit of hot plate drama! Ordered the not-your-average Saba fish set, which came out sizzling like it had a story to tell. Pro tip: take your photo fast, because if you spend too long trying to get that perfect foodie shot, the fish might level up from “crispy” to “smoked art installation.”

Thankfully, I managed to strike a delicate balance between camera angles and not setting off the fire alarm. The fish was still juicy inside, packed with flavour, and paired beautifully with rice and a K-drama-worthy kimchi. 10/10 would risk mild overfry again.

#SabaSaga #HotPlateAdventures #KoufuCravings #FoodieFailSuccess
Nothing beats a good cup of smooth Matcha Latte in the morning. Pleasantly surprised to see a cute little bear waving at me halfway through my drink while sipping. What a lovely start to the day!

One of the most unwanted i felt at a coffee place.

🪑 stepped in and wasn’t greeted nor acknowledged for the better part of 3 minutes whilst we decide if we want to sit on the inside separately (since there’s no seats for 2), or to take it to Koufu where there’s ample of seats

☕ ordered our usual iced and hot white, and saw the staff stir the iced white in front of us, using my straw.. Taste wise, it was decent on the first sip but quickly became very diluted, likely due to the amount of ice used - it also wasn’t a full cup for the iced drink which made it look very petty (or maybe i’m just bias now). The hot drink was largely bitter without fragrance too, probably not the beans that we like in general.

Whilst i’m not looking to be greeted upon entry, we both felt there could be some sort of interaction to let us know there’s no tables for now, and we would be very happy to wait or again decide to sit outside - but the lack of interaction even upon ordering and enquiring if we are dining in makes the entire experience very poor. I can’t recommend this place for sure, though i see that it could have been a better experience.

Was at their somerset outlet previously before becoming a regular here at their new location. I like that there are various seatings to choose from. 2 seats outdoors, bar or bench seats indoors.

Most people get their drinks/ice creams to go. There are usually seats available throughout the day on both weekdays and weekends.

I've tried most of their drinks from filter coffees to espresso to matcha lattes. Consistently delicious.

Delicious Sandwiches & Peach Sencha Cold Brewed Tea is nice too.
Matcha Latte is good too, not much sugar !! Healthy choice.
Rum & Raisin Ice Cream very delicious too !!!

Located within Perennial Business City, take the green or red MRT line and alight at Jurong East Interchange. Exit towards JEM and you will be on the 2nd floor, walk towards DonDonki to cross a bridge to PBC, travel straight towards Kopitiam and they are right beside it.

This clean, aesthetical cafe is a great place for coffee. Staff here are friendly and attentive. They have indoor and outdoor seating arrangements. I would highly recommend to have your break here and enjoy your cup of coffee. Their different flavored cold brews are a must try as well, their menu do serve sandwiches and gelato.

Prices here are affordable and do visit them when you're in the neighborhood to help keep them around. : )

Rating highly for service because I spilled half a cup of Milo dinosaur all over myself, and the polite uncle who previously served me was super nice about it and cleaned up my seating area and refilled my cup for free while I was cleaning up in the bathroom. Really appreciate it!! 😊😊

Portions are quite small, but very reasonable for the prices of the food :)

Neighborhood mamak shop that is popular among the locals in Jurong Lake and Jurong Spring areas, regardless of ethnicities. Tend to be pretty crowded during breakfast time. The shop is run by a funny uncle who often jokingly multiplies the price of the dishes by a factor of 1000 (lol).

It has an extensive menu, ranging from standard Tamil trade like dosa, sambar, roti paratha, fried briyani, Chicken 65, and poriyal, to Malay gravy (gulai), nasi goreng, maggi noodle, and cakes (kuih). The price tend to be fair and of standard mamak shop. The dishes taste alright, but does the job done.

Very disappointing experience.
There is a lady named Nadia (a Muslim woman) who behaves very rudely. She seems to make up her own prices when selling items, which is confusing and unfair. If that’s the case, then why display a menu at all? It’s misleading to show a menu if the prices are not followed.

She talks only about money and acts like the boss, even though she’s just a staff member. This kind of behaviour creates a very unpleasant atmosphere.

I strongly suggest avoiding this place. There are many better places to eat with more honest and respectful service.

Service is totally 0

Price: $3.60 (Plain Thosai Set)

Plain Thosai: 3.5/5
Crispy edges, but slightly missing that sour flavour.

Sambar: 4.5/5
Very flavourful, thick and not salty. Slight nutty flavour. The sambal in it is really spicy.

Chutney: 4/5
Red chutney is very flavourful with a hint of ginger.

Total score: 12/15

(Beware that the fried rice (or nasi goreng) by default would be spicy)

I like the mutton coin prata here. The plain coin prata also tastes good, it is like a smaller but more crispier prata with caramel (from sugar).

From a frequent visitor of Teban Gardens Sheng Siong,
1. Because we used to not be very clear with where things were placed, we asked the staff at Teban Gardens Sheng Siong, and even thought they were busy their own errands, they stopped to help us find it happily and did not seem to be bothered with our request. I find that very commendable.
2. When we are buying pork especially, we always ask extra questions, and he never seemed to be irritated by it, and always introduce more suitable meats for us to choose from.
3. Always when we are paying, the staff near the payment counters always ask if we need help in a friendly and considerate manner. If there is any problems with payment, they will be quick to lend a helping hand to assist us without seeming to be annoyed.
4. They are very good at advertising us more products that are on offer.
5. I called the Sheng Siong a couple of times to find the milo plushie, and they were very friendly towards my consistent calling.

I am happy to write that it has been a great experience for me and my aunt to visit daily, and they are very helpful no matter the circumstances.
Reasonably sized supermarket and decently stocked
They give us best offer.

The staffs are diligent workers. They care for their jobs. Most are foreigners.
The shop is well replenished. I noticed that the team will quickly
maintain stock control.
The shop staffs is friendly and allert. The supervisor is a team player. It is convenient and near for us Tebanians shopping there.
Keep up the excellent performance team Sheng Shiong.

Offering great Asian foods and spices with the occasional discounts for promotional items. Additional discounts and PAssion points. POSB Everyday Card enjoy additional discounts. Popular supermarket for all. Quick checkout with cash or credit/debit cards. Wonderful service, happy staff at helm to assist. A good neighbourhood supermarket you can trust.

well stocked withcgreat produce and daily essential wode aisle, brightly lighted. very easy to shop st this supermarket. we got what we want. bought some prawns, vegetable, milk, sweets and milo. check out was a breeze with many self checkout counters. nice shopping experience.

Sheng Siong is definitely my go to supermarket as most of their items are reasonably priced and are in my opinion cheaper than FairPrice. Vegetables and home brand ready to eat products are affordable and tasty too! Staff there also very happy and friendly to customers.

Their cashiers have dropped the ball.
Poor packing, "forgot" to remind shoppers of Chas discount, stuffing every single thing into ONE bag to the point that carrying it is ridiculously stupid, even after clearly telling them to use as many as necessary.
This outlet had 5 stars staff.
But currently they're not.

I am writing to express my profound appreciation and heartfelt commendation for one of your exceptional team members, Ah Long, whose outstanding service on Friday, 30 January 2026, left an indelible impression on our entire organization.

On that afternoon, our team visited your Jurong Superbowl outlet to purchase supplies for our upcoming community outreach event—8 cartons of mandarin oranges and 2 cartons of mineral water. What began as a routine shopping trip transformed into an extraordinary demonstration of customer service excellence that deserves the highest recognition.

Ah Long's dedication went far beyond the call of duty. With meticulous care and genuine concern for quality, he personally opened and inspected every single carton of mandarin oranges, ensuring that each piece met the highest standards. When he discovered items that fell short, he promptly arranged replacements without hesitation. This level of attention to detail speaks volumes about his commitment to customer satisfaction.

But his exceptional service did not stop there. Understanding the weight and volume of our purchases, Ah Long took the initiative to load everything onto a push cart. Then, in an act of kindness that truly moved us, he personally pushed the cart across the road to our office building and helped us transport everything up to the 3rd floor—all with a cheerful and willing spirit.

Perhaps what impressed us most was Ah Long's unwavering integrity. When my colleague attempted to offer him a monetary tip in appreciation for his extraordinary efforts, he politely but firmly declined. He even refused a simple drink of water, respectfully explaining that Sheng Siong's service policy guides his actions. This display of professionalism and principle is genuinely rare and deeply admirable.

Ah Long embodies the very best of what customer service should be: attentive, proactive, generous, and principled. His exceptional magnanimity and genuine desire to serve reflect not only his personal character but also the outstanding training and values that Sheng Siong instills in its team members.

Thank you for cultivating such remarkable talent. Your commitment to service excellence through team members like Ah Long sets a gold standard for the retail industry.

Large supermarket with fresh produces to electrical stuff such as fans etc. Live fishes available with shell sea food such as venus clams, lobsters, crabs. In the morning, butcher will be around to cut up freshly delivered pork. Attached side is a air conditioned food court with outdoor seats available. Place is wheelchair accessible with public toilets. No WiFi coverage. Building itself is rather old, indoor condition looked good.

People can find over local products as well as from India.. a wide variety of vegetables and groceries available all the time with reasonable price.

Some of the goods really lesser price than supermarkets.

We are going there to buy greens, veggie(fresh) and some seasonal products.
Free Home Delivery at customers convenient time. Prompt response for stocks and availability of groceries when check via whatsapp. Overall Good service! Can recommend to others

Friendly and kind approach, Fresh spinach’s especially Methi, Palak and others as well.
Easy to identify variety of grocery & organic groceries.
Compare to other Gracery shop, Free Home delivery, items all very cheap, vegetables very fresh all the time. Very friendly and polite Shop Owner. Worth it.
All products r ultimate especially veggies r fresh

Located downstairs in the apartment building, this is a very convenient convenience store. It has a wide variety of Chinese snacks and offers regular online group-buying deals every week. It easily meets my daily needs. 👍

If u see something fall in the store even it was happened near to you, think twice before telling the staff cuz u may end up being blamed for it 🙂 Want checking the CCTV to clarify what actually happened was impossible. The apology was only verbal, but payment was still required.

It was only one soya milk so no one will try to avoid responsibility okay? I only wanted to know whether the item actually fell because of me. Yet from the store staff to the follow-up customer service, no one was willing to provide the CCTV footage or seriously help clarify the facts.

Honestly, who cares about a 1 or 2 dollar refund? That was never the issue. I only wanted to understand what actually happened but apparently even that was too ambitious. The attitude was, of course, very sincere 🙂 verbally. The actions were arrogant, the customer service was nowhere to be found, and the whole experience was a wonderful reminder that some big stores are very good at making customers feel very small haha excellent

10 June 2026 - Extremely rude Muslim lady cashier. None of the item prices was listed on the shelf, and when I realised something was way more expensive than I thought, I asked to have it removed. When I was about to go put it back, she scolded me rudely about not checking the prices with her. Who would have thought that a bag of potato chips would cost 11 dollars? Am I supposed to ask the price of each and every single item at the cashier before buying it?

Whoever is in charge of this store, please teach your staff to be respectful and have actual manners instead of being uncouth. I understand I should have double-checked the price, but I don't see the reason to be scolded for it, even after apologising. She could only keep quiet and look smug after I had had enough and told her to learn some manners first.

3 February 2026 at approximately 11:24 PM.
While I was preparing my chicken breast, I had not yet opened the packaging, which accidentally caused a loud sound. The Indian female staff on duty reacted by scolding me loudly in an extremely rude and disrespectful manner.

27/2 at around 0630hrs, I wanted to pay for my food using cash however the Indian lady staff refuses cash payment and only allow paywave. When questioned about why, rudely responded by saying she is closing counter so not accepting cash. The other counter also not opened.

Amazing staff, I wish all 7elevens had such a great team. Always welcoming, very friendly, very professional! Do NOT listen to bad reviews, I literally go there everyday and all the ladies are really amazing. Definitely one of the friendliest 7eleven team I have met so far!

place_name: Extreme Fitness - Personal Training Jurong East
review_text:
My sincere review here with extreme fitness.

Coach Zany has been very patient and friendly to my fitness needs and conditions. Appreciate every push and accountability even outside of my sessions, so to help me reach goals.

The environment has been open and inviting. Hope they some equipments and facilities can get and upgrade.

place_name: Extreme Fitness - Personal Training Jurong East
review_text: If I could give Kamila 15 stars I would. I was so desperatly in need of help as a mother of two small children, overweight and tired... and since she specializes in postpartum recovery, I decided to give it a try. Wow.. simply wow!! Not only I went from 72kg to 55kg but I also regained my confidence and my laughter. She listened well, she supported me when I had bad days, answered all my questions and gave me all the tips and tricks to help me get the best out of my exercise routine. I look better and I feel like a million bucks and through her advice, I completely changed my approach to nutrition and weight management. I would totally recommend her to anyone who is looking for a trainer, this is money well spent.

place_name: Extreme Fitness - Personal Training Jurong East
review_text: My training experience here has been great so far and my coach has been really encouraging and motivating. I was really intimidated initially but feel more comfortable coming to the gym now :) Initially I knew nothing about fitness and gymming, but now I have become stronger and know more about various exercises. The training here is really tailored to each person’s strengths and good for beginners!  Many many many thanks to my trainer Coach Zany and also Coach Shawn :) everyone here has been really friendly and patient with me.

place_name: Extreme Fitness - Personal Training Jurong East
review_text: I've been with Extreme Fitness for about 4 months. I have achieved a body fat loss of over 9% through their program. The gym has also better facilities than the ones out that. Coach Didi and Coach Shawn's programs are really effective in helping me achieve my goals. I highly highly highly recommend Extreme Fitness to anyone who wants to get fit and ripped.

place_name: Extreme Fitness - Personal Training Jurong East
review_text:
Extreme Fitness Family
​It’s a super friendly environment with very nice and knowledgeable coaches! From the moment you walk in, it feels like stepping into one big family—everyone is non-judgmental and incredibly helpful. 🤝
​The gym itself is cozy and well-maintained, plus having access to the pool is a huge bonus! 🥳
​If you’re looking for real strength training, expert guidance, and the motivation to actually hit your fitness goals, this is the place to be. I’ve seen so many positive changes in myself already! 🥳
​Thank you to the whole team for the amazing training and support. Keep up the great work guys! 💪🔥

I conduct personal training sessions here for my clients and I love how clean, bright and spacious the gym is. It’s well-equipped, well-maintained and never overcrowded, thanks to the 20-pax cap per timeslot. Booking through the Dungeon app is smooth and hassle-free. The team is also friendly and always ready to help. Highly recommend this space for anyone looking for a quality workout environment!

I have conducted several personal training session here for all ages from teenagers, working adults to elderly for sports strength and conditoning or weighloss.

I really love the equipment provided by the Gym. The quality and variety of it over there. Shower and toilet facility is outside with a walking distance but it good enough to clean up after training.

Love the private training time there so far and wish there will be such quality fitness Gym more around Singapore.

If you are looking for a private space to train for any strength and conditioning sports, hydrox, crossfit or conduct personal training session, I would definitely recommend such space.

excellent service recovery! thank you to the kind staff who bothered to fix a problem on a public holiday!

also, the equipment is not bad. the less common equipment include mag grips, pendulum squat machines, skiergs (like 2 ski ergs!).
It is hard to find a gym with a trap bar, safety bar, space for plyometrics and every cable you can possibly need! An athlete’s haven, highly recommend!

This Gym is great for athletes looking to improve their sporting performance. They have many equipment not found in large commercial and 24/7 gyms. Especially love the Eleiko weights, multi hips and the glutes ham extension machines. Super value for money as its largely quiet. They also have a proper indoor running track. Amazing place.

Pretty spacious park, ample space to walk/jog/cycle/rollerblade! Have fields and spaces around to picnic or run and play.

Can consider coming by on a hot day when aircon is preferred to avoid crowds and enjoy the lake view and greenery.
Pool is small but clean. Very few people using the pool during weekdays. Good place for a quiet swim. Weekends is crowded due to children's swimming classes.

Lovely place to go for evening strolls, runs, biking, social family outings.
Well maintained and beautifully landscaped.
Also have a designated place for pets to roam leash free.
A perfect public pool! Found this rare gem in Jurong Lake Garden on a Sunday—and it wasn't even crowded! The water was clean and the facility is very well-maintained. An absolute delight!

Great place to exercise as there are public exercising equipments for all to use and a swimming pool to enjoy on a sunny day. There are table tennis tables for those who love playing table tennis. It’s indoors so everyone can play, rain or shine!
Don’t have rackets? You can get them at the  ActiveSG Office beside the pool.

Overall, The park is a great place to connect with friends and family!

Professional warm services for IMAX cinemas with 3D and realistic sound effects locates in neighborhood Hartland mall next to MRT station and bus interchange. Plenty of local delights and Western classics shopping banking library portage community club supermarket entertainments and amenities nearby. Good alternative to the crowned cinemas in the city. Free wifi. Friendly fast attentive cosy comfy welcoming ambience for romantic intimates groups students tourists Expats locals visitors and families.

The Experience of watching Movie in Shaw IMAX is an Amazing feel ☺️
Very Good Ambience and Really a Big Screen 😮
Sound Quality is Best Best Best 👍
Seat n Spacing are better
Waiting for Avatar 2 🥳

Even though it has less number of rows, the screen is big enough to experience the IMAX. The sounds and visuals are awesome. The air-conditioning was heavy and I could see many people came with jackets.

I watched a few movies there and the price is cheap for students. Only $7. The location is convenient as it is in a mall with many restaurants. The staff are nice and helpful as well. Sad that the mall, as well as the cinema, is closing soon.
Love how it's usually quieter here compared to other theatres in town, and how Westies can watch IMAX movie without traveling far. Seats are also comfortable in the IMAX theatre.
Great initiative to host a National Day celebration to bring together the whole estate - with food vouchers that helps to bring about business in the hawker centre! 🇸🇬

I attend the event for EZlink card presentation.  We were told to proceed to the buffet after taking our photo. I waited like fool when all the people at my rows and people behind me was given theirs.
I approach one of the staff, he assist to check for me. He came back to inform me that that photos was passed downstairs.
It's odd that 2 staff can distribute the photos and evepass to the lady beside me, accept me????

Great facilities and service from the staff here. I also like that they have a book exchange corner for residents to take and give away books.

I took the 1st dose of Pfizer Covid 19 vaccine here on 23 Jun 2021 through Parkway Shenton. it was smooth and orderly and well organized. The air conditioned badminton court was used as the vaccination centre.
Screened ,Vaccinated and discharged free gift from MOH 2nd on 05 June 2021
Clean, tidy and spacious. Many interesting course for adults and children.

Forest Ramble is a fantastic children’s play wonderland nestled in nature. The themed play zones — Monkey Play, Squirrel Play, and Snake Play — are creatively designed and encourage lots of climbing, balancing, and imaginative play. It feels adventurous yet thoughtfully integrated into the surrounding greenery

The playground is well laid out with different zones catering to kids of various ages, making it easy for children to explore while adults supervise comfortably. Do note that some sections may be under maintenance at times, but there’s still plenty to keep kids entertained
A beautiful relaxing park and with nice playground. Except that some parts are closed for maintenance since 7th July 2025. That’s quite a long time for maintenance….

One of the most exciting children outdoor playground.
Plenty of different play stations.
Swings, spring jumps, slides, flying fox, mini trampoline, adventure bridges, rope runs, see saws and many many more.
Exciting moments for kids age 5 to 12 years old and 2 to 5 yrs old with adult supervision.
Shaded area, water cooler, drinks vending machine and toilets are available.
Opening hrs:
8:00am - 10:00pm,
Closed on Mon (except for PH) and Tue if preceding Mon is a PH

This was a great place to bring my nephews and cousins. The playground is spacious, fun, and has a lot of different areas to explore, so it kept everyone entertained. It’s well designed, safe, and a really nice outdoor spot for families to spend time together.

Very nice playground with lot of play setup for kids from swings to slides. Jumping climbing everything was there in the playground. On Rainy days play equipments are not allowed to use until dry. Totally recommended

A fantastic nature-themed play area that kids absolutely love. The Heron, Butterfly, and Frog play zones are thoughtfully designed and blend beautifully with the surrounding greenery, encouraging imaginative and active play in a natural setting

The layout is spacious, well maintained, and suitable for different ages, with plenty of room for children to explore while adults can comfortably supervise nearby. It’s especially nice in the early evening when the area is cooler and gently lit
Very good for kids and families
Looks very fun to play in, too bad I can't fit inside.
Very good 👍 place for play your kids
Kids love it in the evening, brightly light.

A huge open green space that’s very versatile and well maintained. It’s often used for outdoor exhibits, community events, and large-scale activities, but on regular days you’ll also see people picnicking, flying kites, or just relaxing on the grass

The openness is its biggest draw—plenty of room to spread out without feeling crowded. While it’s fairly simple in design, it works well as a flexible gathering space within Jurong Lake Gardens

Lakeside Field is a grand lawn located across the Entrance Pavilion.
Lay a mat on the ground for picnic in the evening or have some fun with a frisbee.
It was the venue for the Lights by the Lake 2023, the annual lantern light up event.
The open large lawn is good for organising carnivals, corporate family day and sports day.
Lakeside Field is also available for rent from NParks.

Update:
This year's Mid-Autumn Festival @Jurong Lake Gardens 2024-Lights by the Lake is also held at Lakeside Field.
🌠The Garden was teeming with visitors during the Mid-Autumn nights
🐉Many charming and beautiful brightly lit Chinese forklore themed gigantic lanterns were displayed.
These lantern displays included:
-白蛇传 The Legend of the White Snake
-哪吒闹海 Nezha Conquers the Dragon King
-嫦娥奔月 Chang'e flies to the moon

Located within the scenic Jurong Lake Gardens, Lakeside Field offers a peaceful escape with its vast open lawns and beautifully maintained green spaces. It’s the perfect spot for recreational activities, whether you're gathering with friends, flying kites, or just enjoying a picnic by the lake. The pathways around the field are well-maintained, making them ideal for a leisurely stroll, jog, or cycle through the serene landscape.

Lakeside Field's proximity to other attractions like the Forest Ramble and Rasau Walk adds to its appeal, offering a seamless experience for visitors exploring the rest of Jurong Lake Gardens. The space also frequently hosts community events and festivals. I recently visited during the Mid-Autumn Festival Lights by the Lake event and thoroughly enjoyed the enchanting light displays, which added a festive charm to the tranquil environment. It’s a must-visit for anyone looking for a blend of nature, recreation, and cultural activities.

Went for the Lights by the Lake 2023 experience, and the setup was really lovely.  The colour scheme was beautiful and the exhibits were generally tasteful designed.  Perhaps go during a weeknight since there are many people

Nice place for football, drone flying, rc car , picnics and much more. With the sports centre nearby,drinks are available. Quite a nice spot nearby for some entertainment …. Until some noisy rowdy kids come and interrupts the peace.
Spacious floor good for toddler and family to avoid crowd and let toddler to have big space to explore. Also have many foods and drinks
Feiyue managed. Poor communication and experience with staff and counselors. Gave feedback but no one responded or followed up.
Started in 2000 with an After School Care, Lakeside Family Centre (JE) serves the vulnerable and at-risk families with emerging to high complexity of needs.

been studying at TLC for 5/6 years? My older brother also took lessons there for maybe 4 years? I had a great experience there, teachers were lovely and I managed to pull my math up from AL4/5 - AL1/2 🤭 I also took the English writing classes, and scored 33/36 for prelims this year !! Would 100% recommend , the teachers are so nice and it really helped my grades too! I noticed that my plots started to get more creative and that I could grasp certain concepts for math better. All in all, 5 stars!!

Since my child began attending the Creative Writing and English Comprehension classes at TLC, we have observed a marked improvement in his English composition skills. Previously, his teacher often remarked that his writing resembled that of a very young child; however, his work is now more structured, expressive, and age-appropriate.
We would also like to express our appreciation for Ms Khoo’s patience and thoughtful guidance. As my son is a very introverted child who seldom voices his questions, her gentle and encouraging approach has made a significant positive impact on his learning experience.
We sincerely appreciate the dedicated support provided by Ms Khoo.

My child has been attending Ms Lee’s Math classes at TLC for about a year, and we’ve really seen him improve, especially in Paper 1. He’s quite introverted and often shy about asking questions, but Ms Lee’s patience and encouragement have made a huge difference. She has helped him understand the concepts better and approach math problems with more confidence. We really appreciate all her guidance!

My daughter took English Writing Class with Ms Khoo and Science lessons with Ms Tan since January 2025. Ms Khoo ensured that my daughter was able to catch up with the P6 class and recommended that she joined P5 class to build her foundation in writing for 1st semester. It really helped my daughter to gradually pick up good phrases and to write better plots. The practice papers indeed helped with her prelim and PSLE. As for Science, Ms Tan has taught my daughter on the techniques to use for Section B which she was clueless on attempting the questions. Also knowing her weakness on certain topics, Ms Tan arranged for extra zoom sessions to help and guide my daughter. She has managed to pass her Science from AL7 to AL6. It is a significant improvement for my daughter as she failed Science most time since P5. We are very grateful to Ms Khoo and Ms Tan for their patience and guidance in the short 10 months for such a vast improvement.

I joined TLC when I was P5 and was here learning creative writing until P6 PSLE. Although I joined late, the teacher was very patient and helped me improve my writing by leaps and bounds. This writing class thought me many good phrases to use in composition and also allowed me to find out my mistakes and improve myself. Would definitely recommend this tuition centre, no matter what subject, to everyone!

Western cuisine.
Chicken chop and pork chop were of huge serving! One of the larger serving we had so far 😁 And boy, it was tasty with the black pepper sauce!
It came with sides, fries, mash potatoes, corn kernels and lettuce.

Foodcourt management can look into the hygiene of the place though, we were battling with lots of houseflies while eating.

Sept 25, 20. Try the Ban Mian & fish soup cost about $6.50 with rice, plenty of bitter gourd, tomatoes and plenty of fish. Rice were large and very satisfied. Beside also has Teochew porridge, grill & bbq, stewed soup, chicken rice, Muslim food & Thai food. Can come for lunch .
Plenty of stalls with variety over here. Lunch hour often packed with people and difficult to get a seat. Newly open la Mian xiao long Bao stall with generous portion and great noodle and dumplings.

The chicken rice, roasted delight stall used to be ok. Recently, the quality of food became worse. The stall keeper give you char siew and roasted pork that are full of FATs. Sauce anyhow pour onto the rice. It was so bad that I have to slice the edible meat out of the fatty meat.

I think the soup here is the best and famous one. I usually will order their chicken soup and olive rice. Yesterday we ate the seafood stall and I would say their food is so so and not really nice. It's more on the salty side. Egg and pork both salty. And hygiene wise, they dont wipe the tables clean. I also sometimes order the cheese fries from western stall.

⭐️⭐️⭐️⭐️
Came here to hunt down Ah Bear Seafood after they left our West Coast coffeeshop in 2025. Happy to find them at ORTO, right beside the prawning area.

It’s not air-conditioned, but the place has fresh air, is bright, and well-ventilated — no smell like the previous vendor. Washrooms are clean and spacious, and pop English music makes it lively.

Prices are still affordable, similar to the West Coast coffeeshop near Sheng Siong. The fried rice, motaka, and other dishes were fresh and delicious. Our family of five (2 adults, 3 kids) spent around $50.

During our 2-hour dinner, we also enjoyed watching the prawning — many fruitful catches! There was even a skilled person who fished out several lobsters, which was fun to see. The kids were very satisfied, watching the prawning, eating delicious motaka, and playing the claw machines non-stop.

The staff were very helpful, and the owner was kind to my kids, even giving them tokens to try the claw machine.

Ah Bear Seafood is open from 5 pm to 2 am, while the prawning area runs from 10 am to 2 am.

A nice, family-friendly spot with good seafood, motaka, and prawning fun.

Le West Den prawning & Ah Bear mookata - A very cool place to hangout for the prawning brothers & sisters out there! Met the extremely nice, down to earth owners Brother Ah Bear & Brother Kiam & Another Brother (sorry i didnt get your name :)) were extremely kind, very warm and friendly! An honest review from me and my husband from the bottom of our hearts as we really enjoyed our time there! They offer refreshments, fresh seafood, and even have mookata, which was the highlight- all at affordable prices! Clean rest rooms, well ventilated prawning pool & excellent customer service by all staff too! A definite must visit place to enjoy your weekends with loved ones & they're family friendly too! Kudos brothers, wishing u more successful business ventures! God Bless!

New Prawning place there name Le west den and ah bear mootaka Zhichar.

Friendly staffs and great environment. Check out their lunch specials too!

Our favourite spot for prawn fishing. Followed by yummy dinner at their restaurant. And a tip from my son: the slot machines have an almost a winning guarantee. So bring coins! 😜

For Le West Den - Prawning @ ORTO: thank you very much! I had great time prawning with my family on my husband’s birthday! I had super low expectations coming in because of claimed low catch rates but lo and behold, 1-2 minutes of renting 2 rods, we caught a prawn each rod! By the end of an hour + 3 rods, we probably caught 12-15 prawns (800g)!! It was our first time prawning! I overheard a staff telling another stranger aunty “No matter what I will catch something for you” tells me very much about the sincerity of this establishment. I’ll leave a short review here until Le West Den gets their own Google Review page. Till then, i highly highly highly recommend this place for prawning! Boss said management just changed 1st November 2025, so dont confuse the old reviews for the new one! Will visit again! Thank you for making our birthday celebration such a fun one 😊

1st visit and followed GMaps to reach. Nearer walk from Lakeside mrt station & Had walked from North carpark or private car hire dropoff point. Marked by NParks as located at Lakeside Garden, Lakeside House. Crowded after 9am onwards

The only place opened early in the morning, before 8am, very spacious with lots of indoor and outdoor seating (some shelter) and enjoying great view of the lake and greenery. Its Breakfast set menu  from $6.90 ends at 2pm.

Saw some reviewed a 60min limit by staff for those working on their laptop, fortunately? T2T being there for more than an hour enjoying breakfast and the scenery, surfing on mobile without an issue on a Friday.

Staff walked around the store clearing some tables, however when T2T switched to the window seating, the table had some food bits.

One of the nicest Starbucks locations in Singapore. The outdoor seating is pet-friendly, which makes it a great stop if you’re visiting the park with your dog. Sitting outside with a coffee, overlooking the lake, is genuinely relaxing

The views are a highlight—especially the floating wetlands and the open expanse of water, which give this outlet a calm, almost resort-like feel
June 2026: ambience is top notch. Seats outside facing the lake and inside where there’s aircon. Walkable from lake side mrt even if you dun intend to walk the lake.

Walked to Jurong Lakes and nestle on the side of the lake is Starbucks, a most refreshing stop in the midst of a hot afternoon. No wonder they're no people in the park, all crowding into the Aircon join

Awesome cafe, and it’s the only eatery available at jurong lake garden. We appreciated that they are pet friendly and we saw many dog owners around while we were there. The pets are only allowed at the outdoor areas just for your information.

It has a very nice atmosphere and the outdoor area offered a fantastic view of the lake area, very soothing and peaceful sipping coffee sitting outdoor. Drinks and food was as expected at all Starbucks outlets here in Singapore. Service was friendly too, making it a pleasant experience dining here.

A fantastic gym located right beside Jurong Lake Gardens. The facility is large, spacious, and well ventilated, with plenty of cardio machines, weight machines, free weights, and functional training equipment. Even during busier periods, there is usually enough equipment available for a complete workout

The entry fee is excellent value at just $2.50 per session, making it one of the best-value gyms in Singapore

One thing to note: you must bring your own towel, or purchase one at the gym if you forget

Super good experience here . Well equipped with world class equipment and big with many floor machines and toys. And you can end your workout with a walk at the jurong lake park that is just next to it !

Great facilities and spacious environment.

However, there are concerns regarding staff conduct. Yesterday, a staff member approached my friend to question her about her towel, even before she had started her workout. This felt unnecessary and uncomfortable.
My friend is a nurse. Her cleaniness and hygiene is her top priority. She left her towel in the locker. She eventually took her towel before she starts her towel.
It also raised concerns about fairness, as there were other members actively exercising without towels who were not approached. Please ensure that all members are treated equally and respectfully, and that staff are mindful of how they communicate with gym users.

Additionally, kindly ensure that all hygiene stations are consistently stocked with sufficient sanitizers and cleaning brushes.

If towel checks are required, they should be conducted fairly and appropriately across all members.

Pros: Lovely big space with ample space to accomodate the evening crowds. I like they have a sled push and curved treadmills, along with multiple power racks.

Cons: A large part of the space is dominated by the grass patch, space optimization could be better. Slightly similar to senja activesg, this results in:
1) the warmup area being quite cramped in a corner
2) The free weights, power racks, smith machines etc being condensed into a small area. Bench press felt cramped, was literally bumping shoulders with the person on incline bench next to me.
3) because of that the main workout area can feel stuffy - I noticed I was sweating quite considerably even at night.

Miscellanous:
1)  warmup area/ water bottle + bags / toilets being quite far from the main weights/power racks (I don't mind, just something to take note)
2) Gym can be abit inaccessible, even while driving it took me 25-30 mins from jurong east (just something to factor in)

Their gym capacity tracker doesn't accurately reflect the actual gym capacity, which is way higher. Probably because of their turnstile gantry system instead of the typical QR code scanners at other gyms. The management should do something about it as its inconveniencing gym goers.

place_name: ActiveSG Park Swimming pool @ Jurong Lake Gardens
review_text:
Visited ActiveSG Park Swimming Pool @ Jurong Lake Gardens and had a pleasant experience. The pool is clean, well maintained, and surrounded by the greenery of Jurong Lake Gardens, making it a relaxing place to swim and unwind.

The facilities, including the changing rooms, were tidy and well kept. There is ample space for both casual swimmers and families, and the overall environment feels peaceful compared to many other public pools. Being located within the park also makes it easy to enjoy a walk or other outdoor activities before or after your swim.

Overall, a great place for swimming, exercise, or spending quality time outdoors in a clean and scenic setting.

Wave Plaza is a great spot to sit down and rest after a run or cycle around Jurong Lake Gardens. The open space is well laid out, comfortable, and feels nicely integrated into the surrounding greenery and waterfront

What makes it even better is that there are occasional performances and activities held here, which add a lively community vibe to the area. Whether you’re catching your breath, stretching, or just enjoying the atmosphere, it’s a pleasant place to pause
Love the passion wave plaza @Jurong lake garden .  Done dragon boat race n recent Run for inclusion here .  Easy accessible .  View is wonderful for the level 3 of plaza .
Very convenient location. Walking distance from lakeside mrt.
3km run for cross country wa lao eh
A place for family recreation. Loved it.

Penjuru Recreation Centre is a dedicated hub for migrant workers in Singapore, offering a wide array of essential services, sports facilities, and communal spaces. It is currently open daily from 8:00 AM to 12:00 AM.
The centre serves as a vital community space where residents from nearby dormitories can access daily necessities and engage in social activities. Recent reports indicate that the Ministry of Manpower (MOM) has plans to further upgrade the facility starting in 2025, which will include a new administrative block for training classes and events.
* Diverse Sports Facilities: The centre is equipped for a variety of sports, including a soccer field, basketball court, futsal court, and volleyball court. There are also specific areas for cricket training, carrom, and sepak takraw.
* Essential Retail & Services: Visitors have access to a supermarket, retail shops selling groceries, mobile phones, and SIM cards, as well as a dedicated remittance service outlet for sending money home.
* Community Amenities: A multi-purpose hall and various communal areas are available for gatherings, social interaction, and congregational prayers.
* On-site Dining: The facility features a canteen and food court providing various food and drink options tailored to the community's preferences.
* Medical Services: It houses a regional medical centre to provide healthcare access to workers in the vicinity.
The atmosphere at Penjuru Recreation Centre is described by visitors as vibrant and collaborative, fostering cultural exchange and networking. It is a popular weekend destination where workers gather to relax, play sports, and participate in festive celebrations and workshops organized by various NGOs.
Nice place. Necessary all food Goods have💝

It's very popular place for dental clinic whoever in Singapore under spacial pass and work pass.I went penjuru RC for removing stone on teeth. perhaps that, People can come here for any issues of teeth problem.
It's foreign workers dormitory, they can find their own food, groceries, vegetables, beer garden etc.
Genuinely feel bad for the hardworking migrants that live around here, glad I was there with CRA to give back to the community and help out with games for the workers! :)
Wonderful place to get together. We organised Bengali cultural festival every year here with thousands of Bangladeshi worker brothers.
Attended ACJC's traditional biennial funfair. Such an exciting event! Kudos to all the students and teachers who made the event so grand.

Not only does this school love sucking out money from its students to allegedly maintain facilities that 1. students NEVER get to use and 2. NEVER become cleaner or better in condition, they also enjoy developing bladder diseases in their poor students.

Teachers, especially the discipline master, ridicule and punish, sometimes even forbid, students from simply using the toilet. A 5 minute trip to the toilet to empty one’s bladder from holding it in for hours is seen as an act of sin, a crime being committed by this school that praises itself for being so loving with the grace of God.

During chapel, assembly, or school events where students are gathered in the hall, using to the toilet is seen as a threat, a crime. Students will either be held captive in another room where they will not even be able to see what is going on in the main hall to appreciate the event, will be told to take the route that is much longer to come back to the hall (which makes absolutely no sense as one would have to walk past much more people if their reason is disturbing the course of event), or simply, told no and scolded for “not being able to hold it in”.

Teachers say to students in a humiliating, condescending tone that if they cannot hold their bladder for more than a few hours, they should “get it checked”. This is either implying that students should not stay hydrated or using the toilet is undesirable, when it is part and parcel of how the normal human body works. Over time, with students not being able to use the restroom when they urgently need it, kidney and bladder diseases may be manifested. Urinary Tract Infections are amongst one of the many diseases students would have a much higher chance of having after holding in their urine for extended periods of time just to adhere to some school expectations. Not only that, it is simply extremely uncomfortable sitting still with a full bladder.

I sincerely hope the school will be more considerate of students’ health and needs and be more understanding.

I went to ACJC this morning at 11:30am as I wanted to purchase a swimming paddle for my child. At the entrance, the security guard Mr. Liao asked me to register and requested for my IC. I simply asked him whether it was necessary to provide my IC, but instead of explaining politely, he responded in a rude manner and told me not to “talk too much,” threatening not to let me in and even challenging me to complain to the authorities.

I believe such an attitude is not appropriate, especially within a school environment that should set an example for students in communication and respect. If students were to overhear such an exchange, what impression would they be left with?

I sincerely hope the school can look into this matter and ensure better communication and professionalism from the staff. Your improvement will be very much appreciated.

Wonderful Junior College providing a holistic education. Tremendously benefitted from my experience here at ACJC in 2003-2004, with lifelong friendships and bonds still going strong 20+ years later. Blessed to be part of the ACSian family, with teachers and even our former Principal being ever supportive of our individual endeavours. The sports facilities were fantastic too from the track to the swimming pool and gym, I credit a lot of my sporting journey from this too. Thank you, ACJC! 🙏🏻

The location is strategic, as it sits between 2 mrt stations, and surrounded by many eateries and shopping malls. The sport complex is big, there's football field, running track, swimming pools, as well as badminton court.

Not cheap, the portion keeps getting smaller, and when giving feedback to the cashier, the response was rude.

Was doing CNY house visit nearby on CNY Day 1 and couldn’t find a place to eat because most places are closed. First choice was the Thai cuisine but they ended up closed already! So we went for Indian cuisine and Chinese cuisine.
Indian cuisine was really bland and below average but clearly popular due to the heavily populated of migrant workers.
The Chinese cuisine was just barely ok, it was edible but the serving was really too little for their price of average $10 per dishes.

Overall still ok, am grateful they operated on CNY Day 1, a PH.

This place is always the hidden jem for those who know.

We followed the lights and found another sparkly paradise... so we decided to order a little bit of everything and everything taste good... will return to try a little of everything again.

I must say one of the best value places I’ve gone to. Food quality is also outstanding especially the western food and the soup.

Came at lunch time to try the herbal soup and ended up getting the fried horfun. But what luck! The fried horfun was sooo good - amazing flavours and texture with a lingering wok hei with every bite. It was good to the very last bite. At $5.50, the portion was good for two if you're not a big eater. So satisfying that I'm definitely coming back for more.

Was recommended the soup at this coffeeshop which is probably only known to residents and those working in the nearby industrial area. Clean and well-ventilated with a good selection of food. The soup was good and quite similar to Ah Er. Also tried the China food stall which offers a spread of ready to eat dishes and cook to order food. Filled with beer drinkers at night. Staff are quick to clean tables.

The cleanliness at this coffeeshop needs significant improvement. The chairs and tables were dirty, dusty, and oily, making it uncomfortable to dine in. Although there are staff members cleaning the tables, the overall hygiene remains poor. Additionally, people were smoking in the outdoor seating area, even though it isn’t a designated smoking zone. Better maintenance and stricter enforcement of cleanliness and smoking regulations are definitely needed.
Things are keep food is good.. outside cleaniness not so good .. alot of crows and pigeons. I myself have been attacked twice by a crow while have coffee.
My favorite go to for my sambal chicken fried rice during working weekdays

My normal lunch place being nearest to my office. Food is fine although it would be better if there's more stalls open.

Easy to get a place to sit as there are many tables.

Chinese economy rice service is nice and fast, although the queue can be very long at peak times. Long queue, short wait.

Nasi Padang food is nice, but service is extremely slow. Short queue, long wait.

Cleanliness needs to improve. Lots of flies. Chairs are old. Some need replacement

Most convenient place to dine in if you are working in Penjuru region. Currently have 5 stalls operating only. Do note that most stalls are closed by 1:30pm. Go for lunch early to avoid disappointment
Morning economical fry noodle price is reasonable and tasty
There's a wide variety of food to choose from, and it tastes delicious. The atmosphere is nice.

Had a Christmas gathering with friends. The unique dining-in-tent concept was fun and the ambience was great until it started raining. However, the boss was really kind to move us to a private room with ktv as there was company dinner going on at the main dining hall. The best part, the boss even changed out the soup base and dishes to fresh ones for us. Overall, ambience great with it being nice and private. The service definitely deserve a 10/10 stars and the food was good and price is cheaper than shilifang! Will definitely visit again despite it being a little inaccessible. Highly recommended for people looking for a nice and private place for gathering!

I feel a strong need to bring up the low ratings in Google Review as this place really isn't as bad as what many people said.

Firstly, the food is actually decent. Vegetables are fresh, prawns and meat are nice. The skewers chicken wings is quite good too. The Tom yam soup doesn't taste typical though. Price may be abit higher than average steamboat but it is still reasonable.
As long as we don't compare with HDL or beauty in a pot, then it's really not too bad.

The main attraction here is still the deco which is really instagramable. However the deco feels more suitable for lovers/couples, although it's alright for families as well.

Service was good from the start till the end too so no complaints.

So finally the reason for the 4 stars and not 5 is because of the location and more importantly to us, the heat! It may not be that hot, but with the steamboat, then it can get really uncomfortable. But we heard they are speaking with the management on providing a fan of some sorts in the near future. So let's see if they can earn the last star from me soon!

Overall, 8/10 and I will be back again with the right person!

am not sure why but perhaps they have improved recently.

Finally got myself to the 网红餐厅 last weekend. I was really surprised by how it looked in real life. The tents and decorations were laid out over an area just above the sheltered pickup point in level 1.

They were supposedly famed for their heart shaped hot pot in the tent, which required a min $100 spending that we only knew when we’ve arrived. So, the good thing for you is, I ate that horrible hot pot so you didn’t have to. Just order their normal Chinese food which is honestly, better than most places.

Their skewers (min 5 sticks for some) were really good and fragrant. So were the mini octopus with chilli and vinegar sauce! If this place wasn’t this out of the way, I might just come back every week just for their Chinese food!!!

A very IG worthy restaurant. Every corner is different theme for you take a photo with. Many have missed the indoor interior of the restaurant, i think is worth to explore as well with their magnificent great wall of china painting, umbrella ceiling and bamboo backdrop at the entrance.

To my surprise, their food are tasty as well.

i will not recommend the Mongolia hut, is good to take photo but even with the fan, is quite hot and stuffy inside.

And a reminder, the seasoning/sauce go by pax and it not as cheap.

A beautifully curated and well-maintained edible garden that’s both educational and relaxing. The beds are thoughtfully laid out, clearly labelled, and easy to stroll through, making it a joy even for casual visitors

What really stood out was seeing so many familiar plant names—herbs, vegetables, and edible plants we recognise from everyday cooking—grown so neatly and healthily

Located behind the Grand Arch, Edible Garden consist of a few sections exhibiting plants with edible flowers, plants, climbers, herbs and spices that can be dish up as food using various parts of the plant.
Some of these edible plants include marigold, hibiscus, egg plants, long beans and sweet basil for example.

It is inside the Chinese Garden after crossing the bridge. I can see many types of edible plants, but I think too many repetitions as well. They should maximise the space by planting more fruit plants instead of similar types of plants around that area.
Everything very yummy. I ate this particular flower and i started seeing my imaginary friend bobby dancing beside me. 10/10 experience
Beautiful place. Nice for a lazy evening stroll or a jog . Lots of greenery and the wind is always blowing and feels very nice and cool

​The park's main activities are conveniently clustered around McDonald's, which serves as a great central spot for dining, socializing, and recreation. Aside from active play, it is a fantastic place for gatherings, featuring camping grounds, barbecue pits, large open lawns for picnics, kite flying and jogging.

West Coast Park is a long but relatively quiet park that stretches along Singapore’s western coastline. It’s a wonderful place for a relaxed outdoor day, especially for families with kids

One of the highlights is the large playground, which is one of the better ones in Singapore. The climbing structures, slides, and play areas keep children entertained for a long time, and there’s plenty of open space nearby for running around

The park is also great for cycling and casual walks. You can easily rent bicycles or family pedal carts near the entrance and ride along the park connectors while enjoying the sea breeze

For those who enjoy spending more time outdoors, the park has BBQ pits and camping areas, making it perfect for weekend gatherings with friends and family. There are also scenic spots along the waterfront where people fish or simply relax and watch the ships in the distance

I do like to come to this nature park by the sea - to take in the salty air and the cool sea breeze, both of which relaxes and then invigorates me. I like to hear the waves crashing against the shore and to be among the greenery in this nature park - in particular, the bamboo fringed walkway as regards the latter. The amenities at this nature park is adequate - with enough car parking spaces and public toilets. And overall, this nature park is well-maintained.
Well maintained park and a good place to spend time alone or with family and friends. Suitable for all ages, whether to exercise in the mornings or catch a lovely sunset in the evenings.

West Coast Park is considerably smaller than East Coast Park (ECP), but it remains a highly viable zone for R&R. I have scouted the perimeter and identified several optimal coordinates with good sightlines to hold position and decompress.

Civilian population density in this grid is minimal. The decreased foot traffic results in a secure perimeter and a highly tranquil atmosphere—ideal for lowering your guard and recovering stamina.

Be advised on two minor variables:

First, visuals on the maritime border are compromised and do not match the aesthetics of ECP.

Second, heavy transport vessels are active in the adjacent waters. My MJOLNIR armor’s environmental sensors occasionally detected industrial exhaust drifting into the area. It is non-lethal, but noticeable without a helmet filtration system.

Overall assessment: A solid, quiet sector for downtime. Proceed to coordinates at your discretion.

This park connects the Pandas Garden and the West Coast area, with a canal stream running beside it. Although signs prohibit fishing, locals are sometimes seen trying their luck. The trees provide good shade, keeping it cool in the late afternoon. It offers a tranquil atmosphere with enough greenery between the HDB flats and nearby condominiums. Otherwise, nothing particularly special.

The only downside is that the pedestrian and vehicle paths share the same side, so you sometimes need to move aside for bicycles or vehicles. Another issue is that the signboard is blocked by electric booths, making it unclear to the public.

Pros
– Shady and cool in late afternoons
– Greenery and tranquility between residential areas
– Canal adds a natural touch

Cons
– Pedestrian and vehicle paths overlap
– Signboard blocked, unclear to the public

⸻

You're most comfortable with the benches provided for enjoying the environment or just watching the birds flying around. The trees provided good shade even in the hot afternoon. And you can view the sunset at the island near the basketball court. With the view onto the Pandan Reservoir. It's good for yourself to be in the Park.
if you're nearby the neighbourhood can come here for a jog, have fitness corner to do calisthenics also. good for those that want to work out outdoors.

Pandan Reservoir MRT station is still under construction. Very good location.

Located at Jurong Town Hall Road near the junction with Pandan Gardens and Teban Gardens Road, this MRT station will be on the second phase of the Jurong Region MRT Line (JRL) (known as the east branch of the Jurong Region MRT line) and will bring direct rail connectivity to serve Calvary Pandan Bible-Presbyterian Church as well as the housing estates of Teban Gardens and Pandan Gardens, Commonwealth Secondary School, Galilee Bible-Presbyterian Church, Teban Market Place, Ayer Rajah Community Club and Cycle & Carriage (Pandan Gardens). This station is named after the nearby reservoir within the vicinity and the bus services serving Pandan Reservoir MRT Station are SBS Transit bus services 30, 51, 51A, 201 and City Direct 655, SMRT Buses service 176, City Transit Premium bus service 762 and Tower Transit Singapore services 78, 78A, 79, 79A, 143 and 143M. The station was originally scheduled to open in 2027 but due to construction delays caused by the COVID-19 pandemic, the station is scheduled to open in 2028 instead. On 8 January 2023, part of Teban Gardens Road between Block 39 and Jurong Town Hall Road junction was realigned to form a junction with Jurong Town Hall Road and Pandan Gardens to facilitate works for the Jurong Region MRT line (JRL).

Jurong Town Hall Bus Interchange is a small but convenient interchange that connects residents and office workers to nearby areas. The layout is straightforward, with clear signboards and sheltered boarding areas. Buses arrive and depart regularly, and the place is generally clean and well-maintained. There are benches for waiting passengers and a sheltered walkway linking to nearby buildings. The interchange is also wheelchair accessible with ramps and wide pathways, making it easy for everyone to use.

New interchange is big and airy. Causeway terminal is right at the back.

Here you can get Malaysia cross border bus, quite funny on Malaysian bus. you will surprise on their strictness on safety awareness, I suppose as long as the bus arrives at the destination --- it is a good and working bus.

Jurong Town Hall Bus Interchange is similar to Jurong East's secondary "airport" where there are now four local bus services and the Causeway Link of Malaysia taking up tenancy at this terminus. This is to further enhance the easy management of bus movements within the Jurong East area as there is construction for J'Den (future residential) and Jurong East JRL (JE5) MRT.

There are five bus berths and three arrival berths (in a parallel parking orientation). All four local buses are trunk route services with 78 (Clementi loop via Pandan Gardens and Penjuru), 79 (Boon Lay loop via Teban Gardens and Jalan Buroh), 160 (Johor Bahru Sentral via Woodlands Checkpoint loop), and 870 (Tengah). The Causeway Link CW3 (Bukit Indah and Perling via Tuas Checkpoint) and CW4 (Terminal Pontian via Tuas Checkpoint) ply between Malaysia's Iskandar Puteri and Kukup areas with Singapore's Jurong East terminus
