Do try the curry rice/noodles from store #01-18. The curry chicken and pork ribs are succulent and well cooked. The meat falls off the bone and the savoury curry is just so appetising. Good serving sizes with potatoes and beancurd puffs. Also do grab their sambal chili. It is sweet with mild spice that goes extremely well with the meal.

We have been here a few times before because we find the food nice. The atmosphere is pleasant and airy.

My wife and I came again today for lunch. I must make special mention about 2 stewards (i call them stewards because their t-shirts have the word 'steward' on it).

I thought what a fantastic way to call them though they are cleaners. Kudos to the cleaning company's initiative. It gives rhem dignity.

The 2 stewards (an indian gentleman and a lady (both about the same height. I use the height thing so that it is easier to identify them, not derogatively). They were so polite and friendly and collecting the cutleries with pride (something not often seen in hawker centres and food courts.

This really makes my day. Thank you to the 2 stewards and the employer who hired then.
nice milk tea @ only $1. Uncle ensured the cup is warmed. Hot milk tea w grass jelly is also nicely done, not too sweet
Probably one of the few places you still can get food for $3 per serving. Large variety which caters to an aging population at Teban Gardens. The mixed veg rice stall is yummy and affordable.

Brought back old memories when I was young. Place have changed a lot with most vendors have either quit or retired. Good foods served here especially if you come for breakfast and lunch. The market usually closes early before 3pm if you're looking for something to eat.

place_name: Madras Coffee House's - Teban Gardens Outlet
review_text: Came here in the evening and it was crowded. There are tables outside whereby you need to stand and eat/drink. Bought suku coffee(packet).Guys at counter were courteous. Suku coffee was sweet with a spicy after taste. Will probably be retuning, another day, this time, on a morning/afternoon to try out their set meals.

place_name: Madras Coffee House's - Teban Gardens Outlet
review_text: Amazing food, highly praised even by my Indian friends. Very affordable prices and generous portions. I love the coffee and tea as well, though they are slightly sweeter than my preference. Definitely a gem if you're into the cuisine.

place_name: Madras Coffee House's - Teban Gardens Outlet
review_text:
It’s a small shop. No tables for dine in. Couple of high table for stand and eat. I think it is for quickly eat and go.

Variety of tea and coffee available made in a traditional way. It was good. Masala tea was too strong. But ginger tea was perfect.

They pour sambar and two chutneys on top of Dosa. Some may not like it. Probably you have to tell them if you don’t want that way.

Good that there’s a hand wash basin just beside counter.

place_name: Madras Coffee House's - Teban Gardens Outlet
review_text: Tea was quite good. But a little expensive for such a tiny glass. Only for standing.

place_name: Madras Coffee House's - Teban Gardens Outlet
review_text:
Budget friendly South Indian coffee shop. Located just in Teban gardens neighbourhood. Was here for breakfast and evening snacks on same day.
Breakfast combos are good and affordable. Best part is they prepare tea/coffee with fresh milk (not condensed milk) and cane sugar (not refined/processed white sugar). The tea/coffee is serves in glass tumbler!
Snacks in the evening time was good as well.  Egg puffs is a must try!
Good and clean.

At least eight stalls available with variety food and many seating. Separate drinks stall with malay "kueh" prepacked displayed infront cashier.
Two seating sections available indoor and outdoor.

Usually, in the morning of the last day of Ramadan, this coffeeshop would sell traditional Malay food such as lontong, ketupat, lepat and lemang.

Many ready-to-eat dishes such as ayam masak merah, serunding and rendang are also sold.

The place starts to get crowded as early as 6 a.m.

Do visit its Facebook page for updates on the sale of these delicacies for both Hari Raya Puasa and Hari Raya Haji.
Ok to get ur quick lunch for Malay food.

Although it’s far, the food at this coffee shop are quite good and affordable. The new western shop Hotbox is quite good. Tried their Steak, Chicken Cutlet and Samyang Prata. *update I tried their Lamb Chop. Worth it. Price are quite affordable.

Ratna chicken rice is also quite good and value for money.

The kueh at the drink stall is also good. The goreng pisang was freshly made when I bought it and the Makcik wasn’t stingy when I ask for extra crispy bits. Gave a full bag 😂

Did not try the other stalls but my colleagues said the food wasn’t bad. Nothing fancy. Overall it’s a good makan place to have lunch or just lepak. That said, the area is quite ghetto. 😌

The sudden crave for Ayam Penyet had led us to “MakanShiok” coffeeshop where a variety of Halal Food can be found and different from the main stream of stalls that we can find in the nearby Hawker Center. I came to know of this stall as one of my colleagues help us to Tabao during the CB (Circuit Breaker) period and I kind of recalled that it tasted above the average.

Just like a standard set of Ayam Penyet, it comes with bowl of soup, rice, vegetables, tempeh, fried Tau Gua, cherry tomato and the delicious looking piece of chicken. The soup is flavourful, garnished with fried onions and goes well with the plate of Ayam Penyet.

Ayam Penyet - $5.50 (Drink not included)

Though the rice was not as good as I had thought ~ it was neither fragrant nor fluffy. However the chicken complemented the plain rice. If you like hot and spicy, you will enjoy their chili too.

Take a look at the glistering skin of the chicken thigh and its sauce, its quite tempting, isn't it?

Comparing Ayam Penyet in the vicinity, there are definitely better ones but if you are looking for non fried Ayam Penyet, this should be considered as one of the better ones around the area.

[Utara]

Nasi Briyani Chicken was special item on menu that day. The execution of the plate of chicken Briyani is a little bit different with the use of mint leaves as garnishing. Briyani rice was fluffy and fragrant with the curry gravy.

The chicken was fried and was fragrant. The meat goes very well with the rice and gravy.

It was my first time ever in ordering Tomato rice. The fluffy long grain rice has hints of tomato taste comes with a big piece of chicken meat with some gravy drenched on the rice.

The dish was rather fiery with chili coated on the chicken. It looks wet in its gravy and from the texture of the skin, the chicken was pre-fried before coating with the gravy.

Pandan Reservoir Park is a peaceful and underrated spot for a long, relaxing walk. The full loop around the reservoir is about 5–6 km, and it took me roughly 1.5 hours at a comfortable pace. The gravel path is flat and easy, making it suitable for casual walkers

One of the highlights is the wildlife — I spotted several birds along the way, with the most common being a white crane-like bird quietly roaming the grass or flying low over the path. It adds a nice, natural touch to the walk

What stood out was how quiet it was, even on a weekend afternoon. I didn’t encounter many people, which makes this a great place if you’re looking for some solitude away from crowded parks

Came here on a PH to run in the morning.

There’s a carpark with toilets and showers at the starting point where the canoes are. There are also people rowing, canoeing and playing with toy speed boats. Strictly no fishing.

One round is 6km. Started running anti clockwise at around 815am and the sun was already high up in the sky. Theres pretty much no shade for the entire route but since it’s a circular loop you get a 360 tan.

Haven't been here for years and it has now been touched up to be much nicer to run at. It's not like a park that you typically imagine like bukit batok nature park or bukit timah hill for example. Its just a very simple small ish reservoir park tucked in the west coast of Singapore. It's really more for western residents to go for a walk. I also see water activities happening there. Pretty cool now.

Beautiful lake followed by a 6km walking path to relax ourselves and enjoy the beautiful scenery in the evening and Night time.

You will get a Positive Vibes, Best place for chitchat, Making Photographs.

Place was very out of the way.  I went there by going in along jalan buroh road. It was a scary road with mainly heavy vehicles with hardly any cars.  There was no proper entry point to the park.  I was searching for it coming from Pandan Loop.  I walked over a vehicle bridge over pandan river.  The bridge has a large pavement segment that was not opened to pedestrian yet. Although it was possible to get on it by clearing some barriers.  Many constructions were going on.  I believed it was to build proper pedestrian and cycling path.  I finally found an entry point.  You have to walk up a gentle grass hill to get on top of the reservoir.  The park was very rudimentary and hardly anyone was around.  There was a sandy pebble path for pedestrians to walk.  Hardly any trees there and it offered a good view of the reservoir and the sky. NDP preview show SG60 was going on and you could see them clearly flying across the reservoir. Together with the sunset, you could get very nice photo like apocalypse now poster.  I didn't have time to walk around the park.  I could see a wooden shed built at one end of the park right behind Pantech Business Hub.  I would say it was a nice place to visit to get away from people and noise.

As a father, there is no greater peace of mind than knowing your children are in an environment where they are truly nurtured. Both of my daughters have attended Columbia Preschool, and the growth I have witnessed in them is a testament to the school’s dedication.

My eldest daughter graduated last year and transitioned to Primary 1. I am incredibly proud of how seamlessly she adapted to the new environment. Thanks to the foundation laid here, she has no issues with learning or reading and faces new challenges with confidence. Her social skills and independence grew significantly after we transferred her to Columbia, where she learned not just academics, but the right methods to excel.

Currently, my younger daughter is in K1 and is thriving in many areas of her life. The school is consistently packed with meaningful events and activities that keep the children engaged and excited to learn. What sets Columbia apart is the high level of involvement and transparent communication with parents; they truly partner with us to ensure the kids have the right environment to strive.

I am deeply grateful to the loving teachers who have given my daughters such a fantastic headstart and a joyful childhood. If you are looking for a school that fosters independence and academic readiness, I highly recommend arranging a school tour to see the environment for yourself.

We are very happy with columbia pre-school, the environment is caring, safe, and very suitable for children to learn and grow. The teachers are patient, supportive, and truly care about the students.

My son enjoys going to school every day and has become more confident, independent, and happy since joining. We can really see his improvement in both learning and social skills.

Thank you to all the teachers and staff for creating such a positive and loving environment for the children. Highly recommended for parents looking for a good kindergarten for their kids.

We are truly grateful to have found this hidden gem of a preschool for our daughter. The principal’s passion and dedication are evident in the way the school is run, and the teachers are caring, attentive, and genuinely invested in the children’s growth.

What stands out most is how well thought out the academic programme is. The school prepares the children meaningfully for primary school, while still nurturing their confidence, curiosity, and love for learning.

We have seen such positive development in our daughter, and it gives us great assurance knowing she is in such good hands. Strongly recommended for parents to pay them a visit and experience the warmth of the school for themselves.

As parents, we always want to make sure our children are happy, comfortable, and excited to go to school every day. I can confidently say that my little one is having the best experience at Columbia. She looks forward to school each morning and has grown so much in such a short time.

Her confidence, communication skills, and ability to express herself have improved tremendously, and it’s amazing to see how quickly she is learning and expanding her understanding of the world around her. The teachers are caring, patient, and genuinely invested in the children’s growth and development, which gives us great peace of mind as parents.

We are truly grateful for the warm, nurturing, and engaging environment that Columbia provides. It has made such a positive impact on our child’s learning journey, and we couldn’t be happier with our experience so far.

Really thankful for all the teachers’ patience, care, and dedication towards both our boys. And especially thanks for teacher Siti, teacher Haz, Lu Laoshi & Wen Laoshi to took the time to thoughtfully share with us about their progress in school, their learning development, strengths, and areas they are working on. We truly appreciate the effort and genuine care put into every child.

It is very reassuring as parents to know that our children are in such a supportive and nurturing environment. Thank you to the whole team for all the hard work and commitment!

Located at Jurong East Central near the junction with Jurong East Street 21, Jurong East Avenue 1 and Toh Guan Road, this MRT station will be on the second phase of the Jurong Region MRT Line (JRL) (known as the east branch of the Jurong Region MRT line) and will bring direct rail connectivity to serve Masjid Al-Mukminin as well as the housing estates of Jurong East View, Yuhua Spring, Yuhua Ville and Jurong East Vista. This station is named after the nearby road (Toh Guan Road) and the bus services serving Toh Guan MRT Station are Tower Transit Singapore services 41 (Jurong East - Jalan Anak Bukit (Loop)), 98 (Jurong East - Jurong Pier Way (Loop)), 98M (Jurong East - Jalan Ahmad Ibrahim (Loop)), 183 (Jurong East - Science Park Road (Loop)) and 183B (Jurong East - Toh Tuck Avenue), SBS Transit services 52 (Bishan - Jurong East), 105 (Serangoon - Jurong East) and 105B (Jurong East - Clementi MRT Station) and SMRT Buses service 176 (Bukit Panjang - Bukit Merah) serving the bus stops outside Blk 209 and Blk 207 along Jurong East Central respectively. The station was originally scheduled to open in 2027 but due to construction delays caused by COVID-19 pandemic, the station is scheduled to open in 2028 instead.

Ah Xiong Fried Hokkien Prawn Mee (One of the open-air food court stalls)

This is a new stall that moved in during the last 1 month or so. I noticed that there are two stalls of the same name located in the Boon Lay area but I am not sure if this is one of those two that have relocated here.

We ordered one plate each of Fried Hokkien Prawn Noodle, Fried Kway Teow and Black Fried Carrot Cake for lunch. However, I did not find their food that fabulous.

Fried Hokkien Prawn Noodle ($5) : Portion is good for a small appetite. Taste is acceptable but needs more stock to enhance the flavor.

Fried Kway Teow ($6) : a somewhat disappointing dish as the cockles are way too raw and the dish can defiantly do with more sweet black sauce, more lard and more intense flame while cooking (the dish lacks "wok hei")

Black Fried Carrot Cake ($4.5) : This is the least favorable dish as it is obviously fried for a very short time. The internal of the carrot cake is not hot at all and similar to above, the dish lacks "wok hei".

Overall, I could only give a 3 star for their food on the quality aspect. (My guideline are a. 5 star for food with "Wow!" factor, b. 4 star for good food that I will revisit (and possible again-and-again), c. 3 star for edible food but I will unlikely to revisit).

Toh Guan Park Futsal Court: Where Passion Meets Precision

The Toh Guan Park Futsal Court is a haven for football enthusiasts seeking fast-paced action and camaraderie. Here's what you need to know:

1. Full-Sized Court:  This regulation-size court mirrors the intensity of professional futsal matches. The dimensions allow for swift passes, quick footwork, and thrilling goals.

2. Spectator Seating: With three levels of seats, the court accommodates fans, families, and curious onlookers. Whether you're cheering for your team or observing the game, these vantage points offer a panoramic view.

3. Location: Nestled within Toh Guan Park, the court enjoys a serene backdrop of greenery. As players sprint and strategize, the rustling leaves and distant birdcalls add to the ambiance.

So, lace up your boots, feel the adrenaline, and embrace the spirit of futsal at Toh Guan Park. 🌿⚽👏

Playground Oasis in the Heart of Nature

Nestled within the serene embrace of a sprawling park, the this playground is a delightful haven for both young adventurers and the young at heart.

Here are the key points that make this playground a standout:

1. Location: The playground's strategic placement within the park ensures a harmonious blend of play and nature. As children play at the playground, parents can soak in the tranquil surroundings.

2. Clean and Spacious: The playground boasts ample space for kids to explore freely. The meticulously maintained equipment invites laughter and imagination. No litter in sight—just pure playfulness.

3. Running Track Encirclement: A 700-meter running track envelops the playground, inviting joggers and walkers.

As kids swing and slide, fitness enthusiasts can clock their laps, merging recreation and exercise.

4. Shady Retreat: Towering trees provide a natural canopy, casting dappled shadows over the play area. Even on sunny days, the coolness of the foliage beckons families to linger.

This bustling hawker center is a culinary haven for both locals and tourists, offering a variety of affordable and delicious local dishes. Whether you're craving chicken rice, bak chor mee, or roast meat, the food options here are diverse and delicious. The hawker centre is often bustling, especially during lunch and dinner times, but it’s a great place to experience authentic Singaporean street food. Very highly reccomended.
Great hawker centre with lots of affordable and tasty local food. Busy but authentic atmosphere — a true neighborhood gem.

Very difficult to get the cut fresh halal chicken even early morning. So I called Blk 345 Jurong East 24hrs to reserve at 653am on 31 May 2026, a China female staff answered and was shocked 2 boxes left at this time! Please look into it as I think the food hawker centre must have reserved everyday more than caused the residents got not enough or no more! Very disappointed as not the first time as most of the day, either there is nothing more in the chiller or you managed to get a miserable 1 box only during morning or evening time. Hope can keep some for residents to buy as Fairprice chicken cut looks more hygienic and clean. Thank you!
Btw, the staff there are friendly and fast doing the cashiering. Overall their service is good! Keep it up!
I walk 0ast andvwent in to shop. managed to all yhe things I need. there were long line in the queue but cleared very fast. impressed with efficiency of the cashier counter.
Very good experience at this place. I am very satisfied
Good place to get the basic necessities and it's 24 hours.
Places to get the basis necessaries, n stocks run out fast. For other things, stocks are not stable. You can arm with a shopping list but come out unfulfilled.

Encountered an unpleasant experience here earlier.
Cashier made a mistake and did not input the 25% discount for the onigiri i took.
I asked for the receipt to made sure, and told the cashier about it. To my dismay she refuted by saying it was already discounted price.

I went back to the rack to double check and she took back the receipt.
I then reconfirmed that it wasn’t discounted yet, and after rescanning the product she finally said “ok you supposed to get back 70cents, I’ll give to you in cash”.

At this time there was another customer who came forward to pay, which she entertained him first before asking me to wait a moment or anything verbal.
After a brief moment, i got the 70 cents back without an apology.

To err is human, but to admitting mistakes and apologising should be common sense.
Very friendly and great customer service from The Cashier known by the name Tag, Mdm : Nithiya. Took great length to explain the current savings on Offer at the store. Thanks so much

Special shout out to Michelle and her team! We're thrilled to have finally reunited with the previous Vision Exchange 7-Eleven team at their new location. It's wonderful to see that the team remains unchanged - same warm smiles, exceptional service, and friendly demeanor. The store is not only bigger but also impeccably clean and well-maintained. My family and I are overjoyed to be greeted by familiar faces, making our shopping experience feel like a homecoming. It's a testament to the team's consistency and dedication to excellence."

Good job!

I would like to express my sincere appreciation for the exceptional service I received from this store in the morning.
I would like to praise the staff members Michelle with her outstanding assistance in helping me to locate the items I needed. She gone beyond with her exceptional service.
The overall experience at this store was fantastic.
Thank you , Michelle for your excellent work!

It’s clear that customer satisfaction is a priority for this 711 store, and I wanted to say thank you for the excellent service. I’ll definitely be back!
Kudos to staff: Michelle, Nicole and Mages

An old woman charged me $5.50 for two Coke Zero. I told her it’s only $1.50 each. But she brushed it off and refused to refund me. Yes, I should have screened before I realised the payment. But still this shop and staff have no integrity.

And She was counting SIM cards for some time when I’m waiting for her to check out. Useless and hopeless staffs. Will never return here no matter what.

The chicken breast i bought on 25th May already turn bad although expire in June. The haagen dazs ice cream stickbar i bought during promo 3pcs for $10 all melted. There are a couple of times i visited the store the frozen food delivered were not stock up in the fridge immediately even though the staff were not attending to customer they just left the food lying on the floor waiting for it to turn bad. Can't post the review without give at least 1 star

The customer service I received was absolutely unacceptable. The staff were unhelpful, dismissive, and showed a complete lack of concern for resolving my issue.My concerns were not addressed properly. This level of incompetence and disregard for customer satisfaction is shocking. I strongly urge your team to improve its training and processes because this experience was beyond frustrating.
Incident happened on 21st Jan 2025 at 7pm. I didn’t manage to get his name but he is a Indian men.

Bought a Milo boost that was on sale (1.70) and only after tapping card did I noticed that it was 3.20. Staff did not help and only tore the special offer after I informed. Turns out promo period was over but tag remained there. Do not visit the store

She just came out from the counter and i was reaching the counter area to make payment. She make an "chek" sound. Horrible service.
Over the years, there have been a change of staff but mostly have attitude problem and rude.

place_name: Star Learners @ Jurong East | Preschool & Childcare
review_text:
My two children has been attending Star Learners for the past four years. Overall, our experience has been positive. The teachers have always been warm, caring, and professional, and my children enjoy going to school.

While I don't have one particular thing that stands out above the rest, I do feel that the environment is supportive and the team is dedicated to the children’s growth and learning. Like many other childcare centres, they follow structured programmes that keep the kids engaged and learning at their pace.

One thing to note is that there has been a relatively high turnover of teachers during our time here. While new staff are generally just as capable and kind, continuity can be a bit of a challenge.

Despite that, I would still recommend Star Learners to other parents looking for a nurturing and safe place for their little ones.

place_name: Star Learners @ Jurong East | Preschool & Childcare
review_text:
Transferred my child from another childcare when she was N1 - the difference was that she stopped crying and had looked forward to school everyday, and in the blink of an eye - she is graduating.

Teachers have come and gone (as with all industries) but under Ms Eda, who has made us feel welcomed since day 1, the children has grown up well.

Everybody we met has been so kind - our school bus team, fellow parents, teachers, the whole community. I appreciate the programme, there’s a lot of opportunities for the kids to go out to nature, and for kids to bond with parents. I cannot recommend Star Learners JE enough. Thank you to the team from the bottom of my heart. Wishing the school continued success.

place_name: Star Learners @ Jurong East | Preschool & Childcare
review_text:
My son still cries at check-in every day, but the teachers have been consistently patient and caring with him, which I really appreciate. It’s reassuring to know that he’s in such a nurturing environment, even as he continues to adjust.

The teachers and Ms. Eda are also very approachable and responsive via WhatsApp, which makes communication easy and comforting for parents.

Although the school doesn’t have a nearby playground, they regularly arrange outdoor activities, and my son truly enjoys those moments outside. Thank you to the team for your dedication and kindness!

place_name: Star Learners @ Jurong East | Preschool & Childcare
review_text:
Star Learners Jurong East has been an incredible part of our child’s early years, from playgroup all the way through to kindergarten. The teachers and staff have consistently provided a warm, nurturing, and engaging environment where children feel safe, encouraged, and excited to learn.

Throughout the years, we’ve seen our child grow in confidence, build friendships, and develop a strong foundation for lifelong learning. The dedication of the teachers is truly remarkable—they are patient, kind, and always go the extra mile to ensure each child thrives. Their creative approach to learning, combined with their genuine care for the children, made every day an enriching experience.

The centre is well-organized, clean, and filled with thoughtful extra activities that support both academic and social development.

Now that our child has graduated, we look back with gratitude for the wonderful experiences and positive impact the centre has had on our family. We highly recommend this centre to any parent looking for a place for their child.

place_name: Star Learners @ Jurong East | Preschool & Childcare
review_text: Transferred my son (K1) from another childcare, within afew months he is able to read sight words. He also enjoys the daily enrichments provided by SL@JE. They are also prompt in replying to messages from time to time.
This Bus Interchange together with Jurong East MRT Station serves the majority of the residents in Western Singapore

Inaccurate bus timing, frequent delay or even no show sometimes.this happens every day. Sometimes, after waiting for 12 min.  we are still not sure whether the bus will show up. Truly disappointing there has been no
improvement even after so many months.

Unfriendly public place . The design like totally upside-down. 70% to 80% of passage at drop of points need to use overhead bridge to do change bus and this % need to share with the 50% or more ppl that come from the mrt. And 2 bus start point is out of interchange. If je going to be full develop this interchange won't able to hold the further crowd. The only star I give is new and clear.

Buses available daily (Causeway Link and SBS 160) to Johor Bahru here, cash or EZlink payment accepted.
Lots of cheap and sometimes tasty food (Takeaway only)
Prepaid SIM and topup too, local newspapers available.
Has toilets, wheelchair accessible all around.

I board bus 51 at teban garden heading Jurong East interchange,the moment I step in the driver immediately close the door without waiting for me to totally enter,as a result I was hit by the door on my hip.The most rediculous is the driver pretend never see anything at all,I was so flare-up so I told him I was hit by the door,he just simply say sorry without any sincerity as it it was nothing at all.we are passenger and using the public transport but was neglected n took for granted of our safety by the drivers,so sad to see such a scene in a civilized country like Singapore.(SBS3146U,I alight 16.15pm opposite j-cube)

The kids playground is close until further notice. No info online. If u intend to go cos of playground better find ways to check with pool first.
No wonder playground look so quiet. I peep in to confirm there is playground before buying tickets. But in the end can't use playground.

Note! It's Feature pool price. I buy under normal cannot scan. Counter say it's feature pool.

I'm a regular at the Jurong East swimming complex but the facilities are either not opened or old so I decided to take two more stops to Pioneer MRT Station where Jurong West swimming complex is.

Just 5 to 10 minutes walk from the train station, Jurong West Swimming Complex share the same building with alot of childcare, enrichment Centre, basically kids friendly place and also a stadium.

It has 2 swimming pool, one of the them is about 0.9 m, suitable for kids who don't know how to swim. The play area is not too bad, 2 slides but not for adults and anyone above 1.2 m. Too bad the bigger slide and lazy pool is not opened. Oh yes, there is a jacuzzi by the side for adults.

Overall, the facilities are very well maintained.

The staff are very nice and extremely helpful in our situation. We had wanted to go swimming in Singapore and remembered that a few years ago, all you had to do was tap your bus card to enter.

However, things have changed- now, in order to enter at all, you have to register for an ActiveSG account. Unfortunately for non- Singapore residents, this process is nearly impossible without a Singaporean phone number, and then also a Singaporean credit card.

Thankfully, we had helpful desk staff figure out a way to sneak through the problem, and one staff offered to use his credit card in exchange for payment in cash. We still needed to provide the postal code for our flat, but eventually, after almost half an hour of trying, we managed to get through without registering for an account.

Then, when we got inside, we realised that the entire waterslide and lazy river had been closed because of no one doing regular maintenance during Covid!

Not only that, but even though we paid for adult passes inside, the kids were not allowed into the jacuzzi because they said only adults. And the kids were not allowed on the kid's playground either because they had to be very short in order to enter.

All that said, we enjoyed swimming in the pool even though we could have went to the pool next to our flat, but we thought this would be more fun with a waterslide.

We are very dissatisfied with the system, but the only reason for 2 stars is the staff were helpful, otherwise we would have had to go home completely.
Cheap entry, normal ticket, not feature pool ticket. The big slide is quite long and the kids love it. There is also a lazy river.

Inexpensive place to spend time with family, friends or even by oneself. Big enough place with more than 4 pools move around including a huge slide. Children can play in the water playground with slides for children under 1.2m, so not much quarrel there. Olympic size indoor pool for more serious swimmers. A lazy pool just to laze around, tubes for rental available at counter.
